blocks.html_en: OpenAI's release of their Whisper family of models was a massive step forward for open-source speech to text. They offered a range of sizes, allowing developers to trade off compute and storage space against accuracy to fit their applications. Their biggest models, like Large v3, also gave accuracy scores that were higher than anything available outside of large tech companies like Google or Apple. At Moonshine we were early and enthusiastic adopters of Whisper, and we still remain big fans of the models and the great frameworks like FasterWhisper and others that have been built around them.

sentences.en: OpenAI's release of their Whisper family of models was a massive step forward for open-source speech to text.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 发布的 Whisper 系列模型是开源语音转文本领域向前迈出的一大步。

sentences.en: They offered a range of sizes, allowing developers to trade off compute and storage space against accuracy to fit their applications.
sentences.zh: 它们提供了一系列的尺寸，允许开发人员在计算和存储空间与精度之间进行权衡，以适应他们的应用程序。

sentences.en: Their biggest models, like Large v3, also gave accuracy scores that were higher than anything available outside of large tech companies like Google or Apple.
sentences.zh: 他们最大的模型（例如 Large v3）的准确度得分也高于 Google 或 Apple 等大型科技公司之外的任何模型。

sentences.en: At Moonshine we were early and enthusiastic adopters of Whisper, and we still remain big fans of the models and the great frameworks like FasterWhisper and others that have been built around them.
sentences.zh: 在 Moonshine，我们是 Whisper 的早期热情采用者，并且我们仍然是这些模型以及 FasterWhisper 等伟大框架和围绕它们构建的其他框架的忠实粉丝。

blocks.html_en: However, as we built applications that needed a live voice interface we found we needed features that weren't available through Whisper:

sentences.en: However, as we built applications that needed a live voice interface we found we needed features that weren't available through Whisper:
sentences.zh: 然而，当我们构建需要实时语音界面的应用程序时，我们发现我们需要 Whisper 无法提供的功能：

blocks.html_en: • Whisper always operates on a 30-second input window. This isn't an issue when you're processing audio in large batches, you can usually just look ahead in the file and find a 30-second-ish chunk of speech to apply it to. Voice interfaces can't look ahead to create larger chunks from their input stream, and phrases are seldom longer than five to ten seconds. This means there's a lot of wasted computation encoding zero padding in the encoder and decoder, which means longer latency in returning results. Since one of the most important requirements for any interface is responsiveness, usually defined as latency below 200ms, this hurts the user experience even on platforms that have compute to spare, and makes it unusable on more constrained devices.

sentences.en: Whisper always operates on a 30-second input window.
sentences.zh: Whisper 始终在 30 秒的输入窗口上运行。

sentences.en: This isn't an issue when you're processing audio in large batches, you can usually just look ahead in the file and find a 30-second-ish chunk of speech to apply it to.
sentences.zh: 当您大批量处理音频时，这不是问题，您通常可以在文件中向前查看并找到 30 秒左右的语音块来应用它。

sentences.en: Voice interfaces can't look ahead to create larger chunks from their input stream, and phrases are seldom longer than five to ten seconds.
sentences.zh: 语音界面无法提前从输入流中创建更大的块，并且短语的长度很少超过五到十秒。

sentences.en: This means there's a lot of wasted computation encoding zero padding in the encoder and decoder, which means longer latency in returning results.
sentences.zh: 这意味着编码器和解码器中存在大量浪费的计算来编码零填充，这意味着返回结果的延迟更长。

sentences.en: Since one of the most important requirements for any interface is responsiveness, usually defined as latency below 200ms, this hurts the user experience even on platforms that have compute to spare, and makes it unusable on more constrained devices.
sentences.zh: 由于任何界面最重要的要求之一是响应能力，通常定义为低于 200 毫秒的延迟，因此即使在具有空闲计算能力的平台上，这也会损害用户体验，并使其无法在更受限的设备上使用。

blocks.html_en: • Whisper doesn't cache anything. Another common requirement for voice interfaces is that they display feedback as the user is talking, so that they know the app is listening and understanding them. This means calling the speech to text model repeatedly over time as a sentence is spoken. Most of the audio input is the same, with only a short addition to the end. Even though a lot of the input is constant, Whisper starts from scratch every time, doing a lot of redundant work on audio that it has seen before. Like the fixed input window, this unnecessary latency impairs the user experience.

sentences.en: Whisper doesn't cache anything.
sentences.zh: Whisper 不缓存任何内容。

sentences.en: Another common requirement for voice interfaces is that they display feedback as the user is talking, so that they know the app is listening and understanding them.
sentences.zh: 语音界面的另一个常见要求是在用户说话时显示反馈，以便他们知道应用程序正在倾听并理解他们。

sentences.en: This means calling the speech to text model repeatedly over time as a sentence is spoken.
sentences.zh: 这意味着在说出句子时，会随着时间的推移重复调用语音到文本模型。

sentences.en: Most of the audio input is the same, with only a short addition to the end.
sentences.zh: 大部分音频输入都是相同的，仅在末尾添加了一小段内容。

sentences.en: Even though a lot of the input is constant, Whisper starts from scratch every time, doing a lot of redundant work on audio that it has seen before.
sentences.zh: 尽管很多输入是恒定的，但 Whisper 每次都从头开始，对以前见过的音频进行大量冗余工作。

sentences.en: Like the fixed input window, this unnecessary latency impairs the user experience.
sentences.zh: 与固定输入窗口一样，这种不必要的延迟会损害用户体验。

blocks.html_en: • Whisper supports a lot of languages poorly. Whisper's multilingual support is an incredible feat of engineering, and demonstrated a single model could handle many languages, and even offer translations. This chart from OpenAI (raw data in Appendix D-2.4) shows the drop-off in Word Error Rate (WER) with the very largest 1.5 billion parameter model.

sentences.en: Whisper supports a lot of languages poorly.
sentences.zh: Whisper 对很多语言的支持很差。

sentences.en: Whisper's multilingual support is an incredible feat of engineering, and demonstrated a single model could handle many languages, and even offer translations.
sentences.zh: Whisper 的多语言支持是一项令人难以置信的工程壮举，并证明单一模型可以处理多种语言，甚至提供翻译。

sentences.en: This chart from OpenAI (raw data in Appendix D-2.4) shows the drop-off in Word Error Rate (WER) with the very largest 1.5 billion parameter model.
sentences.zh: 来自 OpenAI 的图表（附录 D-2.4 中的原始数据）显示了最大 15 亿参数模型的字错误率 (WER) 的下降。

blocks.html_en: 82 languages are listed, but only 33 have sub-20% WER (what we consider usable). For the Base model size commonly used on edge devices, only 5 languages are under 20% WER. Asian languages like Korean and Japanese stand out as the native tongue of large markets with a lot of tech innovation, but Whisper doesn't offer good enough accuracy to use in most applications The proprietary in-house versions of Whisper that are available through OpenAI's cloud API seem to offer better accuracy, but aren't available as open models.

sentences.en: 82 languages are listed, but only 33 have sub-20% WER (what we consider usable).
sentences.zh: 列出了 82 种语言，但只有 33 种语言的 WER（我们认为可用的语言）低于 20%。

sentences.en: For the Base model size commonly used on edge devices, only 5 languages are under 20% WER.
sentences.zh: 对于边缘设备上常用的基本模型大小，只有 5 种语言的 WER 低于 20%。

sentences.en: Asian languages like Korean and Japanese stand out as the native tongue of large markets with a lot of tech innovation, but Whisper doesn't offer good enough accuracy to use in most applications The proprietary in-house versions of Whisper that are available through OpenAI's cloud API seem to offer better accuracy, but aren't available as open models.
sentences.zh: 韩语和日语等亚洲语言作为拥有大量技术创新的大型市场的母语脱颖而出，但 Whisper 的准确性不足以在大多数应用程序中使用。通过 OpenAI 的云 API 提供的 Whisper 专有内部版本似乎提供了更好的准确性，但不能作为开放模型提供。

blocks.html_en: • Fragmented edge support. A fantastic ecosystem has grown up around Whisper, there are a lot of mature frameworks you can use to deploy the models. However these often tend to be focused on desktop-class machines and operating systems. There are projects you can use across edge platforms like iOS, Android, or Raspberry Pi OS, but they tend to have different interfaces, capabilities, and levels of optimization. This made building applications that need to run on a variety of devices unnecessarily difficult.

sentences.en: Fragmented edge support.
sentences.zh: 边缘支撑碎片。

sentences.en: A fantastic ecosystem has grown up around Whisper, there are a lot of mature frameworks you can use to deploy the models.
sentences.zh: 围绕 Whisper 已经形成了一个奇妙的生态系统，有很多成熟的框架可以用来部署模型。

sentences.en: However these often tend to be focused on desktop-class machines and operating systems.
sentences.zh: 然而，这些往往集中在桌面级机器和操作系统上。

sentences.en: There are projects you can use across edge platforms like iOS, Android, or Raspberry Pi OS, but they tend to have different interfaces, capabilities, and levels of optimization.
sentences.zh: 有些项目可以跨 iOS、Android 或 Raspberry Pi OS 等边缘平台使用，但它们往往具有不同的界面、功能和优化级别。

sentences.en: This made building applications that need to run on a variety of devices unnecessarily difficult.
sentences.zh: 这使得构建需要在各种设备上运行的应用程序变得不必要的困难。

blocks.html_en: All these limitations drove us to create our own family of models that better meet the needs of live voice interfaces. It took us some time since the combined size of the open speech datasets available is tiny compared to the amount of web-derived text data, but after extensive data-gathering work, we were able to release the first generation of Moonshine models. These removed the fixed-input window limitation along with some other architectural improvements, and gave significantly lower latency than Whisper in live speech applications, often running 5x faster or more.

sentences.en: All these limitations drove us to create our own family of models that better meet the needs of live voice interfaces.
sentences.zh: 所有这些限制促使我们创建自己的模型系列，以更好地满足实时语音界面的需求。

sentences.en: It took us some time since the combined size of the open speech datasets available is tiny compared to the amount of web-derived text data, but after extensive data-gathering work, we were able to release the first generation of Moonshine models.
sentences.zh: 我们花了一些时间，因为与网络衍生的文本数据量相比，可用的开放语音数据集的总大小很小，但经过大量的数据收集工作，我们能够发布第一代 Moonshine 模型。

sentences.en: These removed the fixed-input window limitation along with some other architectural improvements, and gave significantly lower latency than Whisper in live speech applications, often running 5x faster or more.
sentences.zh: 这些消除了固定输入窗口限制以及其他一些架构改进，并且在实时语音应用程序中比 Whisper 提供了显着更低的延迟，通常运行速度快 5 倍或更多。

blocks.html_en: However we kept encountering applications that needed even lower latencies on even more constrained platforms. We also wanted to offer higher accuracy than the Base-equivalent that was the top end of the initial models. That led us to this second generation of Moonshine models, which offer:

sentences.en: However we kept encountering applications that needed even lower latencies on even more constrained platforms.
sentences.zh: 然而，我们不断遇到在更受限的平台上需要更低延迟的应用程序。

sentences.en: We also wanted to offer higher accuracy than the Base-equivalent that was the top end of the initial models.
sentences.zh: 我们还希望提供比初始模型高端的基本等效精度更高的精度。

sentences.en: That led us to this second generation of Moonshine models, which offer:
sentences.zh: 这导致我们推出了第二代 Moonshine 型号，它提供：

blocks.html_en: • Flexible input windows. You can supply any length of audio (though we recommend staying below around 30 seconds) and the model will only spend compute on that input, no zero-padding required. This gives us a significant latency boost.

sentences.en: Flexible input windows.
sentences.zh: 灵活的输入窗口。

sentences.en: You can supply any length of audio (though we recommend staying below around 30 seconds) and the model will only spend compute on that input, no zero-padding required.
sentences.zh: 您可以提供任意长度的音频（尽管我们建议保持在 30 秒左右），模型只会在该输入上花费计算，不需要零填充。

sentences.en: This gives us a significant latency boost.
sentences.zh: 这给我们带来了显着的延迟提升。

blocks.html_en: • Caching for streaming. Our models now support incremental addition of audio over time, and they cache the input encoding and part of the decoder's state so that we're able to skip even more of the compute, driving latency down dramatically.

sentences.en: Caching for streaming.
sentences.zh: 缓存用于流式传输。

sentences.en: Our models now support incremental addition of audio over time, and they cache the input encoding and part of the decoder's state so that we're able to skip even more of the compute, driving latency down dramatically.
sentences.zh: 我们的模型现在支持随着时间的推移增量添加音频，并且它们缓存输入编码和部分解码器状态，以便我们能够跳过更多计算，从而显着降低延迟。

blocks.html_en: • Language-specific models. We have gathered data and trained models for multiple languages, including Arabic, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese, and Chinese. As we discuss in our Flavors of Moonshine paper, we've found that we can get much higher accuracy for the same size and compute if we restrict a model to focus on just one language, compared to training one model across many.

sentences.en: Language-specific models.
sentences.zh: 特定于语言的模型。

sentences.en: We have gathered data and trained models for multiple languages, including Arabic, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese, and Chinese.
sentences.zh: 我们收集了多种语言的数据并训练了模型，包括阿拉伯语、日语、韩语、西班牙语、乌克兰语、越南语和中文。

sentences.en: As we discuss in our Flavors of Moonshine paper, we've found that we can get much higher accuracy for the same size and compute if we restrict a model to focus on just one language, compared to training one model across many.
sentences.zh: 正如我们在《Flavours of Moonshine》论文中所讨论的那样，我们发现，与跨多种语言训练一个模型相比，如果我们将模型限制为仅关注一种语言，那么在相同的大小和计算量下，我们可以获得更高的准确性。

blocks.html_en: • Cross-platform library support. We're building applications ourselves, and needed to be able to deploy these models across Linux, MacOS, Windows, iOS, and Android, as well as use them from languages like Python, Swift, Java, and C++. To support this we architected a portable C++ core library that handles all of the processing, uses OnnxRuntime for good performance across systems, and then built native interfaces for all the required high-level languages. This allows developers to learn one API, and then deploy it almost anywhere they want to run.

sentences.en: Cross-platform library support.
sentences.zh: 跨平台库支持。

sentences.en: We're building applications ourselves, and needed to be able to deploy these models across Linux, MacOS, Windows, iOS, and Android, as well as use them from languages like Python, Swift, Java, and C++.
sentences.zh: 我们自己构建应用程序，需要能够跨 Linux、MacOS、Windows、iOS 和 Android 部署这些模型，并通过 Python、Swift、Java 和 C++ 等语言使用它们。

sentences.en: To support this we architected a portable C++ core library that handles all of the processing, uses OnnxRuntime for good performance across systems, and then built native interfaces for all the required high-level languages.
sentences.zh: 为了支持这一点，我们构建了一个可移植的 C++ 核心库来处理所有处理，使用 OnnxRuntime 实现跨系统的良好性能，然后为所有所需的高级语言构建本机接口。

sentences.en: This allows developers to learn one API, and then deploy it almost anywhere they want to run.
sentences.zh: 这使得开发人员可以学习一个 API，然后将其部署到几乎任何他们想要运行的地方。

blocks.html_en: • Better accuracy than Whisper V3 Large. On HuggingFace's OpenASR leaderboard, our newest streaming model for English, Medium Streaming, achieves a lower word-error rate than the most-accurate Whisper model from OpenAI. This is despite Moonshine's version using 250 million parameters, versus Large v3's 1.5 billion, making it much easier to deploy on the edge.

sentences.en: Better accuracy than Whisper V3 Large.
sentences.zh: 比 Whisper V3 Large 更准确。

sentences.en: On HuggingFace's OpenASR leaderboard, our newest streaming model for English, Medium Streaming, achieves a lower word-error rate than the most-accurate Whisper model from OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 在 HuggingFace 的 OpenASR 排行榜上，我们最新的英语流模型 Medium Streaming 比 OpenAI 最准确的 Whisper 模型实现了更低的字错率。

sentences.en: This is despite Moonshine's version using 250 million parameters, versus Large v3's 1.5 billion, making it much easier to deploy on the edge.
sentences.zh: 尽管 Moonshine 的版本使用了 2.5 亿个参数，而 Large v3 则使用了 15 亿个参数，这使得它更容易在边缘部署。

blocks.html_en: Hopefully this gives you a good idea of how Moonshine compares to Whisper. If you're working with GPUs in the cloud on data in bulk where throughput is most important then Whisper (or Nvidia alternatives like Parakeet) offer advantages like batch processing, but we believe we can't be beat for live speech. We've built the framework and models we wished we'd had when we first started building applications with voice interfaces, so if you're working with live voice inputs, give Moonshine a try.

sentences.en: Hopefully this gives you a good idea of how Moonshine compares to Whisper.
sentences.zh: 希望这能让您更好地了解 Moonshine 与 Whisper 的比较。

sentences.en: If you're working with GPUs in the cloud on data in bulk where throughput is most important then Whisper (or Nvidia alternatives like Parakeet) offer advantages like batch processing, but we believe we can't be beat for live speech.
sentences.zh: 如果您在云中使用 GPU 处理批量数据，其中吞吐量是最重要的，那么 Whisper（或 Nvidia 替代品，如 Parakeet）可以提供批处理等优势，但我们相信我们在实时语音方面无可匹敌。

sentences.en: We've built the framework and models we wished we'd had when we first started building applications with voice interfaces, so if you're working with live voice inputs, give Moonshine a try.
sentences.zh: 当我们第一次开始构建带有语音界面的应用程序时，我们已经构建了我们希望拥有的框架和模型，所以如果您正在使用实时语音输入，请尝试一下 Moonshine。

sentences.zh: 使用图书馆

blocks.html_en: The Moonshine API is designed to take care of the details around capturing and transcribing live speech, giving application developers a high-level API focused on actionable events. I'll use Python to illustrate how it works, but the API is consistent across all the supported languages.

sentences.en: The Moonshine API is designed to take care of the details around capturing and transcribing live speech, giving application developers a high-level API focused on actionable events.
sentences.zh: Moonshine API 旨在处理捕获和转录实时语音的细节，为应用程序开发人员提供专注于可操作事件的高级 API。

sentences.en: I'll use Python to illustrate how it works, but the API is consistent across all the supported languages.
sentences.zh: 我将使用 Python 来说明它是如何工作的，但该 API 在所有受支持的语言中都是一致的。

blocks.html_en: Our goal is to build a framework that any developer can pick up and use, even with no previous experience of speech technologies. We've abstracted away a lot of the unnecessary details and provide a simple interface that lets you focus on building your application, and that's reflected in our system architecture.

sentences.en: Our goal is to build a framework that any developer can pick up and use, even with no previous experience of speech technologies.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标是构建一个任何开发人员都可以学习和使用的框架，即使以前没有语音技术经验。

sentences.en: We've abstracted away a lot of the unnecessary details and provide a simple interface that lets you focus on building your application, and that's reflected in our system architecture.
sentences.zh: 我们抽象出了许多不必要的细节，并提供了一个简单的界面，让您可以专注于构建应用程序，这反映在我们的系统架构中。

blocks.html_en: The basic flow is:

sentences.en: The basic flow is:
sentences.zh: 基本流程是：

blocks.html_en: • Create a Transcriber or IntentRecognizer object, depending on whether you want the text that's spoken, or just to know that a user has requested an action.

sentences.en: Create a Transcriber or IntentRecognizer object, depending on whether you want the text that's spoken, or just to know that a user has requested an action.
sentences.zh: 创建 Transcriber 或 IntentRecognizer 对象，具体取决于您是否想要朗读的文本，或者只是知道用户已请求操作。

blocks.html_en: • Attach an EventListener that gets called when important things occur, like the end of a phrase or an action being triggered, so your application can respond.

sentences.en: Attach an EventListener that gets called when important things occur, like the end of a phrase or an action being triggered, so your application can respond.
sentences.zh: 附加一个 EventListener，当重要事件发生时（例如短语结尾或触发操作），该事件监听器将被调用，以便您的应用程序可以做出响应。

blocks.html_en: • Use a TextToSpeech object to make it a two-way conversation.

sentences.en: Use a TextToSpeech object to make it a two-way conversation.
sentences.zh: 使用 TextToSpeech 对象使其成为双向对话。

blocks.html_en: Traditionally, adding a voice interface to an application or product required integrating a lot of different libraries to handle all the processing that's needed to capture audio and turn it into something actionable. The main steps involved are microphone capture, voice activity detection (to break a continuous stream of audio into sections of speech), speech to text, speaker identification, and intent recognition. Each of these steps typically involved a different framework, which greatly increased the complexity of integrating, optimizing, and maintaining these dependencies.

sentences.en: Traditionally, adding a voice interface to an application or product required integrating a lot of different libraries to handle all the processing that's needed to capture audio and turn it into something actionable.
sentences.zh: 传统上，向应用程序或产品添加语音界面需要集成许多不同的库来处理捕获音频并将其转化为可操作的内容所需的所有处理。

sentences.en: The main steps involved are microphone capture, voice activity detection (to break a continuous stream of audio into sections of speech), speech to text, speaker identification, and intent recognition.
sentences.zh: 涉及的主要步骤包括麦克风捕获、语音活动检测（将连续的音频流分解为语音片段）、语音转文本、说话人识别和意图识别。

sentences.en: Each of these steps typically involved a different framework, which greatly increased the complexity of integrating, optimizing, and maintaining these dependencies.
sentences.zh: 每个步骤通常都涉及不同的框架，这大大增加了集成、优化和维护这些依赖项的复杂性。

blocks.html_en: Moonshine Voice includes all of these stages in a single library, and abstracts away everything but the essential information your application needs to respond to user speech, whether you want to transcribe it or trigger actions.

sentences.en: Moonshine Voice includes all of these stages in a single library, and abstracts away everything but the essential information your application needs to respond to user speech, whether you want to transcribe it or trigger actions.
sentences.zh: Moonshine Voice 将所有这些阶段包含在一个库中，并抽象出除应用程序响应用户语音所需的基本信息之外的所有内容，无论您是要转录它还是触发操作。

blocks.html_en: Most developers should be able to treat the library as a black box that tells them when something interesting has happened, using our event-based classes to implement application logic. Of course the framework is fully open source, so speech experts can dive as deep under the hood as they'd like, but it's not necessary to use it.

sentences.en: Most developers should be able to treat the library as a black box that tells them when something interesting has happened, using our event-based classes to implement application logic.
sentences.zh: 大多数开发人员应该能够将该库视为一个黑匣子，当发生有趣的事情时，它会告诉他们，使用我们基于事件的类来实现应用程序逻辑。

sentences.en: Of course the framework is fully open source, so speech experts can dive as deep under the hood as they'd like, but it's not necessary to use it.
sentences.zh: 当然，该框架是完全开源的，因此语音专家可以根据需要深入研究，但没有必要使用它。

blocks.html_en: Concepts

sentences.en: Concepts
sentences.zh: 概念

blocks.html_en: A Transcriber takes in audio input and turns any speech into text. This is the first object you'll need to create to use Moonshine, and you'll give it a path to the models you've downloaded.

sentences.en: A Transcriber takes in audio input and turns any speech into text.
sentences.zh: 转录器接收音频输入并将任何语音转换为文本。

sentences.en: This is the first object you'll need to create to use Moonshine, and you'll give it a path to the models you've downloaded.
sentences.zh: 这是使用 Moonshine 需要创建的第一个对象，您将为其提供已下载模型的路径。

blocks.html_en: A MicTranscriber is a helper class based on the general transcriber that takes care of connecting to a microphone using your platform's built-in support (for example sounddevice in Python) and then feeding the audio in as it's captured.

sentences.en: A MicTranscriber is a helper class based on the general transcriber that takes care of connecting to a microphone using your platform's built-in support (for example sounddevice in Python) and then feeding the audio in as it's captured.
sentences.zh: MicTranscriber 是一个基于通用转录器的帮助器类，它负责使用平台的内置支持（例如 Python 中的声音设备）连接到麦克风，然后在捕获音频时将其输入。

blocks.html_en: A Stream is a handler for audio input. The reason streams exist is because you may want to process multiple audio inputs at once, and a transcriber can support those through multiple streams, without duplicating the model resources. If you only have one input, the transcriber class includes the same methods (start/stop/add_audio) as a stream, and you can use that interface instead and forget about streams.

sentences.en: A Stream is a handler for audio input.
sentences.zh: Stream 是音频输入的处理程序。

sentences.en: The reason streams exist is because you may want to process multiple audio inputs at once, and a transcriber can support those through multiple streams, without duplicating the model resources.
sentences.zh: 流存在的原因是因为您可能希望一次处理多个音频输入，而转录器可以通过多个流支持这些输入，而无需复制模型资源。

sentences.en: If you only have one input, the transcriber class includes the same methods (start/stop/add_audio) as a stream, and you can use that interface instead and forget about streams.
sentences.zh: 如果您只有一个输入，则转录器类包含与流相同的方法（start/stop/add_audio），您可以改用该接口并忘记流。

blocks.html_en: A TranscriptLine is a data structure holding information about one line in the transcript. When someone is speaking, the library waits for short pauses (where punctuation might go in written language) and starts a new line. These aren't exactly sentences, since a speech pause isn't a sure sign of the end of a sentence, but this does break the spoken audio into segments that can be considered phrases. A line includes state such as whether the line has just started, is still being spoken, or is complete, along with its start time and duration.

sentences.en: A TranscriptLine is a data structure holding information about one line in the transcript.
sentences.zh: TranscriptLine 是一种数据结构，保存有关转录本中一行的信息。

sentences.en: When someone is speaking, the library waits for short pauses (where punctuation might go in written language) and starts a new line.
sentences.zh: 当有人说话时，图书馆会等待短暂的停顿（书面语言中可能会出现标点符号）并开始新的一行。

sentences.en: These aren't exactly sentences, since a speech pause isn't a sure sign of the end of a sentence, but this does break the spoken audio into segments that can be considered phrases.
sentences.zh: 这些并不完全是句子，因为语音停顿并不是句子结束的确定标志，但这确实将口语音频分解为可以被视为短语的片段。

sentences.en: A line includes state such as whether the line has just started, is still being spoken, or is complete, along with its start time and duration.
sentences.zh: 线路包括状态，例如该线路是否刚刚开始、仍在讲话或已完成，以及其开始时间和持续时间。

blocks.html_en: A Transcript is a list of lines in time order holding information about what text has already been recognized, along with other state like when it was captured.

sentences.en: A Transcript is a list of lines in time order holding information about what text has already been recognized, along with other state like when it was captured.
sentences.zh: 转录本是按时间顺序排列的行列表，其中包含有关已识别文本的信息以及其他状态（例如捕获时的状态）。

blocks.html_en: A TranscriptEvent contains information about changes to the transcript. Events include a new line being started, the text in a line being updated, and a line being completed. The event object includes the transcript line it's referring to as a member, holding the latest state of that line.

sentences.en: A TranscriptEvent contains information about changes to the transcript.
sentences.zh: TranscriptEvent 包含有关转录本更改的信息。

sentences.en: Events include a new line being started, the text in a line being updated, and a line being completed.
sentences.zh: 事件包括开始新行、更新行中的文本以及完成行。

sentences.en: The event object includes the transcript line it's referring to as a member, holding the latest state of that line.
sentences.zh: 事件对象包括它作为成员引用的转录行，保存该行的最新状态。

blocks.html_en: A TranscriptEventListener is a protocol that allows app-defined functions to be called when transcript events happen. This is the main way that most applications interact with the results of the transcription. When live speech is happening, applications usually need to respond or display results as new speech is recognized, and this approach allows you to handle those changes in a similar way to events from traditional user interfaces like touch screen gestures or mouse clicks on buttons.

sentences.en: A TranscriptEventListener is a protocol that allows app-defined functions to be called when transcript events happen.
sentences.zh: TranscriptEventListener 是一种协议，允许在发生转录事件时调用应用程序定义的函数。

sentences.en: This is the main way that most applications interact with the results of the transcription.
sentences.zh: 这是大多数应用程序与转录结果交互的主要方式。

sentences.en: When live speech is happening, applications usually need to respond or display results as new speech is recognized, and this approach allows you to handle those changes in a similar way to events from traditional user interfaces like touch screen gestures or mouse clicks on buttons.
sentences.zh: 当发生实时语音时，应用程序通常需要在识别新语音时做出响应或显示结果，这种方法允许您以与传统用户界面事件（如触摸屏手势或鼠标单击按钮）类似的方式处理这些更改。

blocks.html_en: An IntentRecognizer is a type of TranscriptEventListener that allows you to invoke different callback functions when preprogrammed intents are detected. This is useful for building voice command recognition features.

sentences.en: An IntentRecognizer is a type of TranscriptEventListener that allows you to invoke different callback functions when preprogrammed intents are detected.
sentences.zh: IntentRecognizer 是一种 TranscriptEventListener，它允许您在检测到预编程意图时调用不同的回调函数。

sentences.en: This is useful for building voice command recognition features.
sentences.zh: 这对于构建语音命令识别功能非常有用。

blocks.html_en: A TextToSpeech object synthesizes audio for playback to the user.

sentences.en: A TextToSpeech object synthesizes audio for playback to the user.
sentences.zh: TextToSpeech 对象合成音频以供用户播放。

blocks.html_en: A DialogFlow object manages conversations between the user and an agent.

sentences.en: A DialogFlow object manages conversations between the user and an agent.
sentences.zh: DialogFlow 对象管理用户和代理之间的对话。

blocks.html_en: A Dialog object is created for each conversational exchange, and allows the agent to hold a multi-step discussion with the user.

sentences.en: A Dialog object is created for each conversational exchange, and allows the agent to hold a multi-step discussion with the user.
sentences.zh: 为每个会话交换创建一个 Dialog 对象，并允许代理与用户进行多步骤讨论。

blocks.html_en: Getting Started with Transcription

sentences.en: Getting Started with Transcription
sentences.zh: 转录入门

blocks.html_en: We have examples for most platforms so as a first step I recommend checking out what we have for the systems you're targeting.

sentences.en: We have examples for most platforms so as a first step I recommend checking out what we have for the systems you're targeting.
sentences.zh: 我们有大多数平台的示例，因此作为第一步，我建议您检查一下我们为您的目标系统提供的内容。

blocks.html_en: Next, you'll need to add the library to your project. We aim to provide pre-built binaries for all major platforms using their native package managers. On Python this means a pip install, for Android it's a Maven package, and for MacOS and iOS we provide a Swift package through SPM.

sentences.en: Next, you'll need to add the library to your project.
sentences.zh: 接下来，您需要将该库添加到您的项目中。

sentences.en: We aim to provide pre-built binaries for all major platforms using their native package managers.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标是使用其本机包管理器为所有主要平台提供预构建的二进制文件。

sentences.en: On Python this means a pip install, for Android it's a Maven package, and for MacOS and iOS we provide a Swift package through SPM.
sentences.zh: 在 Python 上，这意味着 pip 安装，对于 Android 来说，它是 Maven 包，对于 MacOS 和 iOS，我们通过 SPM 提供 Swift 包。

blocks.html_en: The transcriber needs access to the files for the model you're using, so after downloading them you'll need to place them somewhere the application can find them, and make a note of the path. This usually means adding them as resources in your IDE if you're planning to distribute the app, or you can use hard-wired paths if you're just experimenting. The download script gives you the location of the models and their architecture type on your drive after it completes.

sentences.en: The transcriber needs access to the files for the model you're using, so after downloading them you'll need to place them somewhere the application can find them, and make a note of the path.
sentences.zh: 转录器需要访问您正在使用的模型的文件，因此下载它们后，您需要将它们放置在应用程序可以找到它们的位置，并记下路径。

sentences.en: This usually means adding them as resources in your IDE if you're planning to distribute the app, or you can use hard-wired paths if you're just experimenting.
sentences.zh: 这通常意味着如果您计划分发应用程序，请将它们作为资源添加到您的 IDE 中，或者如果您只是进行试验，则可以使用硬连线路径。

sentences.en: The download script gives you the location of the models and their architecture type on your drive after it completes.
sentences.zh: 下载脚本完成后会为您提供驱动器上模型的位置及其架构类型。

blocks.html_en: Now you can try creating a transcriber. Here's what that looks like in Python:

sentences.en: Now you can try creating a transcriber.
sentences.zh: 现在您可以尝试创建转录器。

sentences.en: Here's what that looks like in Python:
sentences.zh: Python 中的情况如下：

blocks.html_en: If the model isn't found, or if there's any other error, this will throw an exception with information about the problem. You can also check the console for logs from the core library, these are printed to stderr or your system's equivalent.

sentences.en: If the model isn't found, or if there's any other error, this will throw an exception with information about the problem.
sentences.zh: 如果未找到模型，或者存在任何其他错误，这将引发异常并包含有关问题的信息。

sentences.en: You can also check the console for logs from the core library, these are printed to stderr or your system's equivalent.
sentences.zh: 您还可以在控制台中检查来自核心库的日志，这些日志会打印到 stderr 或您系统的等效项。

blocks.html_en: Now we'll create a listener that contains the app logic that you want triggered when the transcript updates, and attach it to your transcriber:

sentences.en: Now we'll create a listener that contains the app logic that you want triggered when the transcript updates, and attach it to your transcriber:
sentences.zh: 现在我们将创建一个侦听器，其中包含您希望在转录内容更新时触发的应用程序逻辑，并将其附加到您的转录器：

blocks.html_en: The transcriber needs some audio data to work with. If you want to try it with the microphone you can update your transcriber creation line to use a MicTranscriber instead, but if you want to start with a .wav file for testing purposes here's how you feed that in:

sentences.en: The transcriber needs some audio data to work with.
sentences.zh: 转录器需要一些音频数据来处理。

sentences.en: If you want to try it with the microphone you can update your transcriber creation line to use a MicTranscriber instead, but if you want to start with a .wav file for testing purposes here's how you feed that in:
sentences.zh: 如果您想使用麦克风进行尝试，您可以更新转录器创建行以使用 MicTranscriber，但如果您想从 .wav 文件开始进行测试，请按以下方法输入该文件：

blocks.html_en: The important things to notice here are:

sentences.en: The important things to notice here are:
sentences.zh: 这里需要注意的重要事项是：

blocks.html_en: • We create an array of mono audio data from a wav file, using the convenience load_wav_file() function that's part of the Moonshine library.

sentences.en: We create an array of mono audio data from a wav file, using the convenience load_wav_file() function that's part of the Moonshine library.
sentences.zh: 我们使用 Moonshine 库中方便的 load_wav_file() 函数从 wav 文件创建单声道音频数据数组。

blocks.html_en: • We start the transcriber to activate its processing code.

sentences.en: We start the transcriber to activate its processing code.
sentences.zh: 我们启动转录器来激活其处理代码。

blocks.html_en: • The loop adds audio in chunks. These chunks can be any length and any sample rate, the library takes care of all the housekeeping.

sentences.en: The loop adds audio in chunks.
sentences.zh: 该循环以块的形式添加音频。

sentences.en: These chunks can be any length and any sample rate, the library takes care of all the housekeeping.
sentences.zh: 这些块可以是任意长度和任意采样率，库负责所有内务处理。

blocks.html_en: • As audio is added, the event listener you added will be called, giving information about the latest speech.

sentences.en: As audio is added, the event listener you added will be called, giving information about the latest speech.
sentences.zh: 添加音频时，将调用您添加的事件侦听器，提供有关最新语音的信息。

blocks.html_en: In a real application you'd be calling add_audio() from an audio handler that's receiving it from your source. Since the library can handle arbitrary durations and sample rates, just make sure it's mono and otherwise feed it in as-is.

sentences.en: In a real application you'd be calling add_audio() from an audio handler that's receiving it from your source.
sentences.zh: 在真实的应用程序中，您将从从源接收音频处理程序中调用 add_audio() 。

sentences.en: Since the library can handle arbitrary durations and sample rates, just make sure it's mono and otherwise feed it in as-is.
sentences.zh: 由于该库可以处理任意持续时间和采样率，因此只需确保它是单声道的，否则按原样输入。

blocks.html_en: The transcriber analyses the speech at a default interval of every 500ms of input. You can change this with the update_interval argument to the transcriber constructor. For streaming models most of the work is done as the audio is being added, and it's automatically done at the end of a phrase, so changing this won't usually affect the workload or latency massively.

sentences.en: The transcriber analyses the speech at a default interval of every 500ms of input.
sentences.zh: 转录器以每 500 毫秒输入的默认间隔分析语音。

sentences.en: You can change this with the update_interval argument to the transcriber constructor.
sentences.zh: 您可以使用转录器构造函数的 update_interval 参数更改此设置。

sentences.en: For streaming models most of the work is done as the audio is being added, and it's automatically done at the end of a phrase, so changing this won't usually affect the workload or latency massively.
sentences.zh: 对于流模型，大部分工作是在添加音频时完成的，并且是在短语末尾自动完成的，因此更改此设置通常不会对工作负载或延迟产生很大影响。

blocks.html_en: The key takeaway is that you usually don't need to worry about the transcript data structure itself, the event system tells you when something important happens. You can manually trigger a transcript update by calling update_transcription() which returns a transcript object with all of the information about the current session if you do need to examine the state.

sentences.en: The key takeaway is that you usually don't need to worry about the transcript data structure itself, the event system tells you when something important happens.
sentences.zh: 关键的一点是，您通常不需要担心转录数据结构本身，事件系统会告诉您何时发生重要的事情。

sentences.en: You can manually trigger a transcript update by calling update_transcription() which returns a transcript object with all of the information about the current session if you do need to examine the state.
sentences.zh: 如果您确实需要检查状态，您可以通过调用 update_transcription() 来手动触发脚本更新，该方法会返回一个脚本对象，其中包含有关当前会话的所有信息。

blocks.html_en: By calling start() and stop() on a transcriber (or stream) we're beginning and ending a session. Each session has one transcript document associated with it, and it is started fresh on every start() call, so you should make copies of any data you need from the transcript object before that.

sentences.en: By calling start() and stop() on a transcriber (or stream) we're beginning and ending a session.
sentences.zh: 通过在转录器（或流）上调用 start() 和 stop()，我们可以开始和结束会话。

sentences.en: Each session has one transcript document associated with it, and it is started fresh on every start() call, so you should make copies of any data you need from the transcript object before that.
sentences.zh: 每个会话都有一个与其关联的脚本文档，并且它会在每次 start() 调用时重新启动，因此您应该在此之前从脚本对象中复制所需的任何数据。

blocks.html_en: The transcriber class also offers a simpler transcribe_without_streaming() method, for when you have an array of data from the past that you just want to analyse, such as a file or recording.

sentences.en: The transcriber class also offers a simpler transcribe_without_streaming() method, for when you have an array of data from the past that you just want to analyse, such as a file or recording.
sentences.zh: transcriber 类还提供了一个更简单的 transcribe_without_streaming() 方法，适用于当您只想分析过去的一组数据（例如文件或录音）时。

blocks.html_en: We also offer a specialization of the base Transcriber class called MicTranscriber. How this is implemented will depend on the language and platform, but it should provide a transcriber that's automatically attached to the main microphone on the system. This makes it straightforward to start transcribing speech from that common source, since it supports all of the same listener callbacks as the base class.

sentences.en: We also offer a specialization of the base Transcriber class called MicTranscriber.
sentences.zh: 我们还提供了称为 MicTranscriber 的基本 Transcriber 类的专门化。

sentences.en: How this is implemented will depend on the language and platform, but it should provide a transcriber that's automatically attached to the main microphone on the system.
sentences.zh: 如何实现取决于语言和平台，但它应该提供一个自动连接到系统主麦克风的转录器。

sentences.en: This makes it straightforward to start transcribing speech from that common source, since it supports all of the same listener callbacks as the base class.
sentences.zh: 这使得从该公共源开始转录语音变得简单，因为它支持与基类相同的所有侦听器回调。

blocks.html_en: Transcription Event Flow

sentences.en: Transcription Event Flow
sentences.zh: 转录事件流程

blocks.html_en: The main communication channel between the library and your application is through events that are passed to any listener functions you have registered. There are five major event types:

sentences.en: The main communication channel between the library and your application is through events that are passed to any listener functions you have registered.
sentences.zh: 库和应用程序之间的主要通信通道是通过传递给您已注册的任何侦听器函数的事件。

sentences.en: There are five major event types:
sentences.zh: 主要事件类型有五种：

blocks.html_en: • LineStarted. This is sent to listeners when the beginning of a new speech segment is detected. It may or may not contain any text, but since it's dispatched near the start of an utterance, that text is likely to change over time.

sentences.en: LineStarted.
sentences.zh: 线路开始。

sentences.en: This is sent to listeners when the beginning of a new speech segment is detected.
sentences.zh: 当检测到新语音片段的开始时，该信息会发送给听众。

sentences.en: It may or may not contain any text, but since it's dispatched near the start of an utterance, that text is likely to change over time.
sentences.zh: 它可能包含也可能不包含任何文本，但由于它是在话语开始附近发送的，因此该文本可能会随着时间的推移而改变。

blocks.html_en: • LineUpdated. Called whenever any of the information about a line changes, including the duration, audio data, and text.

sentences.en: LineUpdated.
sentences.zh: 线路已更新。

sentences.en: Called whenever any of the information about a line changes, including the duration, audio data, and text.
sentences.zh: 每当有关线路的任何信息发生更改（包括持续时间、音频数据和文本）时调用。

blocks.html_en: • LineTextChanged. Called only when the text associated with a line is updated. This is a subset of LineUpdated that focuses on the common need to refresh the text shown to users as often as possible to keep the experience interactive.

sentences.en: LineTextChanged.
sentences.zh: 行文本已更改。

sentences.en: Called only when the text associated with a line is updated.
sentences.zh: 仅当与行关联的文本更新时调用。

sentences.en: This is a subset of LineUpdated that focuses on the common need to refresh the text shown to users as often as possible to keep the experience interactive.
sentences.zh: 这是 LineUpdated 的一个子集，专注于尽可能频繁地刷新向用户显示的文本以保持体验交互的常见需求。

blocks.html_en: • LineSpeakersChanged. Only fired when the opt-in identify_speakers option is enabled. Called when the speaker spans attached to a line change. Unlike the other line events, this can fire for lines that are already complete, because the diarization algorithm keeps refining its speaker assignments as more audio arrives.

sentences.en: LineSpeakersChanged.
sentences.zh: LineSpeakers 已更改。

sentences.en: Only fired when the opt-in identify_speakers option is enabled.
sentences.zh: 仅当启用 opt-in recognize_speakers 选项时才会触发。

sentences.en: Called when the speaker spans attached to a line change.
sentences.zh: 当扬声器跨越连接到线路变化时调用。

sentences.en: Unlike the other line events, this can fire for lines that are already complete, because the diarization algorithm keeps refining its speaker assignments as more audio arrives.
sentences.zh: 与其他线路事件不同，这可能会触发已经完成的线路，因为随着更多音频到达，二值化算法会不断完善其扬声器分配。

blocks.html_en: • LineCompleted. Sent when we detect that someone has paused speaking, and we've ended the current segment. The line data structure has the final values for the text and duration.

sentences.en: LineCompleted.
sentences.zh: 线路已完成。

sentences.en: Sent when we detect that someone has paused speaking, and we've ended the current segment.
sentences.zh: 当我们检测到有人暂停讲话并且我们已经结束当前片段时发送。

sentences.en: The line data structure has the final values for the text and duration.
sentences.zh: 行数据结构具有文本和持续时间的最终值。

blocks.html_en: We offer some guarantees about these events:

sentences.en: We offer some guarantees about these events:
sentences.zh: 我们对这些事件提供一些保证：

blocks.html_en: • LineStarted is always called exactly once for any segment.

sentences.en: LineStarted is always called exactly once for any segment.
sentences.zh: 对于任何段，LineStarted 总是只调用一次。

blocks.html_en: • LineCompleted is always called exactly once after LineStarted for any segment.

sentences.en: LineCompleted is always called exactly once after LineStarted for any segment.
sentences.zh: 对于任何段，LineCompleted 始终在 LineStarted 之后调用一次。

blocks.html_en: • LineUpdated and LineTextChanged will only ever be called after the LineStarted and before the LineCompleted events for a segment.

sentences.en: LineUpdated and LineTextChanged will only ever be called after the LineStarted and before the LineCompleted events for a segment.
sentences.zh: LineUpdated 和 LineTextChanged 只会在段的 LineStarted 之后和 LineCompleted 事件之前调用。

blocks.html_en: • Those update events are not guaranteed to be called (and in practice can be disabled by setting update_interval to a very large value).

sentences.en: Those update events are not guaranteed to be called (and in practice can be disabled by setting update_interval to a very large value).
sentences.zh: 不保证调用这些更新事件（实际上可以通过将 update_interval 设置为非常大的值来禁用）。

blocks.html_en: • There will only be one line active at any one time for any given stream.

sentences.en: There will only be one line active at any one time for any given stream.
sentences.zh: 对于任何给定的流，在任何时候都只有一条线路处于活动状态。

blocks.html_en: • Once LineCompleted has been called, the library will never alter that line's text, timing, or audio data again. The one exception is the line's speaker spans: when identify_speakers is enabled, those can be revised for recent audio (signaled by LineSpeakersChanged), since diarization re-clusters a sliding window of recent speech. Assignments for audio older than diarization_cluster_window_sec are frozen.

sentences.en: Once LineCompleted has been called, the library will never alter that line's text, timing, or audio data again.
sentences.zh: 一旦调用 LineCompleted，库将永远不会再次更改该行的文本、计时或音频数据。

sentences.en: The one exception is the line's speaker spans: when identify_speakers is enabled, those can be revised for recent audio (signaled by LineSpeakersChanged), since diarization re-clusters a sliding window of recent speech.
sentences.zh: 一个例外是线路的扬声器跨度：当启用 recognize_speakers 时，可以针对最近的音频（由 LineSpeakersChanged 发出信号）修改这些跨度，因为二值化会重新聚类最近语音的滑动窗口。

sentences.en: Assignments for audio older than diarization_cluster_window_sec are frozen.
sentences.zh: 早于 diarization_cluster_window_sec 的音频分配被冻结。

blocks.html_en: • If stop() is called on a transcriber or stream, any active lines will have LineCompleted called.

sentences.en: If stop() is called on a transcriber or stream, any active lines will have LineCompleted called.
sentences.zh: 如果在转录器或流上调用 stop()，则任何活动行都将调用 LineCompleted。

blocks.html_en: • Each line has a 64-bit lineId that is designed to be unique enough to avoid collisions.

sentences.en: Each line has a 64-bit lineId that is designed to be unique enough to avoid collisions.
sentences.zh: 每条线路都有一个 64 位 lineId，其设计足够唯一以避免冲突。

blocks.html_en: • This lineId remains the same for the line over time, from the first LineStarted event onwards.

sentences.en: This lineId remains the same for the line over time, from the first LineStarted event onwards.
sentences.zh: 从第一个 LineStarted 事件开始，随着时间的推移，该线路的 lineId 保持不变。

blocks.html_en: Getting Started with a Conversational Agent

sentences.en: Getting Started with a Conversational Agent
sentences.zh: 会话代理入门

blocks.html_en: Many applications need a voice agent that can understand what users are saying and respond appropriately. To make this as straightforward as possible, we let you define different conversational flows. A flow can be as simple as responding to a query, or be a multi-step, branching conversation that takes actions.

sentences.en: Many applications need a voice agent that can understand what users are saying and respond appropriately.
sentences.zh: 许多应用程序需要一个能够理解用户所说内容并做出适当响应的语音代理。

sentences.en: To make this as straightforward as possible, we let you define different conversational flows.
sentences.zh: 为了使这尽可能简单，我们让您定义不同的对话流程。

sentences.en: A flow can be as simple as responding to a query, or be a multi-step, branching conversation that takes actions.
sentences.zh: 流程可以像响应查询一样简单，也可以是采取操作的多步骤、分支对话。

blocks.html_en: To define these flows, you used a DialogFlow object, with callbacks that take Dialog arguments. Here's an example of a simple flow, taken from the github.com/moonshine-ai/pi-help-bot sample code:

sentences.en: To define these flows, you used a DialogFlow object, with callbacks that take Dialog arguments.
sentences.zh: 为了定义这些流，您使用了 DialogFlow 对象，以及采用 Dialog 参数的回调。

sentences.en: Here's an example of a simple flow, taken from the github.com/moonshine-ai/pi-help-bot sample code:
sentences.zh: 以下是一个简单流程的示例，取自 github.com/moonshine-ai/pi-help-bot 示例代码：

blocks.html_en: This registers the report_ip_address() function to be called whenever the user says anything similar to "What is my IP address?". The matching is done semantically, so alternative phrasings like "Tell me your IP address" or "Can you tell me the local IP address?" should trigger it too. You can register as many top-level conversation starters as you'd like, the system will listen out and route to the closest in meaning.

sentences.en: This registers the report_ip_address() function to be called whenever the user says anything similar to "What is my IP address?".
sentences.zh: 这注册了report_ip_address()函数，每当用户说出类似于“我的IP地址是什么？”的内容时就会调用该函数。

sentences.en: The matching is done semantically, so alternative phrasings like "Tell me your IP address" or "Can you tell me the local IP address?"
sentences.zh: 匹配是在语义上完成的，因此可以使用“告诉我你的 IP 地址”或“你能告诉我本地 IP 地址吗？”之类的替代短语。

sentences.en: should trigger it too.
sentences.zh: 也应该触发它。

sentences.en: You can register as many top-level conversation starters as you'd like, the system will listen out and route to the closest in meaning.
sentences.zh: 您可以根据需要注册任意数量的顶级对话起始者，系统将监听并路由到含义最接近的。

blocks.html_en: The function itself receives a Dialog argument that represents the current conversational exchange. In this simple case we don't need any additional input from the user so we just use it to say() the information that was requested. We break the IP address into separate words for each digit for clarity, and replace the connecting periods with explicit "dot"s, so that 192.178.4.72 becomes "1 9 2 dot 1 7 8 dot 4 dot 72", since that's the conventional way to articulate them in speech.

sentences.en: The function itself receives a Dialog argument that represents the current conversational exchange.
sentences.zh: 该函数本身接收一个代表当前对话交换的 Dialog 参数。

sentences.en: In this simple case we don't need any additional input from the user so we just use it to say() the information that was requested.
sentences.zh: 在这个简单的情况下，我们不需要用户提供任何额外的输入，因此我们只需使用它来 say() 所请求的信息。

sentences.en: We break the IP address into separate words for each digit for clarity, and replace the connecting periods with explicit "dot"s, so that 192.178.4.72 becomes "1 9 2 dot 1 7 8 dot 4 dot 72", since that's the conventional way to articulate them in speech.
sentences.zh: 为了清楚起见，我们将 IP 地址分成每个数字的单独单词，并用明确的“点”替换连接句点，以便 192.178.4.72 变为“1 9 2 点 1 7 8 点 4 点 72”，因为这是在语音中表达它们的传统方式。

blocks.html_en: For more complex conversations, like setting up a new wifi network, you can define multiple steps and branch points directly in Python:

sentences.en: For more complex conversations, like setting up a new wifi network, you can define multiple steps and branch points directly in Python:
sentences.zh: 对于更复杂的对话，例如设置新的 wifi 网络，您可以直接在 Python 中定义多个步骤和分支点：

blocks.html_en: The first thing the function does is ask the user to give them the name of the network they want to join, through the call:

sentences.en: The first thing the function does is ask the user to give them the name of the network they want to join, through the call:
sentences.zh: 该函数做的第一件事是要求用户通过调用提供他们想要加入的网络的名称：

blocks.html_en: The Dialog class lets you ask users questions and will return the string containing the what they said in response. The only unusual feature here, compared to regular Python code, is the yield keyword. Because it may take some time for the user to respond, we call yield to hand back control to the main script until their response has been received. This is a general pattern for DialogFlow and you'll see it wherever we're waiting for the user to say something, to avoid blocking.

sentences.en: The Dialog class lets you ask users questions and will return the string containing the what they said in response.
sentences.zh: Dialog 类允许您向用户提问，并返回包含用户回答内容的字符串。

sentences.en: The only unusual feature here, compared to regular Python code, is the yield keyword.
sentences.zh: 与常规 Python 代码相比，这里唯一不寻常的功能是yield 关键字。

sentences.en: Because it may take some time for the user to respond, we call yield to hand back control to the main script until their response has been received.
sentences.zh: 因为用户可能需要一些时间才能响应，所以我们调用yield 将控制权交还给主脚本，直到收到他们的响应。

sentences.en: This is a general pattern for DialogFlow and you'll see it wherever we're waiting for the user to say something, to avoid blocking.
sentences.zh: 这是 DialogFlow 的通用模式，无论我们在等待用户说话以避免阻塞的任何地方，您都会看到它。

blocks.html_en: Our example application supports a few different input methods - running through a list of networks, spelling out the first few letters, or saying the name. Here we implement the list approach by looping through all the available networks and asking the user whether each is the one they want. Here you can see that regular loops and conditional statements work as you'd expect in Python.

sentences.en: Our example application supports a few different input methods - running through a list of networks, spelling out the first few letters, or saying the name.
sentences.zh: 我们的示例应用程序支持几种不同的输入方法 - 运行网络列表、拼写出前几个字母或说出名称。

sentences.en: Here we implement the list approach by looping through all the available networks and asking the user whether each is the one they want.
sentences.zh: 在这里，我们通过循环所有可用网络并询问用户是否每个网络都是他们想要的来实现列表方法。

sentences.en: Here you can see that regular loops and conditional statements work as you'd expect in Python.
sentences.zh: 在这里您可以看到常规循环和条件语句按照您期望的方式在 Python 中工作。

blocks.html_en: For each network, we call confirm(), which asks a question and then waits for a positive or negative result. Like all matching in the system this is done semantically, so "okay", "affirmative", and "go ahead" will work as well as a straightforward "yes".

sentences.en: For each network, we call confirm(), which asks a question and then waits for a positive or negative result.
sentences.zh: 对于每个网络，我们调用confirm()，它提出一个问题，然后等待肯定或否定的结果。

sentences.en: Like all matching in the system this is done semantically, so "okay", "affirmative", and "go ahead" will work as well as a straightforward "yes".
sentences.zh: 与系统中的所有匹配一样，这是在语义上完成的，因此“好的”、“肯定”和“继续”将与简单的“是”一样有效。

blocks.html_en: Password input is tricky, because they consist of arbitrary letters, digits, and symbols, and so they have to be spelled out by the user. Moonshine supports this through the mode=SPELLED argument. This asks the user to spell out each character, and uses a fine-tuned model to recognise what the user is saying for each. As well as supporting regular utterances like "aitch" or "capital zee", it also supports the NATO alphabet ("alpha", "bravo", etc) and even short descriptive phrases like "E as in elephant". It repeats back what it heard, and lets you delete mistakes.

sentences.en: Password input is tricky, because they consist of arbitrary letters, digits, and symbols, and so they have to be spelled out by the user.
sentences.zh: 密码输入很棘手，因为它们由任意字母、数字和符号组成，因此必须由用户拼写出来。

sentences.en: Moonshine supports this through the mode=SPELLED argument.
sentences.zh: Moonshine 通过 mode=SPELLED 参数支持这一点。

sentences.en: This asks the user to spell out each character, and uses a fine-tuned model to recognise what the user is saying for each.
sentences.zh: 这要求用户拼写出每个字符，并使用微调模型来识别用户对每个字符所说的内容。

sentences.en: As well as supporting regular utterances like "aitch" or "capital zee", it also supports the NATO alphabet ("alpha", "bravo", etc) and even short descriptive phrases like "E as in elephant".
sentences.zh: 除了支持“aitch”或“大写 zee”等常规话语外，它还支持 NATO 字母表（“alpha”、“bravo”等），甚至支持“E as inelephant”等简短的描述性短语。

sentences.en: It repeats back what it heard, and lets you delete mistakes.
sentences.zh: 它会重复听到的内容，并让您删除错误。

blocks.html_en: The flow also works with other control structures like exception handlers, so you can specify your conversations using idiomatic code, even for error recovery.

sentences.en: The flow also works with other control structures like exception handlers, so you can specify your conversations using idiomatic code, even for error recovery.
sentences.zh: 该流程还可以与异常处理程序等其他控制结构配合使用，因此您可以使用惯用代码指定对话，甚至可以进行错误恢复。

blocks.html_en: To give this a try for yourself, run this built-in example:

sentences.en: To give this a try for yourself, run this built-in example:
sentences.zh: 要亲自尝试一下，请运行以下内置示例：

blocks.html_en: Agent Setup

sentences.en: Agent Setup
sentences.zh: 代理设置

blocks.html_en: An agent needs a speech-to-text Transcriber object to receive input, an IntentRecognizer to understand the input, and a TextToSpeech object to respond:

sentences.en: An agent needs a speech-to-text Transcriber object to receive input, an IntentRecognizer to understand the input, and a TextToSpeech object to respond:
sentences.zh: 代理需要一个语音到文本 Transcriber 对象来接收输入，一个 IntentRecognizer 来理解输入，以及一个 TextToSpeech 对象来响应：

blocks.html_en: The add_commands() function calls register_flow() for all of the phrases the agent should recognize.

sentences.en: The add_commands() function calls register_flow() for all of the phrases the agent should recognize.
sentences.zh: add_commands() 函数调用 register_flow() 来获取代理应识别的所有短语。

blocks.html_en: Getting Started with Text to Speech

sentences.en: Getting Started with Text to Speech
sentences.zh: 文本转语音入门

blocks.html_en: Voice interfaces often need to talk back, and Moonshine's TextToSpeech is designed to make that easy, across multiple languages. It's also self-contained, so you can use it independently from the transcription and intent recognition modules.

sentences.en: Voice interfaces often need to talk back, and Moonshine's TextToSpeech is designed to make that easy, across multiple languages.
sentences.zh: 语音界面通常需要进行回复，而 Moonshine 的 TextToSpeech 旨在跨多种语言轻松实现这一点。

sentences.en: It's also self-contained, so you can use it independently from the transcription and intent recognition modules.
sentences.zh: 它也是独立的，因此您可以独立于转录和意图识别模块使用它。

blocks.html_en: At its simplest, you can just specify the output language to create a speech synthesizer object and then pass text into it to speak it on the default audio device:

sentences.en: At its simplest, you can just specify the output language to create a speech synthesizer object and then pass text into it to speak it on the default audio device:
sentences.zh: 最简单的是，您只需指定输出语言来创建语音合成器对象，然后将文本传递到其中以在默认音频设备上说出它：

blocks.html_en: say() returns immediately and queues the text for background synthesis and playback. Calling say() multiple times queues each utterance in order, and the next utterance is pre-synthesized while the current one plays. You can also pass a list of strings, cancel everything with stop(), or poll with is_talking():

sentences.en: say() returns immediately and queues the text for background synthesis and playback.
sentences.zh: say() 立即返回并将文本排队以供后台合成和播放。

sentences.en: Calling say() multiple times queues each utterance in order, and the next utterance is pre-synthesized while the current one plays.
sentences.zh: 多次调用 say() 会将每个话语按顺序排队，并且在播放当前话语时预先合成下一个话语。

sentences.en: You can also pass a list of strings, cancel everything with stop(), or poll with is_talking():
sentences.zh: 您还可以传递字符串列表，使用 stop() 取消所有内容，或使用 is_talking() 进行轮询：

blocks.html_en: If you're on a machine without an audio output, or want to do further processing, you can retrieve the audio samples using the synthesize() method:

sentences.en: If you're on a machine without an audio output, or want to do further processing, you can retrieve the audio samples using the synthesize() method:
sentences.zh: 如果您使用的计算机没有音频输出，或者想要进行进一步处理，您可以使用 Synthesize() 方法检索音频样本：

blocks.html_en: As you can see, text to speech supports multiple languages. To see which are available, run the list_tts_languages() function:

sentences.en: As you can see, text to speech supports multiple languages.
sentences.zh: 如您所见，文本转语音支持多种语言。

sentences.en: To see which are available, run the list_tts_languages() function:
sentences.zh: 要查看可用的语言，请运行 list_tts_languages() 函数：

blocks.html_en: For each language, you can list which voices are available:

sentences.en: For each language, you can list which voices are available:
sentences.zh: 对于每种语言，您可以列出可用的语音：

blocks.html_en: If a voice is marked as downloadable that means if you pass it in to the TextToSpeech constructor then Moonshine will download it to a cache automatically (as long as the download argument is its default true) and will be available on your machine with no internet access required for subsequent calls.

sentences.en: If a voice is marked as downloadable that means if you pass it in to the TextToSpeech constructor then Moonshine will download it to a cache automatically (as long as the download argument is its default true) and will be available on your machine with no internet access required for subsequent calls.
sentences.zh: 如果语音被标记为可下载，这意味着如果您将其传递给 TextToSpeech 构造函数，那么 Moonshine 会自动将其下载到缓存（只要 download 参数为其默认 true），并且可以在您的计算机上使用，后续调用无需访问 Internet。

blocks.html_en: Voice Cloning

sentences.en: Voice Cloning
sentences.zh: 语音克隆

blocks.html_en: The integrated ZipVoice model can imitate someone's voice, given a short audio clip. Pass the clip to the TextToSpeech constructor's clone argument, either as a path to a .wav file or as a (pcm, sample_rate) pair of mono float samples. You can also pass clone_transcript, the text spoken in the clip; when omitted, Moonshine auto-transcribes the clip with its ASR model before cloning (this takes a few extra seconds on first use):

sentences.en: The integrated ZipVoice model can imitate someone's voice, given a short audio clip.
sentences.zh: 集成的 ZipVoice 模型可以模仿某人的声音（给定一个简短的音频剪辑）。

sentences.en: Pass the clip to the TextToSpeech constructor's clone argument, either as a path to a .wav file or as a (pcm, sample_rate) pair of mono float samples.
sentences.zh: 将剪辑传递给 TextToSpeech 构造函数的克隆参数，作为 .wav 文件的路径或作为（pcm，sample_rate）单浮点样本对。

sentences.en: You can also pass clone_transcript, the text spoken in the clip; when omitted, Moonshine auto-transcribes the clip with its ASR model before cloning (this takes a few extra seconds on first use):
sentences.zh: 您还可以传递clone_transcript，即剪辑中所说的文本；如果省略，Moonshine 会在克隆之前使用其 ASR 模型自动转录剪辑（首次使用时需要额外花费几秒钟）：

blocks.html_en: The ZipVoice engine is selected automatically when clone is set, so no voice argument is needed (passing a voice together with clone raises an error).

sentences.en: The ZipVoice engine is selected automatically when clone is set, so no voice argument is needed (passing a voice together with clone raises an error).
sentences.zh: 设置克隆时会自动选择 ZipVoice 引擎，因此不需要语音参数（将语音与克隆一起传递会引发错误）。

blocks.html_en: You can also try cloning from the command line. Since you won't always have easy access to a clean transcript of the speech you want to clone from, you can leave it out and have Moonshine automatically generate one, in both the API and command line.

sentences.en: You can also try cloning from the command line.
sentences.zh: 您还可以尝试从命令行进行克隆。

sentences.en: Since you won't always have easy access to a clean transcript of the speech you want to clone from, you can leave it out and have Moonshine automatically generate one, in both the API and command line.
sentences.zh: 由于您并不总是能够轻松访问要从中克隆的语音的干净转录本，因此您可以将其省略，让 Moonshine 在 API 和命令行中自动生成一份转录本。

blocks.html_en: Voice Samples

sentences.en: Voice Samples
sentences.zh: 语音样本

blocks.html_en: To help you choose a voice, here are sample clips of each one saying "Welcome to Moonshine Voice text to speech". Each entry is the voice name you can pass to the TextToSpeech constructor; click the ▶ next to it to hear it.

sentences.en: To help you choose a voice, here are sample clips of each one saying "Welcome to Moonshine Voice text to speech".
sentences.zh: 为了帮助您选择声音，以下是每个声音的示例剪辑：“欢迎使用 Moonshine Voice 文本转语音”。

sentences.en: Each entry is the voice name you can pass to the TextToSpeech constructor; click the ▶ next to it to hear it.
sentences.zh: 每个条目都是您可以传递给 TextToSpeech 构造函数的语音名称；单击旁边的 ▶ 即可收听。

blocks.html_en: ZipVoice

sentences.en: ZipVoice

blocks.html_en: These voices were created using the zero-shot voice cloning capabilities of ZipVoice, a high-quality flow-matching TTS model from the k2-fsa team. It takes significantly longer to generate than Kokoro or PiperTTS, but offers voice cloning and more realistic speech.

sentences.en: These voices were created using the zero-shot voice cloning capabilities of ZipVoice, a high-quality flow-matching TTS model from the k2-fsa team.
sentences.zh: 这些声音是使用 ZipVoice 的零样本语音克隆功能创建的，ZipVoice 是 k2-fsa 团队的高质量流匹配 TTS 模型。

sentences.en: It takes significantly longer to generate than Kokoro or PiperTTS, but offers voice cloning and more realistic speech.
sentences.zh: 它的生成时间比 Kokoro 或 PiperTTS 长得多，但提供语音克隆和更真实的语音。

blocks.html_en: Piper TTS

sentences.en: Piper TTS
sentences.zh: 派珀 TTS

blocks.html_en: The Piper project provides over a hundred lightweight voices across all of the languages Moonshine supports, from many contributors — too many to sample here. You can listen to every Piper voice on the Piper voice samples page, and use any of them with Moonshine through the piper_ voice names returned by list_tts_voices().

sentences.en: The Piper project provides over a hundred lightweight voices across all of the languages Moonshine supports, from many contributors — too many to sample here.
sentences.zh: Piper 项目提供了超过一百种轻量级语音，涵盖了 Moonshine 支持的所有语言，这些语音来自许多贡献者——太多了，这里无法举例。

sentences.en: You can listen to every Piper voice on the Piper voice samples page, and use any of them with Moonshine through the piper_ voice names returned by list_tts_voices().
sentences.zh: 您可以在 Piper 语音示例页面上收听每个 Piper 语音，并通过 list_tts_voices() 返回的 Piper_ 语音名称将其中任何语音与 Moonshine 一起使用。

blocks.html_en: Converting Graphemes to Phonemes

sentences.en: Converting Graphemes to Phonemes
sentences.zh: 将字素转换为音素

blocks.html_en: As you may notice from the voice names, Moonshine Voice uses models from the fantastic Kokoro and PiperTTS projects. You can find full details on all the model and data sources we use for text to speech at core/moonshine-tts/data/README.md.

sentences.en: As you may notice from the voice names, Moonshine Voice uses models from the fantastic Kokoro and PiperTTS projects.
sentences.zh: 正如您可能从语音名称中注意到的那样，Moonshine Voice 使用来自出色的 Kokoro 和 PiperTTS 项目的模型。

sentences.en: You can find full details on all the model and data sources we use for text to speech at core/moonshine-tts/data/README.md.
sentences.zh: 您可以在 core/moonshine-tts/data/README.md 中找到有关我们用于文本转语音的所有模型和数据源的完整详细信息。

blocks.html_en: Given that there are other great TTS projects out there, why does the world need yet another implementation? Moonshine tries to run on as many platforms as possible and supports commercial applications, and both Kokoro and Piper use espeak-ng to convert text strings into phonemes, representations of the noises associated with the sentence, in the International Pronunciation Alphabet. Espeak-ng is licensed under the GPL, and while I am a fan of free software, the terms do make it hard to incorporate into applications that don't also release their source code under a similar license.

sentences.en: Given that there are other great TTS projects out there, why does the world need yet another implementation?
sentences.zh: 鉴于还有其他出色的 TTS 项目，为什么世界还需要另一个实施方案？

sentences.en: Moonshine tries to run on as many platforms as possible and supports commercial applications, and both Kokoro and Piper use espeak-ng to convert text strings into phonemes, representations of the noises associated with the sentence, in the International Pronunciation Alphabet.
sentences.zh: Moonshine 尝试在尽可能多的平台上运行并支持商业应用程序，Kokoro 和 Piper 都使用 espeak-ng 将文本字符串转换为国际发音字母表中的音素，即与句子相关的噪音的表示。

sentences.en: Espeak-ng is licensed under the GPL, and while I am a fan of free software, the terms do make it hard to incorporate into applications that don't also release their source code under a similar license.
sentences.zh: Espeak-ng 根据 GPL 获得许可，虽然我是自由软件的粉丝，但这些条款确实使其很难合并到未在类似许可下发布源代码的应用程序中。

blocks.html_en: In the cloud this isn't as much of an issue, as many uses of espeak-ng can be implemented by calling out to an external executable, so the dependency isn't as problematic. This isn't an option on many edge operating systems unfortunately, as the only way to include code on iOS or Android is to link it into the application, which requires open sourcing the calling code.

sentences.en: In the cloud this isn't as much of an issue, as many uses of espeak-ng can be implemented by calling out to an external executable, so the dependency isn't as problematic.
sentences.zh: 在云中，这并不是什么大问题，因为 espeak-ng 的许多用途可以通过调用外部可执行文件来实现，因此依赖性不那么有问题。

sentences.en: This isn't an option on many edge operating systems unfortunately, as the only way to include code on iOS or Android is to link it into the application, which requires open sourcing the calling code.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，在许多边缘操作系统上这并不是一个选项，因为在 iOS 或 Android 上包含代码的唯一方法是将其链接到应用程序中，这需要开源调用代码。

blocks.html_en: To allow wider usage, we developed our own "grapheme to phoneme" module that performs a similar role, but has been written from scratch. You'll find the implementation in core/moonshine-tts and it's released under the same MIT License as the rest of this code base.

sentences.en: To allow wider usage, we developed our own "grapheme to phoneme" module that performs a similar role, but has been written from scratch.
sentences.zh: 为了允许更广泛的使用，我们开发了自己的“字素到音素”模块，该模块执行类似的作用，但是是从头开始编写的。

sentences.en: You'll find the implementation in core/moonshine-tts and it's released under the same MIT License as the rest of this code base.
sentences.zh: 您将在 core/moonshine-tts 中找到该实现，它与此代码库的其余部分在相同的 MIT 许可证下发布。

blocks.html_en: Every language requires a different process to convert its written form into speech, and often it varies by dialect too. This is why espeak-ng is so widely used, it has had years of work put into it to encode linguistic knowledge into a complex set of rules, many of which are heuristics that require a lot of testing to get right. The Moonshine Voice G2P engine is still new, and will need similar tuning to handle all of the variations across languages, but I'm hoping the initial implementation is a good start and will benefit from community feedback and contributions over time. Here are the current results for intelligibility across languages, using scripts/tts_g2p_intelligibility.py:

sentences.en: Every language requires a different process to convert its written form into speech, and often it varies by dialect too.
sentences.zh: 每种语言都需要不同的过程将其书面形式转换为语音，并且通常也因方言而异。

sentences.en: This is why espeak-ng is so widely used, it has had years of work put into it to encode linguistic knowledge into a complex set of rules, many of which are heuristics that require a lot of testing to get right.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么 espeak-ng 被如此广泛使用的原因，它经过多年的努力将语言知识编码成一组复杂的规则，其中许多规则都是启发式的，需要大量的测试才能正确。

sentences.en: The Moonshine Voice G2P engine is still new, and will need similar tuning to handle all of the variations across languages, but I'm hoping the initial implementation is a good start and will benefit from community feedback and contributions over time.
sentences.zh: Moonshine Voice G2P 引擎仍然是新的，需要类似的调整来处理跨语言的所有变化，但我希望最初的实现是一个良好的开始，并且随着时间的推移将受益于社区反馈和贡献。

sentences.en: Here are the current results for intelligibility across languages, using scripts/tts_g2p_intelligibility.py:

We use the usual tooling per stack (Android Studio and Gradle, Xcode and Swift on Apple platforms, Visual Studio on Windows). GitHub Releases currently ship the downloadable assets below (example trees are mostly named {platform}-{Project}.tar.gz; Windows and C++ also include prebuilt native library bundles).

blocks.html_en: The examples usually include one minimal project that just creates a transcriber and then feeds it data from a WAV file, and another that's pulling audio from a microphone using the platform's default framework for accessing audio devices. For Android, examples/android/IntentRecognizer is a self-contained Gradle project you can copy out of the tree: it depends on ai.moonshine:moonshine-voice:0.0.68 from Maven Central (includes IntentRecognizer) and bundles small English streaming ASR plus embeddinggemma-300m under app/src/main/assets/ (Git LFS).

sentences.en: The examples usually include one minimal project that just creates a transcriber and then feeds it data from a WAV file, and another that's pulling audio from a microphone using the platform's default framework for accessing audio devices.
sentences.zh: 这些示例通常包括一个最小项目，该项目仅创建一个转录器，然后从 WAV 文件向其提供数据，另一个项目使用平台的默认框架来访问音频设备，从麦克风中提取音频。

sentences.en: For Android, examples/android/IntentRecognizer is a self-contained Gradle project you can copy out of the tree: it depends on ai.moonshine:moonshine-voice:0.0.68 from Maven Central (includes IntentRecognizer) and bundles small English streaming ASR plus embeddinggemma-300m under app/src/main/assets/ (Git LFS).
sentences.zh: 对于 Android，examples/android/IntentRecognizer 是一个独立的 Gradle 项目，您可以从树中复制出来：它依赖于 Maven Central（包括 IntentRecognizer）的 ai.moonshine:moonshine-voice:0.0.68，并在 app/src/main/assets/ (Git LFS) 下捆绑小型英语流 ASR 和 embeddinggemma-300m。

blocks.html_en: Streaming weights are mirrored from assets to internal storage at runtime, then loaded with MicTranscriber.loadFromFiles and MOONSHINE_MODEL_ARCH_SMALL_STREAMING. examples/android/TextToSpeech is the same style of Gradle sample for on-device TTS: it uses the TextToSpeech class from moonshine-voice and bundles everything the default English voice needs to run fully offline — the Kokoro model, the af_alloy voice, and the en_us G2P + OOV files (dict_filtered_heteronyms.tsv, g2p-config.json, oov/model.onnx, oov/onnx-config.json) — under app/src/main/assets/tts-data/ (Git LFS).

sentences.en: Streaming weights are mirrored from assets to internal storage at runtime, then loaded with MicTranscriber.loadFromFiles and MOONSHINE_MODEL_ARCH_SMALL_STREAMING.
sentences.zh: 流权重在运行时从资产镜像到内部存储，然后使用 MicTranscriber.loadFromFiles 和 MOONSHINE_MODEL_ARCH_SMALL_STREAMING 加载。

sentences.en: examples/android/TextToSpeech is the same style of Gradle sample for on-device TTS: it uses the TextToSpeech class from moonshine-voice and bundles everything the default English voice needs to run fully offline — the Kokoro model, the af_alloy voice, and the en_us G2P + OOV files (dict_filtered_heteronyms.tsv, g2p-config.json, oov/model.onnx, oov/onnx-config.json) — under app/src/main/assets/tts-data/ (Git LFS).
sentences.zh: example/android/TextToSpeech 与设备上 TTS 的 Gradle 示例的风格相同：它使用 Moonshine-voice 中的 TextToSpeech 类，并捆绑了默认英语语音完全离线运行所需的所有内容 — Kokoro 模型、af_alloy 语音和 en_us G2P + OOV 文件（dict_filtered_heteronyms.tsv、g2p-config.json、oov/model.onnx、 oov/onnx-config.json) — 在 app/src/main/assets/tts-data/ (Git LFS) 下。

blocks.html_en: Every other voice — the full Kokoro catalog and Piper voices across all supported languages — is resolved from moonshine_get_tts_dependencies and downloaded on demand from https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/ the first time the user picks a voice that needs it, with a small progress indicator while assets are fetched. Downloads are cached under filesDir, so subsequent launches reuse them offline.

sentences.en: Every other voice — the full Kokoro catalog and Piper voices across all supported languages — is resolved from moonshine_get_tts_dependencies and downloaded on demand from https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/ the first time the user picks a voice that needs it, with a small progress indicator while assets are fetched.
sentences.zh: 所有其他声音（完整的 Kokoro 目录和所有受支持语言的 Piper 声音）均从 Moonshine_get_tts_dependency 解析，并在用户第一次选择需要的声音时从 https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/ 按需下载，并在获取资源时显示一个小进度指示器。

sentences.en: Downloads are cached under filesDir, so subsequent launches reuse them offline.
sentences.zh: 下载内容缓存在 filesDir 下，因此后续启动可以离线重用它们。

blocks.html_en: examples/ios/TextToSpeech follows the same pattern on Apple platforms: the Xcode project pulls MoonshineVoice from the Swift package and bundles the same Kokoro + af_alloy + en_us offline set under tts-data/ (Git LFS). On first launch the bundled tree is staged into Application Support/tts-data/, then TextToSpeech.getDependencies is used to download any missing files from https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/, with a progress indicator in the UI. Switching to a different voice triggers the same on-demand download, and cached files are reused on subsequent launches.

sentences.en: examples/ios/TextToSpeech follows the same pattern on Apple platforms: the Xcode project pulls MoonshineVoice from the Swift package and bundles the same Kokoro + af_alloy + en_us offline set under tts-data/ (Git LFS).
sentences.zh: example/ios/TextToSpeech 在 Apple 平台上遵循相同的模式：Xcode 项目从 Swift 包中提取 MoonshineVoice，并在 tts-data/ (Git LFS) 下捆绑相同的 Kokoro + af_alloy + en_us 离线集。

sentences.en: On first launch the bundled tree is staged into Application Support/tts-data/, then TextToSpeech.getDependencies is used to download any missing files from https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/, with a progress indicator in the UI.
sentences.zh: 首次启动时，捆绑的树会暂存在 Application Support/tts-data/ 中，然后使用 TextToSpeech.getDependency 从 https://download.moonshine.ai/tts/ 下载任何丢失的文件，并在 UI 中显示进度指示器。

sentences.en: Switching to a different voice triggers the same on-demand download, and cached files are reused on subsequent launches.
sentences.zh: 切换到不同的语音会触发相同的按需下载，并且缓存的文件会在后续启动时重复使用。

blocks.html_en: Adding the Library to your own App

sentences.en: Adding the Library to your own App

blocks.html_en: We distribute the library through the most widely-used package managers for each platform. Here's how you can use these to add the framework to an existing project on different systems.

sentences.en: We distribute the library through the most widely-used package managers for each platform.
sentences.zh: 我们通过每个平台最广泛使用的包管理器分发该库。

sentences.en: Here's how you can use these to add the framework to an existing project on different systems.
sentences.zh: 以下是您如何使用这些将框架添加到不同系统上的现有项目中。

blocks.html_en: Python

sentences.en: Python

blocks.html_en: The Python package is hosted on PyPi, so all you should need to do to install it is pip install moonshine-voice, and then import moonshine_voice in your project.
