blocks.html_en: iOS or MacOS

sentences.en: iOS or MacOS
sentences.zh: iOS 或 MacOS

blocks.html_en: For iOS we use the Swift Package Manager, with an auto-updated GitHub repository holding each version. To use this right-click on the file view sidebar in Xcode and choose "Add Package Dependencies..." from the menu. A dialog should open up, paste https://github.com/moonshine-ai/moonshine-swift/ into the top search box and you should see moonshine-swift. Select it and choose "Add Package", and it should be added to your project. You should now be able to import MoonshineVoice and use the library. You will need to add any model files you use to your app bundle and ensure they're copied during the deployment phase, so they can be accessed on-device.

sentences.en: For iOS we use the Swift Package Manager, with an auto-updated GitHub repository holding each version.
sentences.zh: 对于 iOS，我们使用 Swift 包管理器，并通过自动更新的 GitHub 存储库保存每个版本。

sentences.en: To use this right-click on the file view sidebar in Xcode and choose "Add Package Dependencies..."

sentences.en: from the menu.
sentences.zh: 从菜单中。

sentences.en: Select it and choose "Add Package", and it should be added to your project.
sentences.zh: 选择它并选择“添加包”，它应该添加到您的项目中。

blocks.html_en: Android

sentences.en: Android
sentences.zh: 安卓

blocks.html_en: On Android we publish the package to Maven. To include it in your project using Android Studio and Gradle, first add the version number you want to the gradle/libs.versions.toml file by inserting a line in the [versions] section, for example moonshineVoice = "0.0.68". Then in the [libraries] part, add a reference to the package: moonshine-voice = { group = "ai.moonshine", name = "moonshine-voice", version.ref = "moonshineVoice" }.

sentences.en: On Android we publish the package to Maven.
sentences.zh: 在 Android 上，我们将包发布到 Maven。

sentences.en: To include it in your project using Android Studio and Gradle, first add the version number you want to the gradle/libs.versions.toml file by inserting a line in the [versions] section, for example moonshineVoice = "0.0.68".
sentences.zh: 要将其包含在使用 Android Studio 和 Gradle 的项目中，请首先通过在 [versions] 部分中插入一行，将所需的版本号添加到 gradle/libs.versions.toml 文件中，例如 MoonshineVoice = "0.0.68"。

sentences.en: Then in the [libraries] part, add a reference to the package: moonshine-voice = { group = "ai.moonshine", name = "moonshine-voice", version.ref = "moonshineVoice" }.
sentences.zh: 然后在 [libraries] 部分中，添加对包的引用：moonshine-voice = { group = "ai.moonshine", name = "moonshine-voice", version.ref = "moonshineVoice" }。

blocks.html_en: Finally, in your app/build.gradle.kts add the library to the dependencies list: implementation(libs.moonshine.voice). The examples/android/IntentRecognizer and examples/android/TextToSpeech samples use the same coordinates (moonshineVoice = "0.0.68" in their catalogs).

sentences.en: Finally, in your app/build.gradle.kts add the library to the dependencies list: implementation(libs.moonshine.voice).
sentences.zh: 最后，在 app/build.gradle.kts 中将库添加到依赖项列表中：implementation(libs.moonshine.voice)。

sentences.en: The examples/android/IntentRecognizer and examples/android/TextToSpeech samples use the same coordinates (moonshineVoice = "0.0.68" in their catalogs).
sentences.zh: example/android/IntentRecognizer 和examples/android/TextToSpeech 示例使用相同的坐标（其目录中的moonshineVoice = "0.0.68"）。

sentences.en: Windows/C++
sentences.zh: 视窗/C++

blocks.html_en: We couldn't find a single package manager that is used by most Windows developers, so instead we've made the raw library and headers available as a download. The script in examples/windows/cli-transcriber/download-lib.bat will fetch these for you. You'll see an include folder that you should add to the include search paths in your project settings, and a lib directory that you should add to the include search paths. Then add all of the library files in the lib folder to your project's linker dependencies.

sentences.en: We couldn't find a single package manager that is used by most Windows developers, so instead we've made the raw library and headers available as a download.

sentences.en: You'll see an include folder that you should add to the include search paths in your project settings, and a lib directory that you should add to the include search paths.
sentences.zh: 您将看到一个应添加到项目设置中的包含搜索路径的包含文件夹，以及一个应添加到包含搜索路径的 lib 目录。

sentences.zh: 然后将 lib 文件夹中的所有库文件添加到项目的链接器依赖项中。

blocks.html_en: The recommended interface to use on Windows is the C++ language binding. This is a header-only library that offers a higher-level API than the underlying C version. You can #include "moonshine-cpp.h" to access Moonshine from your C++ code. If you want to see an example of all these changes together, take a look at examples/windows/cli-transcriber.

blocks.html_en: Debugging

sentences.en: Debugging
sentences.zh: 调试

blocks.html_en: Console Logs

sentences.en: Console Logs
sentences.zh: 控制台日志

blocks.html_en: The library is designed to help you understand what's going wrong when you hit an issue. If something isn't working as expected, the first place to look is the console for log messages. Whenever there's a failure point or an exception within the core library, you should see a message that adds more information about what went wrong. Your language bindings should also recognize when the core library has returned an error and raise an appropriate exception, but sometimes the logs can be helpful because they contain more details.

sentences.en: The library is designed to help you understand what's going wrong when you hit an issue.
sentences.zh: 该库旨在帮助您在遇到问题时了解出了什么问题。

sentences.en: If something isn't working as expected, the first place to look is the console for log messages.
sentences.zh: 如果某些东西没有按预期工作，首先要查看的地方是日志消息控制台。

sentences.en: Whenever there's a failure point or an exception within the core library, you should see a message that adds more information about what went wrong.
sentences.zh: 每当核心库中出现故障点或异常时，您都应该看到一条消息，其中添加了有关错误原因的更多信息。

sentences.en: Your language bindings should also recognize when the core library has returned an error and raise an appropriate exception, but sometimes the logs can be helpful because they contain more details.
sentences.zh: 您的语言绑定还应该识别核心库何时返回错误并引发适当的异常，但有时日志可能会有所帮助，因为它们包含更多详细信息。

To make this easier, you can pass in the save_input_wav_path option when you create a transcriber. That will save any audio received into .wav files in the folder you specify. Here's a Python example:

sentences.zh: 这是一个 Python 示例：

blocks.html_en: This will run test audio through a transcriber, and write out the audio it has received into an input_1.wav file in the current directory. If you're running multiple streams, you'll see input_2.wav, etc for each additional one. These wavs only contain the audio data from the latest session, and are overwritten after each one is started. Listening to these files should help you confirm that the input you're providing is as you expect it, and not distorted or corrupted.

blocks.html_en: API Call Logging

sentences.en: API Call Logging
sentences.zh: API调用记录

blocks.html_en: If you're running into errors it can be hard to keep track of the timeline of your interactions with the library. The log_api_calls option will print out the underlying API calls that have been triggered to the console, so you can investigate any ordering or timing issues.

sentences.en: If you're running into errors it can be hard to keep track of the timeline of your interactions with the library.

sentences.zh: log_api_calls 选项将打印出已触发到控制台的底层 API 调用，以便您可以调查任何顺序或计时问题。

blocks.html_en: Building from Source

blocks.html_en: If you want to debug into the library internals, or add instrumentation to help understand its operation, or add improvements or customizations, all of the source is available for you to build it for yourself.

sentences.zh: 如果您想调试库内部结构，或添加工具以帮助理解其操作，或添加改进或自定义，您可以使用所有源代码自行构建它。

blocks.html_en: Tip

Because the project includes large files like models, this repository uses git lfs. If you clone the repo and don't have lfs already set up, you'll see compile errors like moonshine/core/speaker-embedding-model-data.cpp:1:1: error: 'version' does not name a type. This is because LFS files are replaced by small text files that point to the actual location of the stored data, and if your git isn't aware of LFS it will happily leave them that way. The fix is to make sure you have git-lfs installed through your favorite OS package manager, and then run git lfs install before trying a new clone.

sentences.en: Because the project includes large files like models, this repository uses git lfs.
sentences.zh: 由于该项目包含模型等大文件，因此该存储库使用 git lfs。

sentences.en: If you clone the repo and don't have lfs already set up, you'll see compile errors like moonshine/core/speaker-embedding-model-data.cpp:1:1: error: 'version' does not name a type.
sentences.zh: 如果您克隆存储库并且尚未设置 lfs，您将看到类似 Moonshine/core/speaker-embedding-model-data.cpp:1:1: error: 'version' does not name a type 的编译错误。

sentences.en: This is because LFS files are replaced by small text files that point to the actual location of the stored data, and if your git isn't aware of LFS it will happily leave them that way.
sentences.zh: 这是因为 LFS 文件被替换为指向存储数据实际位置的小文本文件，如果您的 git 不知道 LFS，它会很高兴地让它们保持这种状态。

sentences.en: The fix is to make sure you have git-lfs installed through your favorite OS package manager, and then run git lfs install before trying a new clone.
sentences.zh: 修复方法是确保您通过您最喜欢的操作系统包管理器安装了 git-lfs，然后在尝试新克隆之前运行 git lfs install。

This is the script we call for every release, and it builds all of the artifacts we upload to the various package manager systems.

sentences.en: This is the script we call for every release, and it builds all of the artifacts we upload to the various package manager systems.

blocks.html_en: The different platforms and languages have a layer on top of the C interfaces to enable idiomatic use of the library within the different environments. The major systems have their own top-level folders in this repo, for example: python, android, and swift for iOS and MacOS. This is where you'll find the code that calls the underlying core library routines, and handles the event system for each platform.

sentences.en: The different platforms and languages have a layer on top of the C interfaces to enable idiomatic use of the library within the different environments.
sentences.zh: 不同的平台和语言在 C 接口之上都有一个层，以便能够在不同的环境中惯用地使用该库。

sentences.en: The major systems have their own top-level folders in this repo, for example: python, android, and swift for iOS and MacOS.
sentences.zh: 主要系统在此存储库中都有自己的顶级文件夹，例如：适用于 iOS 和 MacOS 的 python、android 和 swift。

sentences.en: This is where you'll find the code that calls the underlying core library routines, and handles the event system for each platform.
sentences.zh: 您可以在此处找到调用底层核心库例程并处理每个平台的事件系统的代码。

blocks.html_en: If you have a device that isn't supported, you can try building using cmake on your system. The only major dependency that the C++ core library has is the Onnx Runtime. We include pre-built binary library files for all our supported systems, but you'll need to find or build your own version if the libraries we offer don't cover your use case.

blocks.html_en: Intent Recognition Models

sentences.zh: 意图识别模型

sentences.en: Text to Speech Models
sentences.zh: 文本转语音模型

blocks.html_en: A large variety of models, dictionaries and other files are needed for TTS, and these vary widely by language. You can use the download module to pull down exactly what you need for a particular language, and optionally a voice:

sentences.en: A large variety of models, dictionaries and other files are needed for TTS, and these vary widely by language.
sentences.zh: TTS 需要各种各样的模型、词典和其他文件，并且这些文件因语言而异。

sentences.en: You can use the download module to pull down exactly what you need for a particular language, and optionally a voice:
sentences.zh: 您可以使用下载模块来准确下载特定语言和可选语音所需的内容：

blocks.html_en: The downloaded models are placed in child folders underneath the root folder, and by default the text to speech module expects the files to have the same relative paths so it can find them automatically given only the parent's path. If you do need to move them to different locations, you can supply new paths for each file using the options argument to TextToSpeech's constructor, with the usual relative path as the key, and the actual path to the file as the key.

sentences.en: The downloaded models are placed in child folders underneath the root folder, and by default the text to speech module expects the files to have the same relative paths so it can find them automatically given only the parent's path.
sentences.zh: 下载的模型放置在根文件夹下的子文件夹中，默认情况下，文本转语音模块期望文件具有相同的相对路径，以便仅在给定父路径的情况下即可自动找到它们。

sentences.en: If you do need to move them to different locations, you can supply new paths for each file using the options argument to TextToSpeech's constructor, with the usual relative path as the key, and the actual path to the file as the key.
sentences.zh: 如果您确实需要将它们移动到不同的位置，则可以使用 TextToSpeech 构造函数的 options 参数为每​​个文件提供新路径，并以通常的相对路径为键，以文件的实际路径为键。

blocks.html_en: If you have an application that may be stored in an arbitrary location after installation, you can also pass in a tts_root value as an option to set the path to the actual root folder of the TTS data at runtime.

sentences.en: If you have an application that may be stored in an arbitrary location after installation, you can also pass in a tts_root value as an option to set the path to the actual root folder of the TTS data at runtime.
sentences.zh: 如果您的应用程序在安装后可能存储在任意位置，您还可以传入 tts_root 值作为选项，以在运行时设置 TTS 数据的实际根文件夹的路径。

blocks.html_en: On-Device Auto-Download (iOS / macOS / Android)

sentences.en: On-Device Auto-Download (iOS / macOS / Android)
sentences.zh: 设备上自动下载（iOS / macOS / Android）

blocks.html_en: The Python download module is the right tool for build-time and desktop workflows, but mobile apps often want to fetch models on first run instead of shipping every model inside the app bundle. Both the Swift (iOS/macOS) and Android bindings include an opt-in downloader for this.

sentences.en: The Python download module is the right tool for build-time and desktop workflows, but mobile apps often want to fetch models on first run instead of shipping every model inside the app bundle.
sentences.zh: Python 下载模块是构建时和桌面工作流程的正确工具，但移动应用程序通常希望在首次运行时获取模型，而不是传送应用程序包内的每个模型。

sentences.en: Both the Swift (iOS/macOS) and Android bindings include an opt-in downloader for this.
sentences.zh: Swift (iOS/macOS) 和 Android 绑定都包含一个选择加入的下载器。

blocks.html_en: This is strictly opt-in: apps that bundle their models and load them with the usual from files / from assets / from memory paths behave exactly as before and never touch the network. The downloader only resolves the file list from the native dependency catalog (the same catalog the Python module uses), so you never hardcode filenames, and it writes each file atomically (through a .part file), resumes interrupted transfers with HTTP Range, checks free space before large writes, and reports per-file progress.

sentences.en: This is strictly opt-in: apps that bundle their models and load them with the usual from files / from assets / from memory paths behave exactly as before and never touch the network.
sentences.zh: 这是严格选择加入的：捆绑模型并使用通常的文件/资产/内存路径加载模型的应用程序的行为与以前完全相同，并且从不接触网络。

sentences.en: The downloader only resolves the file list from the native dependency catalog (the same catalog the Python module uses), so you never hardcode filenames, and it writes each file atomically (through a .part file), resumes interrupted transfers with HTTP Range, checks free space before large writes, and reports per-file progress.
sentences.zh: 下载器仅解析本机依赖项目录（与 Python 模块使用的目录相同）中的文件列表，因此您无需硬编码文件名，它会自动写入每个文件（通过 .part 文件），使用 HTTP Range 恢复中断的传输，在大量写入之前检查可用空间，并报告每个文件的进度。

blocks.html_en: Swift (iOS 15+ / macOS 12+)

sentences.en: Swift (iOS 15+ / macOS 12+)
sentences.zh: 斯威夫特（iOS 15+ / macOS 12+）

blocks.html_en: AssetDownloader.ensureModelPresent downloads whatever is missing under a directory you choose and returns that directory, ready to hand to Transcriber, IntentRecognizer, or TextToSpeech. Call it off the main actor (it is async).

sentences.en: AssetDownloader.ensureModelPresent downloads whatever is missing under a directory you choose and returns that directory, ready to hand to Transcriber, IntentRecognizer, or TextToSpeech.
sentences.zh: AssetDownloader.ensureModelPresent 下载您选择的目录下缺少的任何内容并返回该目录，准备好交给 Transcriber、IntentRecognizer 或 TextToSpeech。

sentences.en: Call it off the main actor (it is async).
sentences.zh: 将其从主角中取消（它是异步的）。

blocks.html_en: isModelPresent(root:spec:) is a cheap synchronous check you can use to skip the download UI entirely when everything is already on disk. Download failures throw AssetDownloadError (HTTP status, insufficient space, cancellation, …) so you can distinguish "couldn't fetch" from a later load failure. No extra Info.plist entries or permissions are required for HTTPS downloads.

sentences.en: isModelPresent(root:spec:) is a cheap synchronous check you can use to skip the download UI entirely when everything is already on disk.
sentences.zh: isModelPresent(root:spec:) 是一种廉价的同步检查，当所有内容都已在磁盘上时，您可以使用它完全跳过下载 UI。

sentences.en: Download failures throw AssetDownloadError (HTTP status, insufficient space, cancellation, …)
sentences.zh: 下载失败会抛出 AssetDownloadError（HTTP 状态、空间不足、取消等）

sentences.en: so you can distinguish "couldn't fetch" from a later load failure.
sentences.zh: 这样您就可以将“无法获取”与后来的加载失败区分开来。

sentences.en: No extra Info.plist entries or permissions are required for HTTPS downloads.
sentences.zh: HTTPS 下载不需要额外的 Info.plist 条目或权限。

blocks.html_en: Android

sentences.en: Android
sentences.zh: 安卓

blocks.html_en: The Android AssetDownloader mirrors the Swift API and performs blocking network I/O, so run it off the main thread. For reliable background downloads that survive process death, honor a network constraint, and retry with backoff, use MoonshineDownloadWorker (WorkManager).

sentences.en: The Android AssetDownloader mirrors the Swift API and performs blocking network I/O, so run it off the main thread.
sentences.zh: Android AssetDownloader 镜像 Swift API 并执行阻塞网络 I/O，因此从主线程运行它。

sentences.en: For reliable background downloads that survive process death, honor a network constraint, and retry with backoff, use MoonshineDownloadWorker (WorkManager).
sentences.zh: 要获得可靠的后台下载，在进程死亡后仍能存活、遵守网络约束并通过回退重试，请使用 MoonshineDownloadWorker (WorkManager)。

blocks.html_en: ModelSpec also has tts(language, voice), intent(modelName, variant), and g2p(language) factories, matching the Swift specs. The library manifest declares the INTERNET and ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE permissions, which merge into your app automatically; observe MoonshineDownloadWorker progress via WorkInfo.getProgress() using the worker's PROGRESS_* data keys. Using the downloader pulls in OkHttp and WorkManager transitively; apps that bundle their models still ship these but never invoke the network path.

sentences.en: ModelSpec also has tts(language, voice), intent(modelName, variant), and g2p(language) factories, matching the Swift specs.
sentences.zh: ModelSpec 还具有 tts（语言，语音）、intent（modelName，变体）和 g2p（语言）工厂，与 Swift 规范相匹配。

sentences.en: The library manifest declares the INTERNET and ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE permissions, which merge into your app automatically; observe MoonshineDownloadWorker progress via WorkInfo.getProgress() using the worker's PROGRESS_* data keys.
sentences.zh: 库清单声明了 INTERNET 和 ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE 权限，这些权限会自动合并到您的应用程序中；使用工作人员的 PROGRESS_* 数据键通过 WorkInfo.getProgress() 观察 MoonshineDownloadWorker 进度。

sentences.en: Using the downloader pulls in OkHttp and WorkManager transitively; apps that bundle their models still ship these but never invoke the network path.
sentences.zh: 使用下载器传递性地拉入 OkHttp 和 WorkManager；捆绑模型的应用程序仍然会提供这些模型，但从不调用网络路径。

blocks.html_en: Where files go

sentences.en: Where files go
sentences.zh: 文件去向

blocks.html_en: You pick the destination directory, so choose one appropriate for your platform's cache/storage policy (for example URL.cachesDirectory on Apple platforms, or context.getFilesDir() / context.getCacheDir() on Android). Files are laid out under that directory exactly as the loaders expect: STT and intent files use their bare filenames, while TTS/G2P assets keep their canonical relative paths (e.g. en_us/dict.tsv) so the engine can find them from the root alone.

sentences.en: You pick the destination directory, so choose one appropriate for your platform's cache/storage policy (for example URL.cachesDirectory on Apple platforms, or context.getFilesDir() / context.getCacheDir() on Android).
sentences.zh: 您可以选择目标目录，因此选择适合您平台的缓存/存储策略的目录（例如 Apple 平台上的 URL.cachesDirectory，或 Android 上的 context.getFilesDir() / context.getCacheDir()）。

sentences.en: Files are laid out under that directory exactly as the loaders expect: STT and intent files use their bare filenames, while TTS/G2P assets keep their canonical relative paths (e.g. en_us/dict.tsv) so the engine can find them from the root alone.
sentences.zh: 文件完全按照加载程序的预期放置在该目录下：STT 和意图文件使用其裸文件名，而 TTS/G2P 资产保留其规范的相对路径（例如 en_us/dict.tsv），以便引擎可以仅从根目录找到它们。

sentences.en: Testing the download path
sentences.zh: 测试下载路径

blocks.html_en: Both frameworks ship download tests that hit the real CDN, download a model into an empty directory, and then load and run the matching engine. They pull tens to hundreds of MB, so they are opt-in and skip on a normal test run:

sentences.en: Both frameworks ship download tests that hit the real CDN, download a model into an empty directory, and then load and run the matching engine.
sentences.zh: 这两个框架都提供了到达真实 CDN 的下载测试，将模型下载到空目录中，然后加载并运行匹配引擎。

sentences.en: They pull tens to hundreds of MB, so they are opt-in and skip on a normal test run:
sentences.zh: 他们提取了数十到数百 MB，因此他们选择加入并跳过正常的测试运行：

blocks.html_en: • Swift (AssetDownloaderNetworkTests): MOONSHINE_DOWNLOAD_TESTS=1 swift test --filter AssetDownloaderNetworkTests.

sentences.en: Swift (AssetDownloaderNetworkTests): MOONSHINE_DOWNLOAD_TESTS=1 swift test --filter AssetDownloaderNetworkTests.
sentences.zh: Swift (AssetDownloaderNetworkTests): MOONSHINE_DOWNLOAD_TESTS=1 快速测试 --filter AssetDownloaderNetworkTests。

blocks.html_en: • Android (AssetDownloaderTest): ./gradlew connectedAndroidTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=ai.moonshine.voice.AssetDownloaderTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.moonshineDownloadTests=1 (needs a connected device/emulator).

sentences.en: Android (AssetDownloaderTest): ./gradlew connectedAndroidTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=ai.moonshine.voice.AssetDownloaderTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.moonshineDownloadTests=1 (needs a connected device/emulator).
sentences.zh: Android (AssetDownloaderTest): ./gradlewconnectedAndroidTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=ai.moonshine.voice.AssetDownloaderTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.moonshineDownloadTests=1（需要连接的设备/模拟器）。

blocks.html_en: scripts/test-model-downloads.sh runs the native catalog samples and then drives both of these automatically (skipping a platform when its toolchain or a device is unavailable). The mocked, offline AssetDownloaderTests still run as part of the default swift test and cover manifest parsing, resume, and error handling without a network.

sentences.en: scripts/test-model-downloads.sh runs the native catalog samples and then drives both of these automatically (skipping a platform when its toolchain or a device is unavailable).
sentences.zh: script/test-model-downloads.sh 运行本机目录示例，然后自动驱动这两个示例（当工具链或设备不可用时跳过平台）。

sentences.en: The mocked, offline AssetDownloaderTests still run as part of the default swift test and cover manifest parsing, resume, and error handling without a network.
sentences.zh: 模拟的离线 AssetDownloaderTests 仍然作为默认 swift 测试的一部分运行，并涵盖清单解析、恢复和没有网络的错误处理。

blocks.html_en: Benchmarks

sentences.en: Benchmarks
sentences.zh: 基准测试

blocks.html_en: The core library includes a benchmarking tool that simulates processing live audio by loading a .wav audio file and feeding it in chunks to the model. To run it:

sentences.en: The core library includes a benchmarking tool that simulates processing live audio by loading a .wav audio file and feeding it in chunks to the model.
sentences.zh: 核心库包括一个基准测试工具，该工具通过加载 .wav 音频文件并将其分块馈送到模型来模拟处理实时音频。

sentences.en: To run it:
sentences.zh: 运行它：

blocks.html_en: This will report the absolute time taken to process the audio, what percentage of the audio file's duration that is, and the average latency for a response.

sentences.en: This will report the absolute time taken to process the audio, what percentage of the audio file's duration that is, and the average latency for a response.
sentences.zh: 这将报告处理音频所花费的绝对时间、音频文件持续时间的百分比以及响应的平均延迟。

blocks.html_en: The percentage is helpful because it approximates how much of a compute load the model will be on your hardware. For example, if it shows 20% then that means the speech processing will take a fifth of the compute time when running in your application, leaving 80% for the rest of your code.

sentences.en: The percentage is helpful because it approximates how much of a compute load the model will be on your hardware.
sentences.zh: 该百分比很有用，因为它可以估算模型在硬件上的计算负载量。

sentences.en: For example, if it shows 20% then that means the speech processing will take a fifth of the compute time when running in your application, leaving 80% for the rest of your code.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果它显示 20%，则意味着在应用程序中运行时，语音处理将花费五分之一的计算时间，为其余代码留下 80%。

blocks.html_en: The latency metric needs a bit of explanation. What most applications care about is how soon they are notified about a phrase after the user has finished talking, since this determines how fast the product can respond. As with any user interface, the time between speech ending and the app doing something determines how responsive the voice interface feels, with a goal of keeping it below 200ms. The latency figure logged here is the average time between when the library determines the user has stopped talking and the delivery of the final transcript of that phrase to the client. This is where streaming models have the most impact, since they do a lot of their work upfront, while speech is still happening, so they can usually finish very quickly.

sentences.en: The latency metric needs a bit of explanation.
sentences.zh: 延迟指标需要一些解释。

sentences.en: What most applications care about is how soon they are notified about a phrase after the user has finished talking, since this determines how fast the product can respond.
sentences.zh: 大多数应用程序关心的是用户结束讲话后多久收到有关短语的通知，因为这决定了产品响应的速度。

sentences.en: As with any user interface, the time between speech ending and the app doing something determines how responsive the voice interface feels, with a goal of keeping it below 200ms.
sentences.zh: 与任何用户界面一样，语音结束和应用执行某些操作之间的时间决定了语音界面的响应速度，目标是使其保持在 200 毫秒以下。

sentences.en: The latency figure logged here is the average time between when the library determines the user has stopped talking and the delivery of the final transcript of that phrase to the client.
sentences.zh: 此处记录的延迟数字是图书馆确定用户已停止说话与将该短语的最终转录发送给客户之间的平均时间。

sentences.en: This is where streaming models have the most impact, since they do a lot of their work upfront, while speech is still happening, so they can usually finish very quickly.
sentences.zh: 这是流媒体模型影响最大的地方，因为它们预先做了很多工作，而语音仍在发生，所以它们通常可以很快完成。

blocks.html_en: By default the benchmark binary uses the Tiny English model that's embedded in the framework, but you can pass in the --model-path and --model-arch parameters to choose one that you've downloaded.

sentences.en: By default the benchmark binary uses the Tiny English model that's embedded in the framework, but you can pass in the --model-path and --model-arch parameters to choose one that you've downloaded.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，基准二进制文件使用框架中嵌入的 Tiny English 模型，但您可以传入 --model-path 和 --model-arch 参数来选择您已下载的模型。

blocks.html_en: You can also choose how often the transcript should be updated using the --transcription-interval argument. This defaults to 0.5 seconds, but the right value will depend on how fast your application needs updates. Longer intervals reduce the compute required a bit, at the cost of slower updates.

sentences.en: You can also choose how often the transcript should be updated using the --transcription-interval argument.
sentences.zh: 您还可以使用 --transcription-interval 参数选择更新记录的频率。

sentences.en: This defaults to 0.5 seconds, but the right value will depend on how fast your application needs updates.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 0.5 秒，但正确的值取决于应用程序需要更新的速度。

sentences.en: Longer intervals reduce the compute required a bit, at the cost of slower updates.
sentences.zh: 较长的间隔会稍微减少所需的计算量，但代价是更新速度较慢。

blocks.html_en: Whisper Comparisons

sentences.en: Whisper Comparisons
sentences.zh: 耳语比较

blocks.html_en: For platforms that support Python, you can run the scripts/run-benchmarks.py script which will evaluate similar metrics, with the advantage that it can also download the models so you don't need to worry about path handling.

sentences.en: For platforms that support Python, you can run the scripts/run-benchmarks.py script which will evaluate similar metrics, with the advantage that it can also download the models so you don't need to worry about path handling.
sentences.zh: 对于支持Python的平台，您可以运行scripts/run-benchmarks.py脚本，该脚本将评估类似的指标，其优点是它还可以下载模型，因此您无需担心路径处理。

blocks.html_en: It also evaluates equivalent Whisper models. This is a pretty opinionated benchmark that looks at the latency and total compute cost of the two families of models in a situation that is representative of many common real-time voice applications' requirements:

sentences.en: It also evaluates equivalent Whisper models.
sentences.zh: 它还评估等效的 Whisper 模型。

sentences.en: This is a pretty opinionated benchmark that looks at the latency and total compute cost of the two families of models in a situation that is representative of many common real-time voice applications' requirements:
sentences.zh: 这是一个相当固执己见的基准，它着眼于代表许多常见实时语音应用程序需求的情况下两个模型系列的延迟和总计算成本：

blocks.html_en: • Speech needs to be responded to as quickly as possible once a user completes a phrase.

sentences.en: Speech needs to be responded to as quickly as possible once a user completes a phrase.
sentences.zh: 一旦用户完成一个短语，就需要尽快响应语音。

blocks.html_en: • The phrases are of durations between a range of one to ten seconds.

sentences.en: The phrases are of durations between a range of one to ten seconds.
sentences.zh: 这些短语的持续时间在一到十秒的范围内。

blocks.html_en: These are very different requirements from bulk offline processing scenarios, where the overall throughput of the system is more important, and so the latency on a single segment of speech is less important than the overall throughput of the system. This allows optimizations like batch processing.

sentences.en: These are very different requirements from bulk offline processing scenarios, where the overall throughput of the system is more important, and so the latency on a single segment of speech is less important than the overall throughput of the system.
sentences.zh: 这些与批量离线处理场景的要求非常不同，在批量离线处理场景中，系统的整体吞吐量更为重要，因此单个语音片段的延迟不如系统的整体吞吐量重要。

sentences.en: This allows optimizations like batch processing.
sentences.zh: 这允许像批处理这样的优化。

blocks.html_en: We are not claiming that Whisper is not a great model for offline processing, but we do want to highlight the advantages we that Moonshine offers for live speech applications with real-time latency requirements.

sentences.en: We are not claiming that Whisper is not a great model for offline processing, but we do want to highlight the advantages we that Moonshine offers for live speech applications with real-time latency requirements.
sentences.zh: 我们并不是说 Whisper 不是一个很好的离线处理模型，但我们确实想强调 Moonshine 为具有实时延迟要求的实时语音应用程序提供的优势。

blocks.html_en: The experimental setup is as follows:

sentences.en: The experimental setup is as follows:
sentences.zh: 实验设置如下：

blocks.html_en: • We use the two_cities.wav audio file as a test case, since it has a mix of short and long phrases. You can vary this by passing in your own audio file with the --wav_path argument.

sentences.en: We use the two_cities.wav audio file as a test case, since it has a mix of short and long phrases.
sentences.zh: 我们使用two_cities.wav 音频文件作为测试用例，因为它混合了短短语和长短语。

sentences.en: You can vary this by passing in your own audio file with the --wav_path argument.
sentences.zh: 您可以通过使用 --wav_path 参数传入您自己的音频文件来改变这一点。

blocks.html_en: • We use the Moonshine Tiny, Base, Tiny Streaming, Small Streaming, and Medium Streaming models.

sentences.en: We use the Moonshine Tiny, Base, Tiny Streaming, Small Streaming, and Medium Streaming models.
sentences.zh: 我们使用 Moonshine Tiny、Base、Tiny Streaming、Small Streaming 和 Medium Streaming 模型。

blocks.html_en: • We compare these to the Whisper Tiny, Base, Small, and Large v3 models. Since the Moonshine Medium Streaming model achieves lower WER than Whisper Large v3 we compare those two, otherwise we compare each with their namesake.

sentences.en: We compare these to the Whisper Tiny, Base, Small, and Large v3 models.
sentences.zh: 我们将它们与 Whisper Tiny、Base、Small 和 Large v3 型号进行比较。

sentences.en: Since the Moonshine Medium Streaming model achieves lower WER than Whisper Large v3 we compare those two, otherwise we compare each with their namesake.
sentences.zh: 由于 Moonshine Medium Streaming 模型的 WER 低于 Whisper Large v3，因此我们比较这两个模型，否则我们将每个模型与它们的同名模型进行比较。

blocks.html_en: • We use the Moonshine VAD segmenter to split the audio into phrases, and feed each phrase to Whisper for transcription.

sentences.en: We use the Moonshine VAD segmenter to split the audio into phrases, and feed each phrase to Whisper for transcription.
sentences.zh: 我们使用 Moonshine VAD 分段器将音频分割成短语，并将每个短语输入 Whisper 进行转录。

blocks.html_en: • Response latency for both models is measured as the time between a phrase being identified as complete by the VAD segmenter and the transcribed text being returned. For Whisper this means the full transcription time, but since the Moonshine models are streaming we can do a lot of the work while speech is still happening, so the latency is much lower.

sentences.en: Response latency for both models is measured as the time between a phrase being identified as complete by the VAD segmenter and the transcribed text being returned.
sentences.zh: 两种模型的响应延迟均以 VAD 分段器识别为完整的短语与返回转录文本之间的时间来衡量。

sentences.en: For Whisper this means the full transcription time, but since the Moonshine models are streaming we can do a lot of the work while speech is still happening, so the latency is much lower.
sentences.zh: 对于 Whisper 来说，这意味着完整的转录时间，但由于 Moonshine 模型是流式传输的，我们可以在语音仍在发生时完成大量工作，因此延迟要低得多。

blocks.html_en: • We measure the total compute cost of the models by totalling the duration of the audio processing times for each model, and then expressing that as a percentage of the total audio duration. This is the inverse of the commonly used real-time factor (RTF) metric, but it reflects the compute load required for a real-time application.

sentences.en: We measure the total compute cost of the models by totalling the duration of the audio processing times for each model, and then expressing that as a percentage of the total audio duration.
sentences.zh: 我们通过合计每个模型的音频处理时间的持续时间，然后将其表示为总音频持续时间的百分比来衡量模型的总计算成本。

sentences.en: This is the inverse of the commonly used real-time factor (RTF) metric, but it reflects the compute load required for a real-time application.
sentences.zh: 这是常用的实时因子 (RTF) 指标的倒数，但它反映了实时应用程序所需的计算负载。

blocks.html_en: • We're using faster-whisper for Whisper, since that seems to provide the best cross-platform performance. We're also sticking with the CPU, since most applications can't rely on GPU or NPU acceleration being present on all the platforms they target. We know there are a lot of great GPU/NPU-accelerated Whisper implementations out there, but these aren't portable enough to be useful for the applications we care about.

sentences.en: We're using faster-whisper for Whisper, since that seems to provide the best cross-platform performance.
sentences.zh: 我们对 Whisper 使用 fast-whisper，因为这似乎可以提供最佳的跨平台性能。

sentences.en: We're also sticking with the CPU, since most applications can't rely on GPU or NPU acceleration being present on all the platforms they target.
sentences.zh: 我们还坚持使用 CPU，因为大多数应用程序不能依赖其目标平台上存在的 GPU 或 NPU 加速。

sentences.en: We know there are a lot of great GPU/NPU-accelerated Whisper implementations out there, but these aren't portable enough to be useful for the applications we care about.
sentences.zh: 我们知道有很多出色的 GPU/NPU 加速 Whisper 实现，但这些实现的便携性不足以对我们关心的应用程序有用。

blocks.html_en: Moonshine Voice is based on a family of speech to text models created by the team at Moonshine AI. If you want to download models to use with the framework, you can use the Python package to access them. This section contains more information about the history and characteristics of the models we offer.

sentences.en: Moonshine Voice is based on a family of speech to text models created by the team at Moonshine AI.

sentences.en: This section contains more information about the history and characteristics of the models we offer.

blocks.html_en: These research papers are a good resource for understanding the architectures and performance strategies behind the models:

sentences.en: These research papers are a good resource for understanding the architectures and performance strategies behind the models:
sentences.zh: 这些研究论文是了解模型背后的架构和性能策略的良好资源：

blocks.html_en: • Moonshine: Speech Recognition for Live Transcription and Voice Commands: Describes the first-generation model architecture, which enabled flexible-duration input windows, improving on Whisper's fixed 30 second requirement.

sentences.en: Moonshine: Speech Recognition for Live Transcription and Voice Commands: Describes the first-generation model architecture, which enabled flexible-duration input windows, improving on Whisper's fixed 30 second requirement.
sentences.zh: Moonshine：实时转录和语音命令的语音识别：描述第一代模型架构，该架构支持灵活持续时间的输入窗口，改进了 Whisper 的固定 30 秒要求。

blocks.html_en: • Flavors of Moonshine: Tiny Specialized ASR Models for Edge Devices: How we improved accuracy for non-English languages by training mono-lingual models.

sentences.en: Flavors of Moonshine: Tiny Specialized ASR Models for Edge Devices: How we improved accuracy for non-English languages by training mono-lingual models.
sentences.zh: Moonshine 的风味：用于边缘设备的小型专用 ASR 模型：我们如何通过训练单语言模型来提高非英语语言的准确性。

blocks.html_en: • Moonshine v2: Ergodic Streaming Encoder ASR for Latency-Critical Speech Applications: Introduces our approach to streaming, and the advantages it offers for live voice applications.

sentences.en: Moonshine v2: Ergodic Streaming Encoder ASR for Latency-Critical Speech Applications: Introduces our approach to streaming, and the advantages it offers for live voice applications.
sentences.zh: Moonshine v2：适用于延迟关键语音应用的遍历流编码器 ASR：介绍我们的流方法及其为实时语音应用提供的优势。

blocks.html_en: The English evaluations were done using the HuggingFace OpenASR Leaderboard datasets and methodology. The other languages were evaluated using the FLEURS dataset and the scripts/eval-model-accuracy script, with the character or word error rate chosen per language.

blocks.html_en: Note that the English WER figures above are the Open ASR Leaderboard average across eight datasets, measured on the floating-point reference models. The quantized models this library actually ships score a little higher, especially at the Tiny size. See Accuracy (Word Error Rate) below for a float-vs-quantized comparison and instructions on reproducing the numbers.

blocks.html_en: One common issue to watch out for if you're using models that don't use the Latin alphabet (so any languages except English and Spanish) is that you'll need to set the max_tokens_per_second option to 13.0 when you create the transcriber. This is because the most common pattern for hallucinations is endlessly repeating the last few words, and our heuristic to detect this is to check if there's an unusually high number of tokens for the duration of a segment. Unfortunately the base number of tokens per second for non-Latin languages is much higher than for English, thanks to how we're tokenizing, so you have to manually set the threshold higher to avoid cutting off valid outputs.

sentences.en: One common issue to watch out for if you're using models that don't use the Latin alphabet (so any languages except English and Spanish) is that you'll need to set the max_tokens_per_second option to 13.0 when you create the transcriber.
sentences.zh: 如果您使用的模型不使用拉丁字母（因此除英语和西班牙语之外的任何语言），需要注意的一个常见问题是，您在创建转录器时需要将 max_tokens_per_second 选项设置为 13.0。

sentences.en: This is because the most common pattern for hallucinations is endlessly repeating the last few words, and our heuristic to detect this is to check if there's an unusually high number of tokens for the duration of a segment.
sentences.zh: 这是因为最常见的幻觉模式是无休止地重复最后几个单词，而我们检测这一点的启发式方法是检查一段时间内是否存在异常多的标记。

sentences.en: Unfortunately the base number of tokens per second for non-Latin languages is much higher than for English, thanks to how we're tokenizing, so you have to manually set the threshold higher to avoid cutting off valid outputs.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，由于我们的标记化方式，非拉丁语言每秒的标记基数远高于英语，因此您必须手动将阈值设置得更高，以避免切断有效输出。

blocks.html_en: Accuracy (Word Error Rate)

sentences.en: Accuracy (Word Error Rate)
sentences.zh: 准确度（字错误率）

blocks.html_en: Beyond knowing which models are available, you'll often want to understand how accurate they are and how to reproduce the numbers yourself. The scripts/eval-librispeech.py script measures Word Error Rate (WER) on the LibriSpeech test-clean set using the same dataset and Open ASR Leaderboard methodology (corpus-level WER with the Whisper English text normalizer) reported in our Moonshine v2 paper.

sentences.en: Beyond knowing which models are available, you'll often want to understand how accurate they are and how to reproduce the numbers yourself.
sentences.zh: 除了了解哪些模型可用之外，您通常还想了解它们的准确性以及如何自己重现这些数字。

sentences.en: The scripts/eval-librispeech.py script measures Word Error Rate (WER) on the LibriSpeech test-clean set using the same dataset and Open ASR Leaderboard methodology (corpus-level WER with the Whisper English text normalizer) reported in our Moonshine v2 paper.
sentences.zh: script/eval-librispeech.py​​ 脚本使用我们的 Moonshine v2 论文中报告的相同数据集和 Open ASR Leaderboard 方法（使用 Whisper English 文本标准化器的语料库级别 WER）测量 LibriSpeech 测试集上的单词错误率 (WER)。

blocks.html_en: There's an important subtlety here that can be confusing: the WER numbers in the paper were measured with the floating-point models running in the Hugging Face Transformers library, not the quantized models this framework ships. As the paper notes in section 4.1.2, we use the Transformers implementation to measure accuracy and our own C++/ONNX library to measure latency. The models you download here are 8-bit quantized .ort files chosen for on-device speed and size, so they trade a little accuracy for a lot of portability.

sentences.en: There's an important subtlety here that can be confusing: the WER numbers in the paper were measured with the floating-point models running in the Hugging Face Transformers library, not the quantized models this framework ships.
sentences.zh: 这里有一个可能令人困惑的重要微妙之处：论文中的 WER 数字是使用 Hugging Face Transformers 库中运行的浮点模型测量的，而不是该框架附带的量化模型。

sentences.en: As the paper notes in section 4.1.2, we use the Transformers implementation to measure accuracy and our own C++/ONNX library to measure latency.
sentences.zh: 正如论文第 4.1.2 节所述，我们使用 Transformers 实现来测量准确性，并使用我们自己的 C++/ONNX 库来测量延迟。

sentences.en: The models you download here are 8-bit quantized .ort files chosen for on-device speed and size, so they trade a little accuracy for a lot of portability.
sentences.zh: 您在此处下载的模型是根据设备上的速度和大小选择的 8 位量化 .ort 文件，因此它们牺牲了一点精度来换取大量的可移植性。

blocks.html_en: The table below shows LibriSpeech test-clean WER for all three streaming models, comparing the paper's floating-point reference against the quantized models this library ships. All numbers use whole-utterance (non-streaming) transcription with the VAD disabled, so they're a like-for-like comparison of raw model accuracy.

sentences.en: The table below shows LibriSpeech test-clean WER for all three streaming models, comparing the paper's floating-point reference against the quantized models this library ships.
sentences.zh: 下表显示了所有三种流模型的 LibriSpeech 测试干净 WER，将本文的浮点参考与该库提供的量化模型进行了比较。

sentences.en: All numbers use whole-utterance (non-streaming) transcription with the VAD disabled, so they're a like-for-like comparison of raw model accuracy.
sentences.zh: 所有数字均使用禁用 VAD 的完整话语（非流式）转录，因此它们是原始模型准确性的同类比较。

sentences.zh: 评估脚本会为您下载数据集和模型。

sentences.en: Install the dependencies and run it:
sentences.zh: 安装依赖项并运行：

blocks.html_en: To reproduce the paper's floating-point reference numbers you also need a recent version of Transformers (the streaming models were added in Transformers 5.x) and PyTorch, then pass --backend hf:

sentences.en: To reproduce the paper's floating-point reference numbers you also need a recent version of Transformers (the streaming models were added in Transformers 5.x) and PyTorch, then pass --backend hf:
sentences.zh: 要重现论文的浮点参考数字，您还需要最新版本的 Transformers（流模型已在 Transformers 5.x 中添加）和 PyTorch，然后传递 --backend hf：

blocks.html_en: The script disables the VAD by default (vad_threshold=0 plus a very large vad_max_segment_duration) because the LibriSpeech clips are already single utterances, so any VAD segmentation only adds errors. Pass --enable-vad to see the effect of the segmenter, or --backend moonshine_c_streaming to measure the chunked, real-time streaming path instead of whole-utterance transcription. Use --limit N for a quick smoke test on the first N clips.

sentences.en: The script disables the VAD by default (vad_threshold=0 plus a very large vad_max_segment_duration) because the LibriSpeech clips are already single utterances, so any VAD segmentation only adds errors.
sentences.zh: 该脚本默认禁用 VAD（vad_threshold=0 加上非常大的 vad_max_segment_duration），因为 LibriSpeech 剪辑已经是单个话语，因此任何 VAD 分段只会增加错误。

sentences.en: Pass --enable-vad to see the effect of the segmenter, or --backend moonshine_c_streaming to measure the chunked, real-time streaming path instead of whole-utterance transcription.
sentences.zh: 通过 --enable-vad 来查看分段器的效果，或者通过 --backend Moonshine_c_streaming 来测量分块的实时流路径而不是整个话语转录。

sentences.en: Use --limit N for a quick smoke test on the first N clips.
sentences.zh: 使用 --limit N 对前 N 个剪辑进行快速冒烟测试。

blocks.html_en: Takeaways

sentences.en: Takeaways
sentences.zh: 要点

blocks.html_en: • Quantization is the main reason the shipped models don't hit the paper's numbers, and its impact shrinks quickly with model size. The 8-bit penalty is about +3.0% WER on Tiny (which has the least redundancy to spare), but only +0.5% on Small and +0.2% on Medium. If you need Tiny to be as accurate as possible, run the floating-point checkpoint from Hugging Face in Transformers; Small and Medium are within a few tenths of a percent as shipped.

sentences.en: Quantization is the main reason the shipped models don't hit the paper's numbers, and its impact shrinks quickly with model size.
sentences.zh: 量化是交付的模型未达到论文数字的主要原因，并且其影响随着模型尺寸的增加而迅速缩小。

sentences.en: The 8-bit penalty is about +3.0% WER on Tiny (which has the least redundancy to spare), but only +0.5% on Small and +0.2% on Medium.
sentences.zh: Tiny 上的 8 位损失约为 +3.0%（具有最少的冗余），但 Small 上仅 +0.5%，Medium 上仅 +0.2%。

sentences.en: If you need Tiny to be as accurate as possible, run the floating-point checkpoint from Hugging Face in Transformers; Small and Medium are within a few tenths of a percent as shipped.
sentences.zh: 如果您需要 Tiny 尽可能准确，请运行 Transformers 中 Hugging Face 中的浮点检查点；小型和中型尺寸与发货时相差不到百分之几。

blocks.html_en: • Disable the VAD when evaluating pre-segmented data. On already-segmented clips like LibriSpeech, leaving the default VAD enabled adds roughly +1.5–2% WER on Tiny (mostly extra insertions at segment boundaries). The VAD is there to chop up continuous live audio, not clean single utterances.

sentences.en: Disable the VAD when evaluating pre-segmented data.
sentences.zh: 评估预分段数据时禁用 VAD。

sentences.en: On already-segmented clips like LibriSpeech, leaving the default VAD enabled adds roughly +1.5–2% WER on Tiny (mostly extra insertions at segment boundaries).
sentences.zh: 在 LibriSpeech 等已经分段的剪辑上，启用默认 VAD 会在 Tiny 上增加大约 +1.5–2% 的 WER（主要是在分段边界处的额外插入）。

sentences.en: The VAD is there to chop up continuous live audio, not clean single utterances.
sentences.zh: VAD 的作用是切碎连续的现场音频，而不是干净的单个话语。

blocks.html_en: • Watch which number you're comparing against. The per-model WER in the Available Models table is the Open ASR Leaderboard average across eight datasets (12.00% for Tiny Streaming, 7.84% for Small Streaming, and 6.65% for Medium Streaming), which is much higher than the LibriSpeech-clean-only numbers above. Make sure you're comparing the same benchmark.

sentences.en: Watch which number you're comparing against.
sentences.zh: 注意您要与哪个数字进行比较。

sentences.en: The per-model WER in the Available Models table is the Open ASR Leaderboard average across eight datasets (12.00% for Tiny Streaming, 7.84% for Small Streaming, and 6.65% for Medium Streaming), which is much higher than the LibriSpeech-clean-only numbers above.
sentences.zh: 可用模型表中每个模型的 WER 是八个数据集的开放 ASR 排行榜平均值（Tiny Streaming 为 12.00%，Small Streaming 为 7.84%，Medium Streaming 为 6.65%），这比上面仅 LibriSpeech-clean 的数字要高得多。

sentences.en: Make sure you're comparing the same benchmark.
sentences.zh: 确保您比较的是相同的基准。

blocks.html_en: Domain Customization

sentences.en: Domain Customization
sentences.zh: 域名定制

blocks.html_en: It's often useful to be able to calibrate a speech to text model towards certain words that you're expecting to hear in your application, whether it's technical terms, slang, or a particular dialect or accent. Moonshine AI offers full retraining using our internal dataset for customization as a commercial service and we do hope to support free lighter-weight approaches in the future. You can find a community project working on this at github.com/pierre-cheneau/finetune-moonshine-asr.

sentences.en: It's often useful to be able to calibrate a speech to text model towards certain words that you're expecting to hear in your application, whether it's technical terms, slang, or a particular dialect or accent.
sentences.zh: 能够针对您希望在应用程序中听到的某些单词（无论是技术术语、俚语还是特定的方言或口音）校准语音到文本模型通常很有用。

sentences.en: Moonshine AI offers full retraining using our internal dataset for customization as a commercial service and we do hope to support free lighter-weight approaches in the future.
sentences.zh: Moonshine AI 使用我们的内部数据集提供全面的再培训，以进行定制作为商业服务，我们希望将来支持免费的轻量级方法。

sentences.en: You can find a community project working on this at github.com/pierre-cheneau/finetune-moonshine-asr.
sentences.zh: 您可以在 github.com/pierre-cheneau/finetune-moonshine-asr 上找到一个致力于此的社区项目。

blocks.html_en: Quantization

sentences.en: Quantization
sentences.zh: 量化

blocks.html_en: We typically quantize our models to eight-bit weights across the board, and eight-bit calculations for heavy operations like MatMul. This is all post-training quantization, using a combination of OnnxRuntime's tools and my Onnx Shrink Ray utility. The only anomaly in the process is the treatment of the frontend, which uses convolution layers to generate features, which produces results similar to the more traditional MEL spectrogram preprocessing, but in a learned way with standard ML operations. The inputs to this initial stage correspond to 16-bit signed integers from the raw audio data (though they're encoded as floats) so we've found it necessary to leave the convolution operations in at least B16 float precision.

sentences.en: We typically quantize our models to eight-bit weights across the board, and eight-bit calculations for heavy operations like MatMul.
sentences.zh: 我们通常将模型全面量化为八位权重，并针对 MatMul 等繁重运算进行八位计算。

sentences.en: This is all post-training quantization, using a combination of OnnxRuntime's tools and my Onnx Shrink Ray utility.
sentences.zh: 这都是训练后量化，使用 OnnxRuntime 工具和我的 Onnx Shrink Ray 实用程序的组合。

sentences.en: The only anomaly in the process is the treatment of the frontend, which uses convolution layers to generate features, which produces results similar to the more traditional MEL spectrogram preprocessing, but in a learned way with standard ML operations.
sentences.zh: 该过程中唯一的异常是前端的处理，它使用卷积层生成特征，产生的结果类似于更传统的 MEL 频谱图预处理，但采用标准 ML 操作的学习方式。

sentences.en: The inputs to this initial stage correspond to 16-bit signed integers from the raw audio data (though they're encoded as floats) so we've found it necessary to leave the convolution operations in at least B16 float precision.
sentences.zh: 该初始阶段的输入对应于原始音频数据中的 16 位有符号整数（尽管它们被编码为浮点），因此我们发现有必要将卷积运算保留为至少 B16 浮点精度。

blocks.html_en: You can see the options we use for the conversions in scripts/quantize-streaming-model.sh.

sentences.en: You can see the options we use for the conversions in scripts/quantize-streaming-model.sh.
sentences.zh: 您可以在 script/quantize-streaming-model.sh 中看到我们用于转换的选项。

These are all floating-point checkpoints exported from our training pipeline

blocks.html_en: TranscriberLine

sentences.en: TranscriberLine
sentences.zh: 转录线

blocks.html_en: Represents a single "line" or speech segment in a transcript. It includes information about the timing, speaker, and text content of the utterance, as well as state such as whether the speech is ongoing or done. If you're building an application that involves transcription, this data structure has all of the information available about each line of speech. Be aware that each line can be updated multiple times with new text and other information as the user keeps speaking.

sentences.en: Represents a single "line" or speech segment in a transcript.
sentences.zh: 代表转录中的单个“行”或语音片段。

sentences.en: It includes information about the timing, speaker, and text content of the utterance, as well as state such as whether the speech is ongoing or done.
sentences.zh: 它包括有关讲话的时间、讲话者和文本内容的信息，以及讲话是否正在进行或已完成等状态。

sentences.en: If you're building an application that involves transcription, this data structure has all of the information available about each line of speech.
sentences.zh: 如果您正在构建涉及转录的应用程序，则此数据结构具有有关每行语音的所有可用信息。

sentences.en: Be aware that each line can be updated multiple times with new text and other information as the user keeps speaking.
sentences.zh: 请注意，当用户继续说话时，每行都可以使用新文本和其他信息进行多次更新。

blocks.html_en: • text: A string containing the UTF-8 encoded text that has been extracted from the audio of this segment.

sentences.en: text: A string containing the UTF-8 encoded text that has been extracted from the audio of this segment.
sentences.zh: text：包含从该片段的音频中提取的 UTF-8 编码文本的字符串。

blocks.html_en: • start_time: A float value representing the time in seconds since the start of the current session that the current utterance was first detected.

sentences.zh: start_time：一个浮点值，表示自当前会话开始到首次检测到当前话语的时间（以秒为单位）。

blocks.html_en: • line_id: An unsigned 64-bit integer that represents a line in a collision-resistant way, for use in storage and ensuring the application can keep track of lines as they change over time. See Transcription Event Flow for more details.

sentences.en: See Transcription Event Flow for more details.
sentences.zh: 有关更多详细信息，请参阅转录事件流。

blocks.html_en: • is_complete: A boolean that is false until the segment has been completed, and true for the remainder of the line's lifetime.

blocks.html_en: • is_updated: A boolean that's true if any information about the line has changed since the last time the transcript was updated. Since the transcript will be periodically updated internally by the library as you add audio chunks, you can't rely on polling this to detect changes. You should rely on the event/listener flow to catch modifications instead. This applies to all of the booleans below too.

blocks.html_en: • is_new: A boolean indicating whether the line has been added to the transcript by the last update call.

blocks.html_en: • has_text_changed: A boolean that's set if the contents of the line's text was modified by the last transcript update. If this is set, is_updated will always be set too, but if other properties of the line (for example the duration or the audio data) have changed but the text remains the same, then is_updated can be true while has_text_changed is false.

blocks.html_en: • have_speakers_changed: A boolean that's set if the line's speaker spans were revised by the last transcript update. Unlike the other change flags, this can be set for lines that are already complete, since the diarization algorithm keeps refining speaker assignments for recent audio. Only relevant when the identify_speakers option is enabled.

sentences.en: have_speakers_changed: A boolean that's set if the line's speaker spans were revised by the last transcript update.
sentences.zh: have_speakers_changed：如果该行的扬声器跨度被上次转录更新修改，则设置一个布尔值。

sentences.en: Unlike the other change flags, this can be set for lines that are already complete, since the diarization algorithm keeps refining speaker assignments for recent audio.
sentences.zh: 与其他更改标志不同，可以为已经完成的行设置此标志，因为二值化算法不断优化最近音频的扬声器分配。

sentences.en: Only relevant when the identify_speakers option is enabled.
sentences.zh: 仅当启用identify_speakers选项时相关。

blocks.html_en: have_speakers_changed: A boolean that's set if the line's speaker spans were revised by the last transcript update. Unlike the other change flags, this can be set for lines that are already complete, since the diarization algorithm keeps refining speaker assignments for recent audio. Only relevant when the identify_speakers option is enabled.

blocks.html_en:
• speaker_spans: An array of speaker spans describing who was talking during which parts of the line, ordered by start time and clipped to the line's time range. Empty unless the opt-in identify_speakers option is enabled (which also turns on word timestamps automatically, since they are needed to map spans onto the line text). Each span has:

    • start_time: A float giving the time offset in seconds from the start of the stream.
    • duration: A float giving the length of the span in seconds.
    • speaker_id: A unique-ish unsigned 64-bit integer that is stable for a given speaker within a stream, designed for storage or keeping track of speakers over time.
    • speaker_index: An integer that represents the order in which the speaker first appeared in the transcript, to make it easy to give speakers default names like "Speaker 1:", etc.
    • start_char: A UTF-8 byte offset into the line's text where this span begins (inclusive). Zero when unknown.
    • end_char: A UTF-8 byte offset into the line's text where this span ends (exclusive). Slice with text[start_char:end_char] in Python. Both zero when the span could not be aligned to words yet.

    Be aware that speaker spans are mutable: the diarization algorithm re-clusters the entire audio history on a cadence, so the spans of any line - including completed ones - can move, merge, split, or change speaker on any transcription call. Watch the have_speakers_changed flag (or the LineSpeakersChanged event) to catch revisions.

blocks.html_en: A Transcript contains a list of TranscriberLines, arranged in descending time order. The transcript is reset at every Transcriber.start() call, so if you need to retain information from it, you should make explicit copies. Most applications won't work with this structure, since all of the same information is available through event callbacks.

blocks.html_en: Classes

sentences.en: Classes
sentences.zh: 课程

blocks.html_en: Transcriber

sentences.en: Transcriber
sentences.zh: 转录员

blocks.html_en: Handles the speech to text pipeline.

sentences.en: Handles the speech to text pipeline.
sentences.zh: 处理语音到文本的管道。

blocks.html_en:
• __init__(): Loads and initializes the transcriber.

    • model_path: The path to the directory holding the component model files needed for the complete flow. Note that this is a path to the folder, not an individual file. You can download and get a path to a cached version of the standard models using the download_model() function.
    • model_arch: The architecture of the model to load, from the selection defined in ModelArch.
    • update_interval: By default the transcriber will periodically run text transcription as new audio data is fed, so that update events can be triggered. This value is how often the speech to text model should be run. You can set this to a large duration to suppress updates between a line starting and ending, but because the streaming models do a lot of their work before the final speech to text stage, this may not reduce overall latency by much.
    • options: These are flags that affect how the transcription process works inside the library, often enabling performance optimizations or debug logging. They are passed as a dictionary mapping strings to strings, even if the values are to be interpreted as numbers - for example {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}.
      • skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.
      • max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again. As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding. By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.
      • transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.
      • vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments. This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping. If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.
      • save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline. If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.
      • log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.
      • log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.
      • ort_providers: A comma-separated, ordered list of the ONNX Runtime execution providers the models should try to use, for example "CoreML,CPU" on macOS or "NNAPI,CPU" on Android. The names are case-insensitive and accept either the short form (CPU, CoreML, NNAPI) or the full ONNX Runtime name (CPUExecutionProvider, CoreMLExecutionProvider, NNAPIExecutionProvider). Providers are appended in the order given, and ONNX Runtime falls back to later entries for any operations an earlier provider can't handle, so it's good practice to always list CPU last as a catch-all. If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only. Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error. The alias ort_provider is also accepted.
      • coreml_cache_dir: A directory path where the CoreML execution provider caches its compiled models on macOS and iOS. Compiling a model for CoreML is expensive, so pointing this at a persistent, writable folder lets subsequent runs reuse the cached artifacts and start up much faster. This only has an effect when CoreML is included in ort_providers.
      • vad_window_duration: The VAD runs every 30ms, but to get higher-confidence values we average the results over time. This value is the time in seconds to average over. The default is 0.5s, shorter durations will spot speech faster at the cost of lower accuracy, higher values may increase accuracy, but at the cost of missing shorter utterances.
      • vad_look_behind_sample_count: Because we're averaging over time, the mean VAD signal will lag behind the initial speech detection. To compensate for that, when speech is detected we pull in some of the audio immediately before the average passed the threshold. This value is the number of samples to prepend, and defaults to 8192 (all at 16KHz).
      • vad_max_segment_duration: It can be hard to find gaps in rapid-fire speech, but a lot of applications want their text in chunks that aren't endless. This option sets the longest duration a line can be before it's marked as complete and a new segment is started. The default is 15 seconds, and to increase the chance that a natural break is found, the vad_threshold is linearly decreased over time from two thirds of the maximum duration until the maximum is reached.
      • identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline. When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text. Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode. This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute. Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering. For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.
      • diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes. Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.
      • diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.
      • diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh. Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised). Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length). Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.
      • return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription. You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.
      • log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.
