sentences.zh: model_path：保存完整流程所需的组件模型文件的目录路径。

sentences.en: Note that this is a path to the folder, not an individual file.
sentences.zh: 请注意，这是文件夹的路径，而不是单个文件。

sentences.en: You can download and get a path to a cached version of the standard models using the download_model() function.
sentences.zh: 您可以使用 download_model() 函数下载并获取标准模型的缓存版本的路径。

sentences.en: model_arch: The architecture of the model to load, from the selection defined in ModelArch.
sentences.zh: model_arch：要加载的模型的体系结构，来自 ModelArch 中定义的选择。

sentences.en: update_interval: By default the transcriber will periodically run text transcription as new audio data is fed, so that update events can be triggered.
sentences.zh: update_interval：默认情况下，转录器将在输入新音频数据时定期运行文本转录，以便可以触发更新事件。

sentences.en: This value is how often the speech to text model should be run.
sentences.zh: 该值是语音转文本模型的运行频率。

sentences.en: You can set this to a large duration to suppress updates between a line starting and ending, but because the streaming models do a lot of their work before the final speech to text stage, this may not reduce overall latency by much.
sentences.zh: 您可以将其设置为较长的​​持续时间，以抑制行开始和结束之间的更新，但由于流模型在最终语音到文本阶段之前完成了大量工作，因此这可能不会减少整体延迟太多。

sentences.en: options: These are flags that affect how the transcription process works inside the library, often enabling performance optimizations or debug logging.
sentences.zh: 选项：这些标志会影响转录过程在库内的工作方式，通常可以实现性能优化或调试日志记录。

sentences.en: They are passed as a dictionary mapping strings to strings, even if the values are to be interpreted as numbers - for example {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}.
sentences.zh: 它们作为将字符串映射到字符串的字典传递，即使这些值被解释为数字 - 例如 {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}。

sentences.en: skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.
sentences.zh: Skip_transcription：如果您只需要语音活动检测和分段，但希望在应用程序中进行进一步处理，则可以将其设置为“true”，然后在每行中使用 audioData 数组。

sentences.en: max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again.
sentences.zh: max_tokens_per_second：模型偶尔会陷入无限解码器循环，其中相同的单词会一遍又一遍地重复。

sentences.en: As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding.
sentences.zh: 作为捕获此问题的启发式方法，我们将当前运行中的标记数量与音频的持续时间进行比较，如果标记太多，我们会截断解码。

sentences.en: By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，此值设置为 6.5，但对于模型每秒生成更多原始标记的非英语语言，您可能需要将其设置为 13.0。

sentences.en: transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.
sentences.zh: transcription_interval：运行转录的频率（以秒为单位）。

sentences.en: vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments.
sentences.zh: vad_threshold：控制初始语音活动检测阶段的灵敏度，该阶段决定如何将原始音频分成片段。

sentences.en: This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping.
sentences.zh: 默认为 0.5，较低的值会创建较长的片段，可能会产生更多的背景噪声部分，而较高的值会将语音分解成较小的块，从而存在因剪辑而丢失一些实际语音的风险。

sentences.en: If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.
sentences.zh: 如果将其设置为零，则会完全禁用 VAD，但语音仍会被分解为 vad_max_segment_duration 大小的块。

sentences.en: save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline.
sentences.zh: save_input_wav_path：转录质量差的最常见原因之一是输入管道的音频转换不正确或损坏。

sentences.en: If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.
sentences.zh: 如果您将此选项设置为文件夹路径，转录器将准确保存其收到的 16KHz 单声道 WAV 文件，以便您可以确保输入音频符合您的预期。

sentences.en: log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.
sentences.zh: log_api_calls：另一个调试选项，打开此选项会导致对库中 C API 入口点的所有调用在每次运行时将其参数信息写入到 stderr 或控制台。

sentences.en: log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.
sentences.zh: log_ort_runs：打印有关 ONNXRuntime 推理运行及其所需时间的信息。

sentences.en: ort_providers: A comma-separated, ordered list of the ONNX Runtime execution providers the models should try to use, for example "CoreML,CPU" on macOS or "NNAPI,CPU" on Android.
sentences.zh: ort_providers：模型应尝试使用的 ONNX 运行时执行提供程序的逗号分隔有序列表，例如 macOS 上的“CoreML,CPU”或 Android 上的“NNAPI,CPU”。

sentences.en: The names are case-insensitive and accept either the short form (CPU, CoreML, NNAPI) or the full ONNX Runtime name (CPUExecutionProvider, CoreMLExecutionProvider, NNAPIExecutionProvider).
sentences.zh: 名称不区分大小写，接受缩写形式（CPU、CoreML、NNAPI）或完整的 ONNX 运行时名称（CPUExecutionProvider、CoreMLExecutionProvider、NNAPIExecutionProvider）。

sentences.en: Providers are appended in the order given, and ONNX Runtime falls back to later entries for any operations an earlier provider can't handle, so it's good practice to always list CPU last as a catch-all.
sentences.zh: 提供程序按给定的顺序附加，对于早期提供程序无法处理的任何操作，ONNX 运行时会回退到后面的条目，因此最好始终将 CPU 列为最后一个包罗万象。

sentences.en: If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only.
sentences.zh: 如果您未设置此项，则库仅运行 CPU。

sentences.en: Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error.
sentences.zh: 请求当前平台上不可用的提供程序（例如 Apple 硬件上的 CoreML 或 Android 上的 NNAPI）是错误的。

sentences.en: The alias ort_provider is also accepted.
sentences.zh: 别名 ort_provider 也被接受。

sentences.en: coreml_cache_dir: A directory path where the CoreML execution provider caches its compiled models on macOS and iOS.
sentences.zh: coreml_cache_dir：CoreML 执行提供程序在 macOS 和 iOS 上缓存其编译模型的目录路径。

sentences.en: Compiling a model for CoreML is expensive, so pointing this at a persistent, writable folder lets subsequent runs reuse the cached artifacts and start up much faster.
sentences.zh: 编译 CoreML 模型的成本很高，因此将其指向持久的可写文件夹可以让后续运行重用缓存的工件并更快地启动。

sentences.en: This only has an effect when CoreML is included in ort_providers.
sentences.zh: 仅当 CoreML 包含在 ort_providers 中时，这才有效。

sentences.en: vad_window_duration: The VAD runs every 30ms, but to get higher-confidence values we average the results over time.
sentences.zh: vad_window_duration：VAD 每 30 毫秒运行一次，但为了获得更高的置信度值，我们对一段时间内的结果进行平均。

sentences.en: This value is the time in seconds to average over.
sentences.zh: 该值是以秒为单位的平均时间。

sentences.en: The default is 0.5s, shorter durations will spot speech faster at the cost of lower accuracy, higher values may increase accuracy, but at the cost of missing shorter utterances.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 0.5 秒，较短的持续时间会更快地发现语音，但代价是准确性较低，较高的值可能会提高准确性，但代价是丢失较短的话语。

sentences.en: vad_look_behind_sample_count: Because we're averaging over time, the mean VAD signal will lag behind the initial speech detection.
sentences.zh: vad_look_behind_sample_count：因为我们随着时间的推移进行平均，所以平均 VAD 信号将落后于初始语音检测。

sentences.en: To compensate for that, when speech is detected we pull in some of the audio immediately before the average passed the threshold.
sentences.zh: 为了弥补这一点，当检测到语音时，我们在平均值超过阈值之前立即提取一些音频。

sentences.en: This value is the number of samples to prepend, and defaults to 8192 (all at 16KHz).
sentences.zh: 该值是要前置的样本数，默认为 8192（均为 16KHz）。

sentences.en: vad_max_segment_duration: It can be hard to find gaps in rapid-fire speech, but a lot of applications want their text in chunks that aren't endless.
sentences.zh: vad_max_segment_duration：在快速语音中找到间隙可能很困难，但许多应用程序希望其文本不是无限的。

sentences.en: This option sets the longest duration a line can be before it's marked as complete and a new segment is started.
sentences.zh: 此选项设置一条线在标记为完成并开始新段之前可以持续的最长持续时间。

sentences.en: The default is 15 seconds, and to increase the chance that a natural break is found, the vad_threshold is linearly decreased over time from two thirds of the maximum duration until the maximum is reached.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 15 秒，为了增加发现自然中断的机会，vad_threshold 会随着时间从最大持续时间的三分之二线性减小，直到达到最大值。

sentences.en: identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline.
sentences.zh: identify_speakers：一个布尔值（默认为 false），控制是否运行管道的说话人二值化阶段。

sentences.en: When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text.
sentences.zh: 启用后，每行都带有一个 seller_spans 数组，描述谁在何时发言，包括行文本中的 UTF-8 字符范围。

sentences.en: Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode.
sentences.zh: 在此模式下自动启用字时间戳。

sentences.en: This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute.
sentences.zh: 这会在转录调用内内联运行 pyannote Community-1 管道的 C++ 端口，这会显着增加计算量。

sentences.en: Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering.
sentences.zh: 流会话将 VBx 重新聚类绑定到滑动窗口（请参阅 diarization_cluster_window_sec）；批量/一次性转录仍然使用全历史聚类。

sentences.en: For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.
sentences.zh: 对于测试，test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav 是一个约 28 秒的合成双扬声器剪辑（ZipVoice TTS、Beckett 的 Endgame 对话）；使用 python3 script/generate-diarization-test-audio.py 重新生成它。

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_cadence：一个浮点数（默认 2.0），给出二值化重新聚类过程之间新音频的最小秒数。

sentences.en: Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.
sentences.zh: 提高这个值可以减少长时间会话的计算量，但代价是演讲者分配的细化速度变慢。

sentences.en: diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.
sentences.zh: diarization_analyze_cadence：一个浮点数（默认为 0，表示模型默认为 1.0），给出二值化分段/嵌入模型运行之间的秒数。

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_window_sec：一个浮点数（默认 120.0），给出每次流刷新时馈送到 VBx 的最近音频历史记录的最大秒数。

sentences.en: Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised).
sentences.zh: 较旧的块将从缓存中逐出，并且它们的扬声器轮次被冻结（不再修改）。

sentences.en: Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length).
sentences.zh: 设置为 0 可实现无限制的全历史重新集群（长时间会话的质量更高，但计算和内存会随着会话长度而增长）。

sentences.en: Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.
sentences.zh: 无论此设置如何，批量/一次性二值化始终使用完整历史记录。

sentences.en: return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription.
sentences.zh: return_audio_data：默认情况下，转录器随转录一起返回与一行文本相对应的音频数据段。

sentences.en: You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.
sentences.zh: 如果您想减少内存开销，可以禁用此功能。

sentences.en: log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.
sentences.zh: log_output_text：如果启用此选项，则语音到文本模型的结果将记录到控制台。

blocks.html_en: __init__(): Loads and initializes the transcriber.

sentences.en: __init__(): Loads and initializes the transcriber.
sentences.zh: __init__()：加载并初始化转录器。

blocks.html_en: • model_path: The path to the directory holding the component model files needed for the complete flow. Note that this is a path to the folder, not an individual file. You can download and get a path to a cached version of the standard models using the download_model() function.

sentences.en: model_path: The path to the directory holding the component model files needed for the complete flow.
sentences.zh: model_path：保存完整流程所需的组件模型文件的目录路径。

sentences.en: Note that this is a path to the folder, not an individual file.
sentences.zh: 请注意，这是文件夹的路径，而不是单个文件。

sentences.en: You can download and get a path to a cached version of the standard models using the download_model() function.
sentences.zh: 您可以使用 download_model() 函数下载并获取标准模型的缓存版本的路径。

blocks.html_en: • model_arch: The architecture of the model to load, from the selection defined in ModelArch.

sentences.en: model_arch: The architecture of the model to load, from the selection defined in ModelArch.
sentences.zh: model_arch：要加载的模型的体系结构，来自 ModelArch 中定义的选择。

blocks.html_en: • update_interval: By default the transcriber will periodically run text transcription as new audio data is fed, so that update events can be triggered. This value is how often the speech to text model should be run. You can set this to a large duration to suppress updates between a line starting and ending, but because the streaming models do a lot of their work before the final speech to text stage, this may not reduce overall latency by much.

sentences.en: update_interval: By default the transcriber will periodically run text transcription as new audio data is fed, so that update events can be triggered.
sentences.zh: update_interval：默认情况下，转录器将在输入新音频数据时定期运行文本转录，以便可以触发更新事件。

sentences.en: This value is how often the speech to text model should be run.
sentences.zh: 该值是语音转文本模型的运行频率。

sentences.en: You can set this to a large duration to suppress updates between a line starting and ending, but because the streaming models do a lot of their work before the final speech to text stage, this may not reduce overall latency by much.
sentences.zh: 您可以将其设置为较长的​​持续时间，以抑制行开始和结束之间的更新，但由于流模型在最终语音到文本阶段之前完成了大量工作，因此这可能不会减少整体延迟太多。

blocks.html_en:
• options: These are flags that affect how the transcription process works inside the library, often enabling performance optimizations or debug logging. They are passed as a dictionary mapping strings to strings, even if the values are to be interpreted as numbers - for example {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}.
    • skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.
    • max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again. As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding. By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.
    • transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.
    • vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments. This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping. If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.
    • save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline. If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.
    • log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.
    • log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.
    • ort_providers: A comma-separated, ordered list of the ONNX Runtime execution providers the models should try to use, for example "CoreML,CPU" on macOS or "NNAPI,CPU" on Android. The names are case-insensitive and accept either the short form (CPU, CoreML, NNAPI) or the full ONNX Runtime name (CPUExecutionProvider, CoreMLExecutionProvider, NNAPIExecutionProvider). Providers are appended in the order given, and ONNX Runtime falls back to later entries for any operations an earlier provider can't handle, so it's good practice to always list CPU last as a catch-all. If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only. Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error. The alias ort_provider is also accepted.
    • coreml_cache_dir: A directory path where the CoreML execution provider caches its compiled models on macOS and iOS. Compiling a model for CoreML is expensive, so pointing this at a persistent, writable folder lets subsequent runs reuse the cached artifacts and start up much faster. This only has an effect when CoreML is included in ort_providers.
    • vad_window_duration: The VAD runs every 30ms, but to get higher-confidence values we average the results over time. This value is the time in seconds to average over. The default is 0.5s, shorter durations will spot speech faster at the cost of lower accuracy, higher values may increase accuracy, but at the cost of missing shorter utterances.
    • vad_look_behind_sample_count: Because we're averaging over time, the mean VAD signal will lag behind the initial speech detection. To compensate for that, when speech is detected we pull in some of the audio immediately before the average passed the threshold. This value is the number of samples to prepend, and defaults to 8192 (all at 16KHz).
    • vad_max_segment_duration: It can be hard to find gaps in rapid-fire speech, but a lot of applications want their text in chunks that aren't endless. This option sets the longest duration a line can be before it's marked as complete and a new segment is started. The default is 15 seconds, and to increase the chance that a natural break is found, the vad_threshold is linearly decreased over time from two thirds of the maximum duration until the maximum is reached.
    • identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline. When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text. Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode. This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute. Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering. For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.
    • diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes. Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.
    • diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.
    • diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh. Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised). Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length). Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.
    • return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription. You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.
    • log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.

sentences.en: options: These are flags that affect how the transcription process works inside the library, often enabling performance optimizations or debug logging.
sentences.zh: 选项：这些标志会影响转录过程在库内的工作方式，通常可以实现性能优化或调试日志记录。

sentences.en: They are passed as a dictionary mapping strings to strings, even if the values are to be interpreted as numbers - for example {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}.
sentences.zh: 它们作为将字符串映射到字符串的字典传递，即使这些值被解释为数字 - 例如 {"max_tokens_per_second", "15"}。

sentences.en: skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.
sentences.zh: Skip_transcription：如果您只需要语音活动检测和分段，但希望在应用程序中进行进一步处理，则可以将其设置为“true”，然后在每行中使用 audioData 数组。

sentences.en: max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again.
sentences.zh: max_tokens_per_second：模型偶尔会陷入无限解码器循环，其中相同的单词会一遍又一遍地重复。

sentences.en: As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding.
sentences.zh: 作为捕获此问题的启发式方法，我们将当前运行中的标记数量与音频的持续时间进行比较，如果标记太多，我们会截断解码。

sentences.en: By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，此值设置为 6.5，但对于模型每秒生成更多原始标记的非英语语言，您可能需要将其设置为 13.0。

sentences.en: transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.
sentences.zh: transcription_interval：运行转录的频率（以秒为单位）。

sentences.en: vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments.
sentences.zh: vad_threshold：控制初始语音活动检测阶段的灵敏度，该阶段决定如何将原始音频分成片段。

sentences.en: This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping.
sentences.zh: 默认为 0.5，较低的值会创建较长的片段，可能会产生更多的背景噪声部分，而较高的值会将语音分解成较小的块，从而存在因剪辑而丢失一些实际语音的风险。

sentences.en: If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.
sentences.zh: 如果将其设置为零，则会完全禁用 VAD，但语音仍会被分解为 vad_max_segment_duration 大小的块。

sentences.en: save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline.
sentences.zh: save_input_wav_path：转录质量差的最常见原因之一是输入管道的音频转换不正确或损坏。

sentences.en: If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.
sentences.zh: 如果您将此选项设置为文件夹路径，转录器将准确保存其收到的 16KHz 单声道 WAV 文件，以便您可以确保输入音频符合您的预期。

sentences.en: log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.
sentences.zh: log_api_calls：另一个调试选项，打开此选项会导致对库中 C API 入口点的所有调用在每次运行时将其参数信息写入到 stderr 或控制台。

sentences.en: log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.
sentences.zh: log_ort_runs：打印有关 ONNXRuntime 推理运行及其所需时间的信息。

sentences.en: ort_providers: A comma-separated, ordered list of the ONNX Runtime execution providers the models should try to use, for example "CoreML,CPU" on macOS or "NNAPI,CPU" on Android.
sentences.zh: ort_providers：模型应尝试使用的 ONNX 运行时执行提供程序的逗号分隔有序列表，例如 macOS 上的“CoreML,CPU”或 Android 上的“NNAPI,CPU”。

sentences.en: The names are case-insensitive and accept either the short form (CPU, CoreML, NNAPI) or the full ONNX Runtime name (CPUExecutionProvider, CoreMLExecutionProvider, NNAPIExecutionProvider).
sentences.zh: 名称不区分大小写，接受缩写形式（CPU、CoreML、NNAPI）或完整的 ONNX 运行时名称（CPUExecutionProvider、CoreMLExecutionProvider、NNAPIExecutionProvider）。

sentences.en: Providers are appended in the order given, and ONNX Runtime falls back to later entries for any operations an earlier provider can't handle, so it's good practice to always list CPU last as a catch-all.
sentences.zh: 提供程序按给定的顺序附加，对于早期提供程序无法处理的任何操作，ONNX 运行时会回退到后面的条目，因此最好始终将 CPU 列为最后一个包罗万象。

sentences.en: If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only.
sentences.zh: 如果您未设置此项，则库仅运行 CPU。

sentences.en: Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error.
sentences.zh: 请求当前平台上不可用的提供程序（例如 Apple 硬件上的 CoreML 或 Android 上的 NNAPI）是错误的。

sentences.en: The alias ort_provider is also accepted.
sentences.zh: 别名 ort_provider 也被接受。

sentences.en: coreml_cache_dir: A directory path where the CoreML execution provider caches its compiled models on macOS and iOS.
sentences.zh: coreml_cache_dir：CoreML 执行提供程序在 macOS 和 iOS 上缓存其编译模型的目录路径。

sentences.en: Compiling a model for CoreML is expensive, so pointing this at a persistent, writable folder lets subsequent runs reuse the cached artifacts and start up much faster.
sentences.zh: 编译 CoreML 模型的成本很高，因此将其指向持久的可写文件夹可以让后续运行重用缓存的工件并更快地启动。

sentences.en: This only has an effect when CoreML is included in ort_providers.
sentences.zh: 仅当 CoreML 包含在 ort_providers 中时，这才有效。

sentences.en: vad_window_duration: The VAD runs every 30ms, but to get higher-confidence values we average the results over time.
sentences.zh: vad_window_duration：VAD 每 30 毫秒运行一次，但为了获得更高的置信度值，我们对一段时间内的结果进行平均。

sentences.en: This value is the time in seconds to average over.
sentences.zh: 该值是以秒为单位的平均时间。

sentences.en: The default is 0.5s, shorter durations will spot speech faster at the cost of lower accuracy, higher values may increase accuracy, but at the cost of missing shorter utterances.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 0.5 秒，较短的持续时间会更快地发现语音，但代价是准确性较低，较高的值可能会提高准确性，但代价是丢失较短的话语。

sentences.en: vad_look_behind_sample_count: Because we're averaging over time, the mean VAD signal will lag behind the initial speech detection.
sentences.zh: vad_look_behind_sample_count：因为我们随着时间的推移进行平均，所以平均 VAD 信号将落后于初始语音检测。

sentences.en: To compensate for that, when speech is detected we pull in some of the audio immediately before the average passed the threshold.
sentences.zh: 为了弥补这一点，当检测到语音时，我们在平均值超过阈值之前立即提取一些音频。

sentences.en: This value is the number of samples to prepend, and defaults to 8192 (all at 16KHz).
sentences.zh: 该值是要前置的样本数，默认为 8192（均为 16KHz）。

sentences.en: vad_max_segment_duration: It can be hard to find gaps in rapid-fire speech, but a lot of applications want their text in chunks that aren't endless.
sentences.zh: vad_max_segment_duration：在快速语音中找到间隙可能很困难，但许多应用程序希望其文本不是无限的。

sentences.en: This option sets the longest duration a line can be before it's marked as complete and a new segment is started.
sentences.zh: 此选项设置一条线在标记为完成并开始新段之前可以持续的最长持续时间。

sentences.en: The default is 15 seconds, and to increase the chance that a natural break is found, the vad_threshold is linearly decreased over time from two thirds of the maximum duration until the maximum is reached.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 15 秒，为了增加发现自然中断的机会，vad_threshold 会随着时间从最大持续时间的三分之二线性减小，直到达到最大值。

sentences.en: identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline.
sentences.zh: identify_speakers：一个布尔值（默认为 false），控制是否运行管道的说话人二值化阶段。

sentences.en: When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text.
sentences.zh: 启用后，每行都带有一个 seller_spans 数组，描述谁在何时发言，包括行文本中的 UTF-8 字符范围。

sentences.en: Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode.
sentences.zh: 在此模式下自动启用字时间戳。

sentences.en: This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute.
sentences.zh: 这会在转录调用内内联运行 pyannote Community-1 管道的 C++ 端口，这会显着增加计算量。

sentences.en: Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering.
sentences.zh: 流会话将 VBx 重新聚类绑定到滑动窗口（请参阅 diarization_cluster_window_sec）；批量/一次性转录仍然使用全历史聚类。

sentences.en: For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.
sentences.zh: 对于测试，test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav 是一个约 28 秒的合成双扬声器剪辑（ZipVoice TTS、Beckett 的 Endgame 对话）；使用 python3 script/generate-diarization-test-audio.py 重新生成它。

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_cadence：一个浮点数（默认 2.0），给出二值化重新聚类过程之间新音频的最小秒数。

sentences.en: Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.
sentences.zh: 提高这个值可以减少长时间会话的计算量，但代价是演讲者分配的细化速度变慢。

sentences.en: diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.
sentences.zh: diarization_analyze_cadence：一个浮点数（默认为 0，表示模型默认为 1.0），给出二值化分段/嵌入模型运行之间的秒数。

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_window_sec：一个浮点数（默认 120.0），给出每次流刷新时馈送到 VBx 的最近音频历史记录的最大秒数。

sentences.en: Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised).
sentences.zh: 较旧的块将从缓存中逐出，并且它们的扬声器轮次被冻结（不再修改）。

sentences.en: Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length).
sentences.zh: 设置为 0 可实现无限制的全历史重新集群（长时间会话的质量更高，但计算和内存会随着会话长度而增长）。

sentences.en: Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.
sentences.zh: 无论此设置如何，批量/一次性二值化始终使用完整历史记录。

sentences.en: return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription.
sentences.zh: return_audio_data：默认情况下，转录器随转录一起返回与一行文本相对应的音频数据段。

sentences.en: You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.
sentences.zh: 如果您想减少内存开销，可以禁用此功能。

sentences.en: log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.
sentences.zh: log_output_text：如果启用此选项，则语音到文本模型的结果将记录到控制台。

blocks.html_en: • skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.

sentences.en: skip_transcription: If you only want the voice-activity detection and segmentation, but want to do further processing in your app, you can set this to "true" and then use the audioData array in each line.
sentences.zh: Skip_transcription：如果您只需要语音活动检测和分段，但希望在应用程序中进行进一步处理，则可以将其设置为“true”，然后在每行中使用 audioData 数组。

blocks.html_en: • max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again. As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding. By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.

sentences.en: max_tokens_per_second: The models occassionally get caught in an infinite decoder loop, where the same words are repeated over and over again.
sentences.zh: max_tokens_per_second：模型偶尔会陷入无限解码器循环，其中相同的单词会一遍又一遍地重复。

sentences.en: As a heuristic to catch this we compare the number of tokens in the current run to the duration of the audio, and if there seem to be too many tokens we truncate the decoding.
sentences.zh: 作为捕获此问题的启发式方法，我们将当前运行中的标记数量与音频的持续时间进行比较，如果标记太多，我们会截断解码。

sentences.en: By default this is set to 6.5, but for non-English languages where the models produce a lot more raw tokens per second, you may want to bump this to 13.0.
sentences.zh: 默认情况下，此值设置为 6.5，但对于模型每秒生成更多原始标记的非英语语言，您可能需要将其设置为 13.0。

blocks.html_en: • transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.

sentences.en: transcription_interval: How often to run transcription, in seconds.
sentences.zh: transcription_interval：运行转录的频率（以秒为单位）。

blocks.html_en: • vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments. This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping. If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.

sentences.en: vad_threshold: Controls the sensitivity of the initial voice-activity detection stage that decides how to break raw audio into segments.
sentences.zh: vad_threshold：控制初始语音活动检测阶段的灵敏度，该阶段决定如何将原始音频分成片段。

sentences.en: This defaults to 0.5, with lower values creating longer segments, potentially with more background noise sections, and higher values breaking up speech into smaller chunks, at the risk of losing some actual speech by clipping.
sentences.zh: 默认为 0.5，较低的值会创建较长的片段，可能会产生更多的背景噪声部分，而较高的值会将语音分解成较小的块，从而存在因剪辑而丢失一些实际语音的风险。

sentences.en: If you set it to zero, it disables the VAD entirely, though speech will still be broken up into vad_max_segment_duration sized chunks.
sentences.zh: 如果将其设置为零，则会完全禁用 VAD，但语音仍会被分解为 vad_max_segment_duration 大小的块。

blocks.html_en: • save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline. If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.

sentences.en: save_input_wav_path: One of the most common causes of poor transcription quality is incorrect conversion or corruption of the audio that's fed into the pipeline.
sentences.zh: save_input_wav_path：转录质量差的最常见原因之一是输入管道的音频转换不正确或损坏。

sentences.en: If you set this option to a folder path, the transcriber will save out exactly what it has received as 16KHz mono WAV files, so you can ensure that your input audio is as you expect.
sentences.zh: 如果您将此选项设置为文件夹路径，转录器将准确保存其收到的 16KHz 单声道 WAV 文件，以便您可以确保输入音频符合您的预期。

blocks.html_en: • log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.

sentences.en: log_api_calls: Another debugging option, turning this on causes all calls to the C API entry points in the library to write out information on their arguments to stderr or the console each time they're run.
sentences.zh: log_api_calls：另一个调试选项，打开此选项会导致对库中 C API 入口点的所有调用在每次运行时将其参数信息写入到 stderr 或控制台。

blocks.html_en: • log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.

sentences.en: log_ort_runs: Prints information about the ONNXRuntime inference runs and how long they take.
sentences.zh: log_ort_runs：打印有关 ONNXRuntime 推理运行及其所需时间的信息。

blocks.html_en: • ort_providers: A comma-separated, ordered list of the ONNX Runtime execution providers the models should try to use, for example "CoreML,CPU" on macOS or "NNAPI,CPU" on Android. The names are case-insensitive and accept either the short form (CPU, CoreML, NNAPI) or the full ONNX Runtime name (CPUExecutionProvider, CoreMLExecutionProvider, NNAPIExecutionProvider). Providers are appended in the order given, and ONNX Runtime falls back to later entries for any operations an earlier provider can't handle, so it's good practice to always list CPU last as a catch-all. If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only. Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error. The alias ort_provider is also accepted.

sentences.en: If you leave this unset the library runs CPU-only.
sentences.zh: 如果您未设置此项，则库仅运行 CPU。

sentences.en: Requesting a provider that isn't available on the current platform (such as CoreML off Apple hardware, or NNAPI off Android) is an error.
sentences.zh: 请求当前平台上不可用的提供程序（例如 Apple 硬件上的 CoreML 或 Android 上的 NNAPI）是错误的。

sentences.en: The alias ort_provider is also accepted.
sentences.zh: 别名 ort_provider 也被接受。

blocks.html_en: • coreml_cache_dir: A directory path where the CoreML execution provider caches its compiled models on macOS and iOS. Compiling a model for CoreML is expensive, so pointing this at a persistent, writable folder lets subsequent runs reuse the cached artifacts and start up much faster. This only has an effect when CoreML is included in ort_providers.

The default is 0.5s, shorter durations will spot speech faster at the cost of lower accuracy, higher values may increase accuracy, but at the cost of missing shorter utterances.

sentences.zh: 此选项设置一条线在标记为完成并开始新段之前可以持续的最长持续时间。

sentences.en: The default is 15 seconds, and to increase the chance that a natural break is found, the vad_threshold is linearly decreased over time from two thirds of the maximum duration until the maximum is reached.
sentences.zh: 默认值为 15 秒，为了增加发现自然中断的机会，vad_threshold 会随着时间从最大持续时间的三分之二线性减小，直到达到最大值。

blocks.html_en: • identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline. When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text. Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode. This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute. Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering. For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.

sentences.en: identify_speakers: A boolean (default false) that controls whether to run the speaker diarization stage of the pipeline.
sentences.zh: identify_speakers：一个布尔值（默认为 false），控制是否运行管道的说话人二值化阶段。

sentences.en: When enabled, each line carries a speaker_spans array describing who spoke when, including UTF-8 character ranges into the line text.
sentences.zh: 启用后，每行都带有一个 seller_spans 数组，描述谁在何时发言，包括行文本中的 UTF-8 字符范围。

sentences.en: Word timestamps are enabled automatically in this mode.
sentences.zh: 在此模式下自动启用字时间戳。

sentences.en: This runs a C++ port of the pyannote community-1 pipeline inline inside transcription calls, which adds significant compute.
sentences.zh: 这会在转录调用内内联运行 pyannote Community-1 管道的 C++ 端口，这会显着增加计算量。

sentences.en: Streaming sessions bound VBx re-clustering to a sliding window (see diarization_cluster_window_sec); batch/one-shot transcription still uses full-history clustering.
sentences.zh: 流会话将 VBx 重新聚类绑定到滑动窗口（请参阅 diarization_cluster_window_sec）；批量/一次性转录仍然使用全历史聚类。

sentences.en: For tests, test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav is a ~28 second synthetic two-speaker clip (ZipVoice TTS, Beckett's Endgame dialogue); regenerate it with python3 scripts/generate-diarization-test-audio.py.
sentences.zh: 对于测试，test-assets/endgame_nagg_nell.wav 是一个约 28 秒的合成双扬声器剪辑（ZipVoice TTS、Beckett 的 Endgame 对话）；使用 python3 script/generate-diarization-test-audio.py 重新生成它。

blocks.html_en: • diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes. Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_cadence: A float (default 2.0) giving the minimum number of seconds of new audio between diarization re-clustering passes.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_cadence：一个浮点数（默认 2.0），给出二值化重新聚类过程之间新音频的最小秒数。

sentences.en: Raising this reduces compute on long sessions, at the cost of slower refinement of speaker assignments.
sentences.zh: 提高这个值可以减少长时间会话的计算量，但代价是演讲者分配的细化速度变慢。

blocks.html_en: • diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.

sentences.en: diarization_analyze_cadence: A float (default 0, meaning the model default of 1.0) giving the number of seconds between diarization segmentation/embedding model runs.
sentences.zh: diarization_analyze_cadence：一个浮点数（默认为 0，表示模型默认为 1.0），给出二值化分段/嵌入模型运行之间的秒数。

blocks.html_en: • diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh. Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised). Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length). Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.

sentences.en: diarization_cluster_window_sec: A float (default 120.0) giving the maximum seconds of recent audio history fed to VBx on each streaming refresh.
sentences.zh: diarization_cluster_window_sec：一个浮点数（默认 120.0），给出每次流刷新时馈送到 VBx 的最近音频历史记录的最大秒数。

sentences.en: Older chunks are evicted from the cache and their speaker turns are frozen (no longer revised).
sentences.zh: 较旧的块将从缓存中逐出，并且它们的扬声器轮次被冻结（不再修改）。

sentences.en: Set to 0 for unlimited full-history re-clustering (higher quality on long sessions, but compute and memory grow with session length).
sentences.zh: 设置为 0 可实现无限制的全历史重新集群（长时间会话的质量更高，但计算和内存会随着会话长度而增长）。

sentences.en: Batch/one-shot diarization always uses full history regardless of this setting.
sentences.zh: 无论此设置如何，批量/一次性二值化始终使用完整历史记录。

blocks.html_en: • return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription. You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.

sentences.en: return_audio_data: By default the transcriber returns the segment of audio data corresponding to a line of text along with the transcription.
sentences.zh: return_audio_data：默认情况下，转录器随转录一起返回与一行文本相对应的音频数据段。

sentences.en: You can disable this if you want to reduce memory overhead.
sentences.zh: 如果您想减少内存开销，可以禁用此功能。

blocks.html_en: • log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.

sentences.en: log_output_text: If this is enabled then the results of the speech to text model will be logged to the console.
sentences.zh: log_output_text：如果启用此选项，则语音到文本模型的结果将记录到控制台。

blocks.html_en:
• transcribe_without_streaming(): A convenience function to extract text from a non-live audio source, such as a file. We optimize for streaming use cases, so you're probably better off using libraries that specialize in bulk, batched transcription if you use this a lot and have performance constraints. This will still call any registered event listeners as it processes the lines, so this can be useful to test your application using pre-recorded files, or to easily integrate offline audio sources.

    • audio_data: An array of 32-bit float values, representing mono PCM audio between -1.0 and 1.0, to be analyzed for speech.
    • sample_rate: The number of samples per second. The library uses this to convert to its working rate (16KHz) internally.
    • flags: Integer, currently unused.

sentences.en: transcribe_without_streaming(): A convenience function to extract text from a non-live audio source, such as a file.
sentences.zh: transcribe_without_streaming()：从非实时音频源（例如文件）中提取文本的便捷函数。

sentences.en: We optimize for streaming use cases, so you're probably better off using libraries that specialize in bulk, batched transcription if you use this a lot and have performance constraints.
sentences.zh: 我们针对流媒体用例进行了优化，因此，如果您经常使用专门从事批量转录的库并且存在性能限制，那么您可能最好使用专门从事批量转录的库。

sentences.en: This will still call any registered event listeners as it processes the lines, so this can be useful to test your application using pre-recorded files, or to easily integrate offline audio sources.
sentences.zh: 这仍然会在处理线路时调用任何已注册的事件侦听器，因此这对于使用预先录制的文件测试您的应用程序或轻松集成离线音频源非常有用。

sentences.zh: audio_data：32 位浮点值数组，表示 -1.0 到 1.0 之间的单声道 PCM 音频，用于进行语音分析。

sentences.en: sample_rate: The number of samples per second.
sentences.zh: Sample_rate：每秒的样本数。

sentences.en: The library uses this to convert to its working rate (16KHz) internally.
sentences.zh: 该库使用它在内部转换为其工作速率（16KHz）。

sentences.en: flags: Integer, currently unused.
sentences.zh: flags：整数，当前未使用。

blocks.html_en: • start(): Begins a new transcription session. You need to call this after you've created the Transcriber and before you add any audio.

sentences.zh: start()：开始一个新的转录会话。

blocks.html_en: • stop(): Ends a transcription session. If a speech segment was still active, it's marked as complete and the appropriate event handlers are called.

sentences.en: stop(): Ends a transcription session.
sentences.zh: stop()：结束转录会话。

sentences.en: If a speech segment was still active, it's marked as complete and the appropriate event handlers are called.
sentences.zh: 如果语音段仍处于活动状态，则将其标记为完成并调用适当的事件处理程序。

blocks.html_en:
• add_audio(): Call this every time you have a new chunk of audio from your input, to begin processing. The size and sample rate of the audio should be whatever's natural for your source, since the library will handle all conversions.

    • audio_data: Array of 32-bit floats representing a mono PCM chunk of audio.
    • sample_rate: How many samples per second are present in the input audio. The library uses this to convert the data to its preferred rate.

sentences.en: add_audio(): Call this every time you have a new chunk of audio from your input, to begin processing.
sentences.zh: add_audio()：每次从输入中获得新的音频块时调用此函数以开始处理。

sentences.en: The size and sample rate of the audio should be whatever's natural for your source, since the library will handle all conversions.
sentences.zh: 音频的大小和采样率应该适合您的源，因为库将处理所有转换。

sentences.zh: audio_data：表示单声道 PCM 音频块的 32 位浮点数组。

sentences.en: sample_rate: How many samples per second are present in the input audio.
sentences.zh: Sample_rate：输入音频中每秒有多少个样本。

sentences.en: The library uses this to convert the data to its preferred rate.
sentences.zh: 图书馆使用它来将数据转换为其首选速率。

blocks.html_en:
• update_transcription: The transcript is usually updated periodically as audio data is added, but if you need to trigger one yourself, for example when a user presses refresh, or want access to the complete transcript, you can call this manually.

    • flags: Integer holding flags that are combined using bitwise or (|).
      • MOONSHINE_FLAG_FORCE_UPDATE: By default the transcriber returns a cached version of the transcript if less than 200ms of new audio has come in since the last transcription, but by setting this you can ensure that a transcription happens regardless.

sentences.en: update_transcription: The transcript is usually updated periodically as audio data is added, but if you need to trigger one yourself, for example when a user presses refresh, or want access to the complete transcript, you can call this manually.
sentences.zh: update_transcription：转录文本通常会在添加音频数据时定期更新，但如果您需要自己触发，例如当用户按下刷新时，或者想要访问完整的转录文本，您可以手动调用它。

sentences.en: flags: Integer holding flags that are combined using bitwise or (|).
sentences.zh: flags：保存使用按位或 (|) 组合的标志的整数。

sentences.en: MOONSHINE_FLAG_FORCE_UPDATE: By default the transcriber returns a cached version of the transcript if less than 200ms of new audio has come in since the last transcription, but by setting this you can ensure that a transcription happens regardless.
sentences.zh: MOONSHINE_FLAG_FORCE_UPDATE：默认情况下，如果自上次转录后传入的新音频少于 200 毫秒，转录器将返回转录的缓存版本，但通过设置此选项，您可以确保无论如何都会进行转录。

blocks.html_en:
• create_stream(): If your application is taking audio input from multiple sources, for example a microphone and system audio, then you'll want to create multiple streams on a single transcriber to avoid loading multiple copies of the models. Each stream has its own transcript, and line events are tagged with the stream handle they came from. You don't need to worry about this if you only need to deal with a single input though, just use the Transcriber class's start(), stop(), etc. This function returns Stream class object.

    • flags: Integer, reserved for future expansion.
    • update_interval: Period in seconds between transcription updates.

sentences.en: create_stream(): If your application is taking audio input from multiple sources, for example a microphone and system audio, then you'll want to create multiple streams on a single transcriber to avoid loading multiple copies of the models.
sentences.zh: create_stream()：如果您的应用程序从多个源获取音频输入，例如麦克风和系统音频，那么您将需要在单个转录器上创建多个流，以避免加载模型的多个副本。

sentences.en: Each stream has its own transcript, and line events are tagged with the stream handle they came from.
sentences.zh: 每个流都有自己的转录本，行事件用它们来自的流句柄进行标记。

sentences.en: You don't need to worry about this if you only need to deal with a single input though, just use the Transcriber class's start(), stop(), etc. This function returns Stream class object.

sentences.en: flags: Integer, reserved for future expansion.
sentences.zh: flags：整数，保留用于将来的扩展。

sentences.en: update_interval: Period in seconds between transcription updates.
sentences.zh: update_interval：转录更新之间的时间间隔（以秒为单位）。

blocks.html_en:
• add_listener(): Registers a callable object with the transcriber. This object will be called back as audio is fed in and text is extracted.

    • listener: This is often a subclass of TranscriptEventListener, but can be a plain function. It defines what code is called when a speech event happens.

sentences.zh: 它定义了当语音事件发生时调用什么代码。

blocks.html_en: MicTranscriber

sentences.en: MicTranscriber
sentences.zh: 麦克风转录器

blocks.html_en: This class supports the []start()](#transcriber-start), stop() and listener functions of Transcriber, but internally creates and attaches to the system's microphone input, so you don't need to call add_audio() yourself. In Python this uses the sounddevice library, but in other languages the class uses the native audio API under the hood.

sentences.en: This class supports the []start()](#transcriber-start), stop() and listener functions of Transcriber, but internally creates and attaches to the system's microphone input, so you don't need to call add_audio() yourself.
sentences.zh: 该类支持 Transcriber 的 []start()](#transcriber-start)、stop() 和监听器函数，但在内部创建并附加到系统的麦克风输入，因此您不需要自己调用 add_audio() 。

sentences.en: In Python this uses the sounddevice library, but in other languages the class uses the native audio API under the hood.
sentences.zh: 在 Python 中，该类使用 sounddevice 库，但在其他语言中，该类在底层使用本机音频 API。

blocks.html_en: Stream

sentences.en: Stream
sentences.zh: 溪流

blocks.html_en: The access point for when you need to feed multiple audio inputs into a single transcriber. Supports start(), stop(), add_audio(), update_transcription(), add_listener(), remove_listener(), and remove_all_listeners() as documented in the Transcriber class.

sentences.en: The access point for when you need to feed multiple audio inputs into a single transcriber.
sentences.zh: 当您需要将多个音频输入输入到单个转录器时的接入点。

sentences.en: Supports start(), stop(), add_audio(), update_transcription(), add_listener(), remove_listener(), and remove_all_listeners() as documented in the Transcriber class.
sentences.zh: 支持 start()、stop()、add_audio()、update_transcription()、add_listener()、remove_listener() 和 remove_all_listeners()（如 Transcriber 类中所述）。

blocks.html_en: TranscriptEventListener

sentences.en: TranscriptEventListener
sentences.zh: 转录事件监听器

blocks.html_en: A convenience class to derive from to create your own listener code. Override any or all of on_line_started(), on_line_updated(), on_line_text_changed(), and on_line_completed(), and they'll be called back when the corresponding event occurs.

sentences.en: A convenience class to derive from to create your own listener code.
sentences.zh: 派生一个方便的类来创建您自己的侦听器代码。

sentences.en: Override any or all of on_line_started(), on_line_updated(), on_line_text_changed(), and on_line_completed(), and they'll be called back when the corresponding event occurs.
sentences.zh: 重写 on_line_started()、on_line_updated()、on_line_text_changed() 和 on_line_completed() 中的任何一个或全部，当相应的事件发生时它们将被回调。

blocks.html_en: IntentRecognizer

sentences.en: IntentRecognizer
sentences.zh: 意图识别器

blocks.html_en: A specialized kind of event listener that you add as a listener to a Transcriber, and it then analyzes the transcription results to determine if any of the specified commands have been spoken, using natural-language fuzzy matching.

sentences.en: A specialized kind of event listener that you add as a listener to a Transcriber, and it then analyzes the transcription results to determine if any of the specified commands have been spoken, using natural-language fuzzy matching.
sentences.zh: 一种特殊类型的事件侦听器，您可以将其作为侦听器添加到转录器，然后它会使用自然语言模糊匹配分析转录结果，以确定是否已说出任何指定的命令。

blocks.html_en:
• register_intent(): Registers a canonical phrase for the recognizer to match against, with optional pre-computed embedding and priority.
    • trigger_phrase: The canonical command sentence to match against.
    • handler: (optional) A callable (canonical_phrase, utterance, similarity) -> None invoked by process_utterance() for the best match.
    • embedding: (optional, keyword-only) A list of floats representing a pre-computed embedding. When None (the default) the native library computes the embedding from trigger_phrase automatically. Use calculate_embedding() to pre-compute embeddings.
    • priority: (optional, keyword-only) An integer priority. Higher-priority intents rank above lower-priority ones in get_closest_intents(), even when their similarity score is lower. Defaults to 0.

sentences.en: register_intent(): Registers a canonical phrase for the recognizer to match against, with optional pre-computed embedding and priority.
sentences.zh: register_intent()：注册一个规范短语供识别器进行匹配，并具有可选的预先计算的嵌入和优先级。

sentences.en: Use calculate_embedding() to pre-compute embeddings.
sentences.zh: 使用calculate_embedding()来预先计算嵌入。

blocks.html_en: DialogFlow

sentences.en: DialogFlow
sentences.zh: 对话流

blocks.html_en: A runner that drives generator-based conversational flows. You register flow functions against trigger phrases, and the runner routes completed transcript lines either to its configured IntentRecognizer (when no flow is active) or to the currently suspended generator (when one is). It implements the TranscriptEventListener interface, so you attach it to a Transcriber or MicTranscriber with add_listener() the same way you would an IntentRecognizer. See Getting Started with a Conversational Agent for usage examples.

sentences.en: A runner that drives generator-based conversational flows.
sentences.zh: 驱动基于生成器的对话流的运行器。

sentences.en: You register flow functions against trigger phrases, and the runner routes completed transcript lines either to its configured IntentRecognizer (when no flow is active) or to the currently suspended generator (when one is).
sentences.zh: 您根据触发短语注册流函数，运行器将完成的转录行路由到其配置的 IntentRecognizer（当没有流处于活动状态时）或当前挂起的生成器（当有流时）。

sentences.en: It implements the TranscriptEventListener interface, so you attach it to a Transcriber or MicTranscriber with add_listener() the same way you would an IntentRecognizer.
sentences.zh: 它实现了 TranscriptEventListener 接口，因此您可以使用 add_listener() 将其附加到 Transcriber 或 MicTranscriber，就像使用 IntentRecognizer 一样。

sentences.en: See Getting Started with a Conversational Agent for usage examples.
sentences.zh: 有关使用示例，请参阅会话代理入门。

blocks.html_en: A flow is an ordinary Python generator function that takes a Dialog as its argument and yields prompt objects back to the runner. The runner carries out each prompt (speaking text, waiting for the user's response) and resumes the generator with the answer via .send(). This lets you write multi-step, branching conversations using regular Python control flow, including loops and exception handlers, without any async machinery. Trigger matching, confirmation, and option selection are all done semantically through the embedding model, so alternative phrasings will work without you needing to enumerate them.

sentences.en: A flow is an ordinary Python generator function that takes a Dialog as its argument and yields prompt objects back to the runner.
sentences.zh: 流是一个普通的 Python 生成器函数，它采用 Dialog 作为其参数，并将提示对象返回给运行器。

sentences.en: The runner carries out each prompt (speaking text, waiting for the user's response) and resumes the generator with the answer via .send().
sentences.zh: 跑步者执行每个提示（说出文本，等待用户的响应）并通过 .send() 恢复生成器并提供答案。

sentences.en: This lets you write multi-step, branching conversations using regular Python control flow, including loops and exception handlers, without any async machinery.
sentences.zh: 这使您可以使用常规 Python 控制流（包括循环和异常处理程序）编写多步骤、分支对话，而无需任何异步机制。

sentences.en: Trigger matching, confirmation, and option selection are all done semantically through the embedding model, so alternative phrasings will work without you needing to enumerate them.
sentences.zh: 触发器匹配、确认和选项选择都是通过嵌入模型在语义上完成的，因此替代措辞将起作用，而无需您枚举它们。

blocks.html_en:
• __init__(): Constructs the runner with optional TTS, intent recognizer, and audio plumbing hooks. All arguments are keyword-only.

    • tts: An optional TextToSpeech instance used to speak prompts. When set, the runner calls tts.say(text) and blocks on tts.wait() before resuming the flow. If tts.play_success and tts.play_error are available they're auto-wired as the recognition-cue beep callbacks.
    • intent_recognizer: An optional IntentRecognizer used to compute the embeddings that drive trigger-phrase matching against incoming utterances. Utterances that don't match any registered flow or global are also forwarded to this recognizer for app-level handling.
    • speak_fn: Optional callable (text) -> None that speaks the text and blocks until playback finishes. Overrides tts when set, which is useful for tests and alternative TTS backends.
    • mute_fn: Optional callable (should_mute: bool) -> None invoked before and after each spoken prompt so you can silence the microphone while the assistant is talking, to avoid the agent transcribing itself.
    • spelling_mode_fn: Optional callable (active: bool) -> None invoked whenever the runner enters or leaves a SPELLED / DIGITS prompt. Wire this to the underlying transcriber's set_transcribe_flags() to flip MOONSHINE_FLAG_SPELLING_MODE on only while spelled input is expected, so the spelling-CNN fusion path is used for password and code dictation without perturbing free-form recognition.
    • success_beep_fn: Optional callable () -> None played the moment a completed transcript line is recognized, just before any TTS response begins. Defaults to tts.play_success() when tts exposes one. Pass lambda: None to silence.
    • error_beep_fn: Optional callable () -> None played when a completed transcript line isn't recognized: no trigger matched, no active flow could interpret it, and no global handler took it. Defaults to tts.play_error() when available.
    • trigger_threshold: A float between 0 and 1 setting the minimum embedding-similarity score required for an utterance to count as matching a registered trigger phrase. Defaults to 0.7.
    • spell_feedback: A boolean (default True) that controls whether every character recognized during a SPELLED / DIGITS prompt is spoken back to the user as confirmation, along with "deleting <character>" for undo commands. Pass False to silence the character-by-character echo (for example when no TTS is wired up).
    • ignore_stt_during_tts: A boolean (default True). When set, every utterance that arrives while the runner is mid-prompt (i.e. the TTS is actively speaking) is dropped before it can advance the flow, match a global trigger, or fall through to the intent recognizer. This guards against self-capture on devices with weak echo cancellation. Disable only when you have reliable echo cancellation and want barge-in.
    • log_io: A boolean (default False). When enabled, every utterance the runner receives and every prompt the assistant speaks is logged to stderr in a clean user: ... / assistant: ... format. Distinct from debug: this is the user-facing dialogue transcript without the verbose internal trace.
    • debug: A boolean (default False). When enabled, stage-transition traces with per-step and cumulative timings are written to stderr, which is useful for diagnosing latency or missing-beep issues.

sentences.en: __init__(): Constructs the runner with optional TTS, intent recognizer, and audio plumbing hooks.
sentences.zh: __init__()：使用可选的 TTS、意图识别器和音频管道挂钩构造运行器。

sentences.en: All arguments are keyword-only.
sentences.zh: 所有参数都是关键字。

sentences.en: tts: An optional TextToSpeech instance used to speak prompts.
sentences.zh: tts：用于说出提示的可选 TextToSpeech 实例。

sentences.en: When set, the runner calls tts.say(text) and blocks on tts.wait() before resuming the flow.
sentences.zh: 设置后，运行程序会调用 tts.say(text) 并在恢复流程之前阻塞 tts.wait()。

sentences.en: If tts.play_success and tts.play_error are available they're auto-wired as the recognition-cue beep callbacks.
sentences.zh: 如果 tts.play_success 和 tts.play_error 可用，它们会自动连接为识别提示蜂鸣回调。

sentences.en: intent_recognizer: An optional IntentRecognizer used to compute the embeddings that drive trigger-phrase matching against incoming utterances.
sentences.zh: intent_recognizer：一个可选的 IntentRecognizer，用于计算驱动触发短语与传入话语匹配的嵌入。

sentences.en: Utterances that don't match any registered flow or global are also forwarded to this recognizer for app-level handling.
sentences.zh: 与任何注册流或全局不匹配的话语也会转发到此识别器以进行应用程序级处理。

sentences.en: speak_fn: Optional callable (text) -> None that speaks the text and blocks until playback finishes.
sentences.zh: talk_fn：可选的可调用（文本）->无，朗读文本并阻塞直到播放完成。

sentences.en: Overrides tts when set, which is useful for tests and alternative TTS backends.
sentences.zh: 设置后覆盖 tts，这对于测试和替代 TTS 后端很有用。

sentences.en: mute_fn: Optional callable (should_mute: bool) -> None invoked before and after each spoken prompt so you can silence the microphone while the assistant is talking, to avoid the agent transcribing itself.
sentences.zh: mute_fn：可选可调用 (should_mute: bool) -> 在每个语音提示之前和之后都不会调用，以便您可以在助理说话时使麦克风静音，以避免代理转录自己。

sentences.en: spelling_mode_fn: Optional callable (active: bool) -> None invoked whenever the runner enters or leaves a SPELLED / DIGITS prompt.
sentences.zh: pelling_mode_fn：可选可调用（活动：bool）->每当跑步者进入或离开 SPELLED / DIGITS 提示时都不调用。

sentences.en: Wire this to the underlying transcriber's set_transcribe_flags() to flip MOONSHINE_FLAG_SPELLING_MODE on only while spelled input is expected, so the spelling-CNN fusion path is used for password and code dictation without perturbing free-form recognition.
sentences.zh: 将其连接到底层转录器的 set_transcribe_flags() ，仅在需要拼写输入时才打开 MOONSHINE_FLAG_SPELLING_MODE，因此拼写-CNN 融合路径用于密码和代码听写，而不会干扰自由形式识别。

sentences.en: success_beep_fn: Optional callable () -> None played the moment a completed transcript line is recognized, just before any TTS response begins.
sentences.zh: success_beep_fn：可选的可调用 () -> 在识别出完整的转录行时、任何 TTS 响应开始之前，不播放任何内容。

sentences.en: Defaults to tts.play_success() when tts exposes one.
sentences.zh: 当 tts 暴露一个时，默认为 tts.play_success() 。

sentences.en: Pass lambda: None to silence.
sentences.zh: 将 lambda: None 传递给静音。

sentences.en: error_beep_fn: Optional callable () -> None played when a completed transcript line isn't recognized: no trigger matched, no active flow could interpret it, and no global handler took it.
sentences.zh: error_beep_fn：可选的可调用 () -> 当无法识别已完成的转录行时，不播放：没有匹配的触发器，没有活动流可以解释它，并且没有全局处理程序接受它。

sentences.en: Defaults to tts.play_error() when available.
sentences.zh: 默认为 tts.play_error()（如果可用）。

sentences.en: trigger_threshold: A float between 0 and 1 setting the minimum embedding-similarity score required for an utterance to count as matching a registered trigger phrase.
sentences.zh: trigger_threshold：0 到 1 之间的浮点数，设置将话语视为与注册的触发短语匹配所需的最小嵌入相似度分数。

sentences.en: Defaults to 0.7.
sentences.zh: 默认为 0.7。

sentences.en: spell_feedback: A boolean (default True) that controls whether every character recognized during a SPELLED / DIGITS prompt is spoken back to the user as confirmation, along with "deleting <character>" for undo commands.
sentences.zh: pell_feedback：一个布尔值（默认 True），控制是否在 SPELLED / DIGITS 提示期间识别到的每个字符都会向用户朗读作为确认，以及用于撤消命令的“删除 <字符>”。

sentences.en: Pass False to silence the character-by-character echo (for example when no TTS is wired up).
sentences.zh: 传递 False 可使逐个字符的回声静音（例如，当未连接 TTS 时）。

sentences.en: ignore_stt_during_tts: A boolean (default True).
sentences.zh: ignore_stt_during_tts：布尔值（默认 True）。

sentences.en: When set, every utterance that arrives while the runner is mid-prompt (i.e. the TTS is actively speaking) is dropped before it can advance the flow, match a global trigger, or fall through to the intent recognizer.
sentences.zh: 设置后，跑步者在提示中间（即 TTS 正在讲话）时到达的每条话语都会在其推进流程、匹配全局触发器或落入意图识别器之前被丢弃。

sentences.en: This guards against self-capture on devices with weak echo cancellation.
sentences.zh: 这可以防止回声消除较弱的设备上的自捕获。

sentences.en: Disable only when you have reliable echo cancellation and want barge-in.
sentences.zh: 仅当您具有可靠的回声消除功能并且想要插入时才禁用。

sentences.en: log_io: A boolean (default False).
sentences.zh: log_io：布尔值（默认为 False）。

sentences.en: When enabled, every utterance the runner receives and every prompt the assistant speaks is logged to stderr in a clean user: ... / assistant: ...
sentences.zh: 启用后，跑步者收到的每句话和助手所说的每一个提示都会以干净的用户方式记录到 stderr: ... / Assistant: ...

sentences.en: format.
sentences.zh: 格式。

sentences.en: Distinct from debug: this is the user-facing dialogue transcript without the verbose internal trace.
sentences.zh: 与调试不同：这是面向用户的对话记录，没有详细的内部跟踪。

sentences.en: debug: A boolean (default False).
sentences.zh: debug：布尔值（默认 False）。

sentences.en: When enabled, stage-transition traces with per-step and cumulative timings are written to stderr, which is useful for diagnosing latency or missing-beep issues.
sentences.zh: 启用后，带有每步和累积计时的阶段转换跟踪将写入 stderr，这对于诊断延迟或丢失蜂鸣声问题非常有用。

blocks.html_en: __init__(): Constructs the runner with optional TTS, intent recognizer, and audio plumbing hooks. All arguments are keyword-only.

sentences.en: __init__(): Constructs the runner with optional TTS, intent recognizer, and audio plumbing hooks.
sentences.zh: __init__()：使用可选的 TTS、意图识别器和音频管道挂钩构造运行器。

sentences.en: All arguments are keyword-only.
sentences.zh: 所有参数都是关键字。

blocks.html_en: • tts: An optional TextToSpeech instance used to speak prompts. When set, the runner calls tts.say(text) and blocks on tts.wait() before resuming the flow. If tts.play_success and tts.play_error are available they're auto-wired as the recognition-cue beep callbacks.

sentences.en: tts: An optional TextToSpeech instance used to speak prompts.
sentences.zh: tts：用于说出提示的可选 TextToSpeech 实例。

sentences.en: When set, the runner calls tts.say(text) and blocks on tts.wait() before resuming the flow.
sentences.zh: 设置后，运行程序会调用 tts.say(text) 并在恢复流程之前阻塞 tts.wait()。

sentences.en: If tts.play_success and tts.play_error are available they're auto-wired as the recognition-cue beep callbacks.
sentences.zh: 如果 tts.play_success 和 tts.play_error 可用，它们会自动连接为识别提示蜂鸣回调。

blocks.html_en: • intent_recognizer: An optional IntentRecognizer used to compute the embeddings that drive trigger-phrase matching against incoming utterances. Utterances that don't match any registered flow or global are also forwarded to this recognizer for app-level handling.

sentences.en: intent_recognizer: An optional IntentRecognizer used to compute the embeddings that drive trigger-phrase matching against incoming utterances.
sentences.zh: intent_recognizer：一个可选的 IntentRecognizer，用于计算驱动触发短语与传入话语匹配的嵌入。

sentences.en: Utterances that don't match any registered flow or global are also forwarded to this recognizer for app-level handling.
sentences.zh: 与任何注册流或全局不匹配的话语也会转发到此识别器以进行应用程序级处理。

blocks.html_en: • speak_fn: Optional callable (text) -> None that speaks the text and blocks until playback finishes. Overrides tts when set, which is useful for tests and alternative TTS backends.

Wire this to the underlying transcriber's set_transcribe_flags() to flip MOONSHINE_FLAG_SPELLING_MODE on only while spelled input is expected, so the spelling-CNN fusion path is used for password and code dictation without perturbing free-form recognition.

blocks.html_en:
• register_flow(): Registers a flow generator function to be started whenever the trigger phrase is matched against an incoming utterance.

    • trigger_phrase: A canonical phrase that is embedded once at registration time and compared against utterances via cosine similarity, so alternative phrasings of the same meaning will all start the flow.
    • flow: A callable that takes a Dialog and returns a generator yielding prompts. Typically a generator function.

sentences.en: register_flow(): Registers a flow generator function to be started whenever the trigger phrase is matched against an incoming utterance.
sentences.zh: register_flow()：注册一个流生成器函数，每当触发短语与传入话语匹配时就启动该函数。

sentences.en: trigger_phrase: A canonical phrase that is embedded once at registration time and compared against utterances via cosine similarity, so alternative phrasings of the same meaning will all start the flow.
sentences.zh: trigger_phrase：在注册时嵌入一次的规范短语，并通过余弦相似度与话语进行比较，因此相同含义的替代短语将全部启动流程。

sentences.en: flow: A callable that takes a Dialog and returns a generator yielding prompts.
sentences.zh: flow：一个可调用的，它接受一个对话框并返回一个产生提示的生成器。

sentences.en: Typically a generator function.
sentences.zh: 通常是生成器函数。

blocks.html_en: register_flow(): Registers a flow generator function to be started whenever the trigger phrase is matched against an incoming utterance.

sentences.zh: register_flow()：注册一个流生成器函数，每当触发短语与传入话语匹配时就启动该函数。

blocks.html_en: • trigger_phrase: A canonical phrase that is embedded once at registration time and compared against utterances via cosine similarity, so alternative phrasings of the same meaning will all start the flow.

sentences.en: trigger_phrase: A canonical phrase that is embedded once at registration time and compared against utterances via cosine similarity, so alternative phrasings of the same meaning will all start the flow.

blocks.html_en: • flow: A callable that takes a Dialog and returns a generator yielding prompts. Typically a generator function.

sentences.en: flow: A callable that takes a Dialog and returns a generator yielding prompts.

sentences.en: Typically a generator function.
sentences.zh: 通常是生成器函数。

blocks.html_en:
• unregister_flow(): Removes a previously registered flow. Returns True if a flow was removed, False otherwise.

    • trigger_phrase: The trigger phrase used when the flow was registered.

sentences.en: unregister_flow(): Removes a previously registered flow.
sentences.zh: unregister_flow()：删除先前注册的流。

sentences.en: Returns True if a flow was removed, False otherwise.
sentences.zh: 如果删除了流，则返回 True，否则返回 False。

sentences.en: trigger_phrase: The trigger phrase used when the flow was registered.
sentences.zh: trigger_phrase：注册流程时使用的触发短语。

blocks.html_en: unregister_flow(): Removes a previously registered flow. Returns True if a flow was removed, False otherwise.

sentences.en: unregister_flow(): Removes a previously registered flow.

sentences.en: Returns True if a flow was removed, False otherwise.
sentences.zh: 如果删除了流，则返回 True，否则返回 False。

blocks.html_en: • trigger_phrase: The trigger phrase used when the flow was registered.

sentences.en: trigger_phrase: The trigger phrase used when the flow was registered.
sentences.zh: trigger_phrase：注册流程时使用的触发短语。

blocks.html_en:
• register_global(): Registers a phrase that is always live, even while a flow is running. Useful for commands like "cancel" or "start over" that should interrupt any in-progress conversation.

    • trigger_phrase: The canonical phrase to match, in the same way as register_flow().
    • handler: A callable that takes the current Dialog and returns an optional prompt to speak (or None). The handler can also call d.cancel() or d.restart() to abandon or reset the active flow.

sentences.en: register_global(): Registers a phrase that is always live, even while a flow is running.
sentences.zh: register_global()：注册一个始终处于活动状态的短语，即使在流程正在运行时也是如此。

sentences.en: Useful for commands like "cancel" or "start over" that should interrupt any in-progress conversation.
sentences.zh: 对于“取消”或“重新开始”等应中断任何正在进行的对话的命令很有用。

sentences.en: trigger_phrase: The canonical phrase to match, in the same way as register_flow().
sentences.zh: trigger_phrase：要匹配的规范短语，与register_flow()的方式相同。

sentences.en: handler: A callable that takes the current Dialog and returns an optional prompt to speak (or None).
sentences.zh: handler：一个可调用的函数，它接受当前的 Dialog 并返回一个可选的说话提示（或 None）。

sentences.en: The handler can also call d.cancel() or d.restart() to abandon or reset the active flow.
sentences.zh: 处理程序还可以调用 d.cancel() 或 d.restart() 来放弃或重置活动流。

blocks.html_en: • handler: A callable that takes the current Dialog and returns an optional prompt to speak (or None). The handler can also call d.cancel() or d.restart() to abandon or reset the active flow.

sentences.en: handler: A callable that takes the current Dialog and returns an optional prompt to speak (or None).

blocks.html_en:
• process_utterance(): Routes an utterance manually, without going through transcript events. Returns True if the utterance was consumed by a flow or a global handler, False otherwise. Useful for unit tests, or for driving the runner from input sources other than a Transcriber.

    • utterance: The string to route.

sentences.en: process_utterance(): Routes an utterance manually, without going through transcript events.
sentences.zh: process_utterance()：手动路由话语，而不经过转录事件。

sentences.en: Returns True if the utterance was consumed by a flow or a global handler, False otherwise.
sentences.zh: 如果话语被流或全局处理程序使用，则返回 True，否则返回 False。

sentences.en: Useful for unit tests, or for driving the runner from input sources other than a Transcriber.
sentences.zh: 对于单元测试或从转录器以外的输入源驱动运行器很有用。

sentences.en: utterance: The string to route.
sentences.zh: utterance：要路由的字符串。

blocks.html_en: process_utterance(): Routes an utterance manually, without going through transcript events. Returns True if the utterance was consumed by a flow or a global handler, False otherwise. Useful for unit tests, or for driving the runner from input sources other than a Transcriber.

sentences.en: Returns True if the utterance was consumed by a flow or a global handler, False otherwise.

• say(): Speaks text through the configured TTS, outside any flow. Useful for welcome messages, status announcements, and error notifications that don't need a full flow registration. Blocks until playback finishes, and shares the same playback path as in-flow prompts so mute_fn and self-capture suppression still apply.

    • text: The string to speak.

blocks.html_en: DialogFlow also implements the TranscriptEventListener interface, so once you attach it via transcriber.add_listener(dialog_flow), completed lines are routed automatically through process_utterance() without you having to call it yourself.

sentences.zh: DialogFlow 还实现了 TranscriptEventListener 接口，因此一旦您通过 transcriber.add_listener(dialog_flow) 附加它，完成的行将通过 process_utterance() 自动路由，而无需您自己调用它。

blocks.html_en: Dialog

sentences.en: Dialog
sentences.zh: 对话

blocks.html_en: The context object passed as the first argument to every flow function. Each method returns a prompt object that the flow yields back to the runner; the runner then carries out the prompt (speaking text, waiting for input) and sends the result, if any, back into the generator via .send(). Dialog itself performs no I/O, so flows can be unit-tested by constructing a Dialog, calling the flow function, and driving the resulting generator manually without any audio, TTS, or event loop.

sentences.en: The context object passed as the first argument to every flow function.
sentences.zh: 上下文对象作为第一个参数传递给每个流函数。

sentences.en: Each method returns a prompt object that the flow yields back to the runner; the runner then carries out the prompt (speaking text, waiting for input) and sends the result, if any, back into the generator via .send().
sentences.zh: 每个方法都会返回一个提示对象，流程将其返回给运行程序；然后，跑步者执行提示（说出文本，等待输入）并通过 .send() 将结果（如果有）发送回生成器。

sentences.en: Dialog itself performs no I/O, so flows can be unit-tested by constructing a Dialog, calling the flow function, and driving the resulting generator manually without any audio, TTS, or event loop.
sentences.zh: Dialog 本身不执行 I/O，因此可以通过构造 Dialog、调用流函数并手动驱动生成的生成器来对流进行单元测试，而无需任何音频、TTS 或事件循环。

    • barge_in: Reserved for future use; when supported, will allow the user to interrupt playback by speaking.

sentences.zh: say()：返回一个提示，当产生时，会说出文本并在播放完成后恢复流程。

sentences.zh: 该流程从收益中接收 None 。
